project.name: maxtext
project.description: High performance, highly scalable, open-source LLM library written in Python/JAX targeting Google Cloud TPUs and GPUs for training and inference

nodes.name: data_loader.py
nodes.summary: Provides DataLoader and RampUpDataLoader classes that handle preprocessed training data loading with goodput recording, sharding, and optional batch-size ramp-up during early training.

nodes.name: DataLoader
nodes.summary: Core data loader that fetches preprocessed training batches, applies input data sharding, records goodput events, and validates example batch shapes.

nodes.name: RampUpDataLoader
nodes.summary: Extends DataLoader to support gradual batch size ramp-up during early training steps, padding smaller batches with zeros to match the target batch size.

nodes.name: gcloud_stub.py
nodes.summary: Centralized decoupling layer for Google Cloud dependencies (JetStream, GCS, Goodput, Monitoring, Vertex TensorBoard, ML Diagnostics) that provides stub replacements when running without cloud access or in test environments.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the factory pattern with lazy import and stub fallback to decouple the codebase from GCP-specific libraries at import time.

nodes.name: _import_or_stub
nodes.summary: Centralizes the try-import / fallback-to-stub logic, returning real dependencies or lightweight stubs based on the DECOUPLE_GCLOUD environment flag.

nodes.name: jetstream
nodes.summary: Returns JetStream engine modules (Engine, Devices, ResultTokens, etc.) or lightweight stubs when JetStream is not available.

nodes.name: gcs_storage
nodes.summary: Returns the google.cloud.storage module or a stub namespace for GCS Client/Blob/Bucket operations.

nodes.name: gcp_workload_monitor.py
nodes.summary: Implements GCPWorkloadMonitor that reports heartbeat and performance metrics to Google Cloud Monitoring for workload health tracking on TPU/GPU clusters.

nodes.name: GCPWorkloadMonitor
nodes.summary: Manages background threads for periodic heartbeat and performance metric reporting to Google Cloud Monitoring APIs.

nodes.name: goodput.py
nodes.summary: Provides goodput tracking utilities that measure and record effective training throughput events using the ml_goodput_measurement library for training efficiency analysis.

nodes.name: maybe_monitor_goodput
nodes.summary: Conditionally starts goodput monitoring based on config flags, setting up cloud monitoring for step time deviation and GCP metrics.

nodes.name: managed_mldiagnostics.py
nodes.summary: Provides a singleton ManagedMLDiagnostics class that wraps Google's managed ML diagnostics APIs for reporting training metrics and performance data.

nodes.name: ManagedMLDiagnostics
nodes.summary: Singleton that lazily initializes and provides access to Google managed ML diagnostics publisher with fallback to no-op if the API is unavailable.

nodes.name: metric_logger.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive metric logging system that writes training and evaluation metrics to TensorBoard, GCS, local files, and managed ML diagnostics with support for buffered and streaming output modes.

nodes.name: MetricLogger
nodes.summary: Central metric logging class that dispatches training/eval metrics to multiple backends (TensorBoard, GCS JSON, local files, managed diagnostics) with buffering, accumulation, and performance queue support.

nodes.name: profiler.py
nodes.summary: Manages JAX profiler lifecycle with support for one-shot and periodic profiling, handling activation, deactivation, and post-processing of profiling traces.

nodes.name: Profiler
nodes.summary: Controls JAX profiler activation and deactivation based on step ranges, with periodic profiling support and trace upload to GCS.

nodes.name: vertex_tensorboard.py
nodes.summary: Integrates with Google Cloud Vertex AI TensorBoard for uploading training metrics and logs to a managed TensorBoard instance with automatic experiment creation.

nodes.name: VertexTensorboardManager
nodes.summary: Manages the lifecycle of Vertex AI TensorBoard integration including setup, data upload via TensorBoard uploader, and cleanup.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init for the configs module, serving as a namespace marker for MaxText configuration utilities.

nodes.name: pyconfig.py
nodes.summary: Core configuration system that loads YAML base configs, applies command-line overrides and environment variables, validates settings, and produces a Pydantic-based MaxTextConfig object used throughout the training and inference pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic v2 BaseModel for type-safe configuration with field validators, model validators, and YAML template rendering via Jinja2.

nodes.name: initialize
nodes.summary: Public entry point for initializing the MaxText configuration from command-line arguments and keyword overrides, delegating to the Pydantic initializer.

nodes.name: _initialize_pydantic
nodes.summary: Internal configuration pipeline that resolves config paths, loads YAML, merges model-specific overrides, applies env/CLI overrides, and constructs the validated Pydantic MaxTextConfig.

nodes.name: HyperParameters
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around MaxTextConfig that provides attribute-style access and JAX pytree compatibility for config values.

nodes.name: pyconfig_deprecated.py
nodes.summary: Legacy configuration system with extensive validation functions for model architecture, parallelism, quantization, and training parameters; maintained for backward compatibility but superseded by the Pydantic-based pyconfig.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains ~60 standalone validation functions covering every aspect of MaxText configuration, reflecting the full complexity of the distributed training system.

nodes.name: _HyperParameters
nodes.summary: Legacy configuration class that loads YAML configs, applies environment variables and CLI overrides, runs all validation functions, and stores the complete set of hyperparameters.

nodes.name: types.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Pydantic configuration type definitions organized into ~90 modular dataclasses covering every aspect of MaxText: model architecture, quantization, attention, MoE, parallelism, optimization, inference, RL, multimodal, and more.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic BaseModel with nested composition to create a modular, type-safe configuration hierarchy with ~3200 lines of field definitions and validators.

nodes.name: MaxTextConfig
nodes.summary: Root Pydantic configuration model that composes all modular config sections and implements model-specific defaults loading, derived value computation, and cross-field validation for the entire MaxText system.

nodes.name: ModelArchitecture
nodes.summary: Configuration section for core model architecture parameters including embedding dimension, attention heads, MLP dimensions, and decoder layers.

nodes.name: Attention
nodes.summary: Configuration section for attention mechanism parameters including attention type, kernel selection, sliding window, Splash attention, and ragged attention options.

nodes.name: MoEGeneral
nodes.summary: Configuration section for Mixture-of-Experts parameters including expert count, routing, capacity factors, and specialized kernel options.

nodes.name: HardwareAndMesh
nodes.summary: Configuration section for hardware targets (TPU/GPU), device mesh layout, scan settings, and parallelism framework options (NNX, Shardy).

nodes.name: Optimizer
nodes.summary: Configuration section for optimizer settings including optimizer type, gradient clipping, accumulation steps, learning rate scheduling, and trainable parameter masks.

nodes.name: RL
nodes.summary: Configuration section for reinforcement learning parameters including GRPO beta, loss algorithm, agentic rollout settings, and off-policy step configuration.

nodes.name: decode.py
nodes.summary: Single-host inference script that loads a MaxEngine model, runs prefill and autoregressive decoding on a configured prompt, and prints the generated text.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates single-host inference: initializes MaxEngine, loads parameters, tokenizes input prompt, runs prefill then autoregressive generation, and detokenizes the output.

nodes.name: decode_multi.py
nodes.summary: Multi-host inference script that runs concurrent prefill and autoregressive decoding across multiple hosts using MaxEngine with distributed JAX coordination.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Multi-host inference entry point that initializes distributed JAX, creates MaxEngine, loads parameters, and runs inference with host-coordinated decoding.

nodes.name: inference_microbenchmark.py
nodes.summary: Benchmarking suite that measures prefill and autoregressive decode performance of MaxEngine, reporting throughput (tokens/sec) and FLOP utilization metrics.

nodes.name: run_benchmarks
nodes.summary: Executes the full benchmark suite: initializes MaxEngine, runs prefill and AR decode benchmarks at configured lengths, collates results, and optionally uploads to GCS.

nodes.name: prefill_benchmark
nodes.summary: Benchmarks prefill latency and throughput by timing repeated prefill operations and computing tokens/sec and TFLOP/sec metrics.

nodes.name: ar_benchmark
nodes.summary: Benchmarks autoregressive decode throughput by timing iterated generate steps and computing per-token latency and memory utilization.

nodes.name: inference_microbenchmark_sweep.py
nodes.summary: Runs the inference microbenchmark suite across a sweep of batch sizes and sequence lengths, collecting performance data for capacity planning.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Iterates over configured batch size and sequence length combinations, running the inference microbenchmark at each setting and aggregating results.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the MaxEngine inference module.

nodes.name: maxengine.py
nodes.summary: Core inference engine implementing MaxEngine with JIT-compiled prefill, autoregressive generation, model loading, quantization, LoRA adapter management, prefix caching, and multi-sampling support for high-performance LLM serving.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavily uses JAX's JIT compilation, custom sharding constraints, and pjit for distributed inference across TPU/GPU pods with both Linen and NNX model support.

nodes.name: MaxEngine
nodes.summary: Primary inference engine class that orchestrates model loading, parameter management, JIT-compiled prefill/generate/insert operations, KV cache management, LoRA adapter hot-swapping, and quantization for production LLM serving.

nodes.name: create_engine_from_config_flags
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates a configured MaxEngine instance from a config file path and runtime parameters like batch size and sequence lengths.

nodes.name: maxengine_config.py
nodes.summary: Provides factory functions for creating MaxEngine instances and parsing server configuration from config strings for the JetStream serving infrastructure.

nodes.name: get_server_config
nodes.summary: Parses a JetStream server configuration string and merges it with MaxText config to produce a complete server configuration for inference deployment.

nodes.name: maxengine_server.py
nodes.summary: JetStream server entry point that initializes MaxEngine with optional prefix caching and launches the gRPC/HTTP serving infrastructure for production inference.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Initializes MaxText config, creates MaxEngine with optional prefix caching, and starts the JetStream gRPC/HTTP server for production inference serving.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the MLPerf inference benchmark module.

nodes.name: benchmark_chunked_prefill.py
nodes.summary: Benchmarks chunked prefill and prefix caching performance for MLPerf inference, measuring latency and throughput of different prefill strategies including HBM and DRAM caching tiers.

nodes.name: benchmark_chunked_prefill
nodes.summary: Runs the chunked prefill benchmark loop across multiple token chunk configurations and collects timing results.

nodes.name: create_prefix_cache
nodes.summary: Creates and populates a prefix cache with pre-computed KV cache entries across HBM and DRAM tiers for benchmark evaluation.

nodes.name: prepare_setting
nodes.summary: Initializes the inference engine, loads parameters, and prepares benchmark settings including tokenization and chunk configurations.

nodes.name: offline_inference.py
nodes.summary: Implements offline batch inference for MLPerf benchmarks with multi-threaded prefill/decode pipeline, bucket-based prefill packing, and callback-driven result collection.

nodes.name: OfflineInference
nodes.summary: Manages offline batch inference with multi-threaded prefill/decode workers, dynamic batching, decode state management, and asynchronous detokenization for high-throughput MLPerf evaluation.

nodes.name: PrefillHelper
nodes.summary: Handles prefill processing with AOT compilation, chunked prefill support, and bucket-based batching for efficient parallel prefill in MLPerf inference.

nodes.name: offline_mode.py
nodes.summary: MLPerf LoadGen SUT (System Under Test) implementation that runs MaxEngine in offline mode with query batching, warmup, and LoadGen-compatible response formatting for official MLPerf inference benchmarks.

nodes.name: SUT
nodes.summary: MLPerf LoadGen System Under Test that bridges MaxEngine's OfflineInference with LoadGen's query/response interface, handling sample loading and query dispatching.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for MLPerf offline benchmark that initializes MaxEngine, creates SUT, runs warmup, configures LoadGen settings, and executes the benchmark with result logging.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Root package entry point for MaxText that exposes the Transformer model and key utilities, setting up C++ logging level before JAX initialization.

nodes.name: compare_hf_ckpt.py
nodes.summary: Compares MaxText checkpoint outputs against HuggingFace reference checkpoints by loading safetensors from GCS or local paths and verifying numerical closeness of each parameter tensor.

nodes.name: load_safetensors_generic
nodes.summary: Loads safetensor shards in parallel from either GCS or local filesystem using a thread pool, merging them into a single dictionary.

nodes.name: verify_dictionaries
nodes.summary: Verifies two state dictionaries match by checking key sets and computing element-wise numerical closeness with configurable tolerances.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that loads both a MaxText checkpoint (safetensors) and a HuggingFace reference model, then runs verification between them.

nodes.name: load_and_quantize_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Loads a MaxText checkpoint via MaxEngine and applies quantization, saving the quantized parameters for inference deployment.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Initializes config and MaxEngine, loads model parameters, and triggers quantized checkpoint saving.

nodes.name: reshard_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Reshards a MaxText checkpoint to a different parallelism configuration by loading via MaxEngine and re-saving with new sharding specs.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Loads a checkpoint with one sharding configuration and re-saves it using Orbax with a new target parallelism layout.

nodes.name: convert_gpt3_ckpt_from_paxml.py
nodes.summary: Converts a GPT-3 checkpoint from PaxML format to MaxText format by mapping weight names and reshaping tensors to match the MaxText Transformer architecture.

nodes.name: convert
nodes.summary: Core conversion function that maps PaxML GPT-3 checkpoint parameters to MaxText parameter structure, handling attention head reshaping and layer-by-layer weight transfer.

nodes.name: llama_mistral_mixtral_orbax_to_hf.py
nodes.summary: Converts Llama, Mistral, or Mixtral checkpoints from MaxText Orbax format to HuggingFace format by mapping internal parameter names and reshaping attention weights.

nodes.name: convert_state_to_hf
nodes.summary: Maps MaxText training state parameters to HuggingFace model format, handling attention weight permutation and MLP weight transposition across all decoder layers.

nodes.name: gcs_reporter.py
nodes.summary: Provides a simple utility to upload evaluation result files from local storage to Google Cloud Storage.

nodes.name: save_param.py
nodes.summary: Agent-based checkpoint conversion utility that loads a MaxText model via MaxEngine and saves individual parameter arrays to a specified output directory for downstream analysis.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Initializes MaxEngine, loads model parameters, and iterates over the parameter tree to save each array individually with its path-based filename.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the experimental reinforcement learning module.

nodes.name: grpo_trainer.py
nodes.summary: Implements the GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) training loop including loss computation with KL divergence, advantage estimation, online completion generation, and integration with MaxText's training infrastructure for RL-based LLM fine-tuning.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports both Linen and NNX JAX model APIs with separate train/eval step implementations, demonstrating MaxText's dual-framework approach.

nodes.name: grpo_loss_fn
nodes.summary: Computes the GRPO loss by running the policy model forward pass, computing log probabilities, calculating KL divergence against reference params, and weighting by group-relative advantages.

nodes.name: train_step
nodes.summary: Executes a single GRPO training step: computes loss and gradients, applies gradient clipping, updates optimizer state, and returns updated metrics.

nodes.name: setup_train_loop
nodes.summary: Initializes the complete GRPO training state including model, optimizer, mesh, data pipeline, checkpointing, and inference engine for online completion generation.

nodes.name: train_loop
nodes.summary: Main GRPO training loop that iterates over batches, generates completions with the inference engine, reshards parameters between training and inference meshes, and logs metrics.

nodes.name: LossAux
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding auxiliary loss metrics for GRPO training including average reward, advantage, KL divergence, and completion length statistics.

nodes.name: grpo_utils.py
nodes.summary: Utility functions for GRPO training including log probability computation, offline completion generation, parameter resharding between training and inference meshes, and prompt-completion concatenation.

nodes.name: compute_log_probs
nodes.summary: Computes per-token log probabilities from the model output logits, masking non-completion tokens and handling segmentation for GRPO advantage computation.

nodes.name: generate_offline_completions
nodes.summary: Generates multiple completions per prompt using the inference engine for offline GRPO data collection, handling tokenization and batch assembly.

nodes.name: reshard_pytree
nodes.summary: Reshards a JAX pytree from one sharding configuration to another, with optional caching and donation for efficient parameter transfer between training and inference meshes.

nodes.name: concatenate_prompt_with_completions
nodes.summary: Concatenates prompts with their generated completions, building proper segmentation and position arrays for GRPO loss computation.

nodes.name: hooks.py
nodes.summary: Defines SFT-specific training and data hooks that extend the base post-training hook interfaces for supervised fine-tuning.

nodes.name: train_sft.py
nodes.summary: Implements SFT training using the Tunix PeftTrainer with MaxText loss functions, supporting LoRA and full fine-tuning workflows.

nodes.name: train_sft_native.py
nodes.summary: Implements a native MaxText SFT training loop without Tunix dependency, using MaxText's own training infrastructure for supervised fine-tuning.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init for the pre-training module that contains the main training loop and compilation utilities.

nodes.name: train.py
nodes.summary: Core pre-training module implementing the loss function, train/eval steps, training loop with checkpointing and metrics, and the main entry point for model training.

nodes.name: train_compile.py
nodes.summary: Ahead-of-time compilation utility that JIT-compiles the training step on a topology mesh and saves the compiled artifact for later execution.

nodes.name: estimator.py
nodes.summary: Automated search tool for finding optimal rematerialization policies and batch sizes by iteratively compiling and testing configurations for OOM avoidance.

nodes.name: SFTTrainingHooks
nodes.summary: SFT-specific training hooks subclass that adapts total weight computation for supervised fine-tuning.

nodes.name: SFTDataHooks
nodes.summary: SFT data hooks subclass for loading supervised fine-tuning batches.

nodes.name: MaxTextPeftTrainer
nodes.summary: Custom Tunix PeftTrainer subclass that overrides the training step to use MaxText's loss function and gradient handling.

nodes.name: get_tunix_config
nodes.summary: Creates the Tunix SFT trainer configuration from MaxText config with loss function and training hyperparameters.

nodes.name: use_maxtext_loss_function
nodes.summary: Computes the SFT loss using MaxText's cross-entropy implementation with proper masking and normalization.

nodes.name: setup_trainer_state
nodes.summary: Initializes the SFT trainer state including model, optimizer, mesh, and checkpoint restoration.

nodes.name: train
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the complete SFT training flow from config initialization through training to completion.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for SFT training that parses config and invokes the training flow.

nodes.name: train_loop
nodes.summary: Native MaxText SFT training loop that runs train/eval steps with checkpointing and metric logging without Tunix.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for native SFT training that initializes and runs the training loop.

nodes.name: loss_fn
nodes.summary: Core model loss function computing cross-entropy with support for multi-token prediction, auxiliary losses, and Z-loss regularization.

nodes.name: train_step
nodes.summary: Single training step performing forward pass, gradient computation with optional accumulation, and optimizer update with metrics collection.

nodes.name: eval_step
nodes.summary: Single evaluation step computing loss without gradient updates for validation monitoring.

nodes.name: train_loop
nodes.summary: Main training loop orchestrating train/eval steps, checkpointing, profiling, metric logging, and goodput tracking.

nodes.name: initialize
nodes.summary: Initializes the complete training infrastructure including model, optimizer, mesh, state, and data pipeline.

nodes.name: get_train_func
nodes.summary: Returns the appropriate training function based on configuration, dispatching to DiLoCo, GRPO, or standard training.

nodes.name: get_topology_mesh
nodes.summary: Creates a device mesh matching the target topology for ahead-of-time compilation without requiring actual hardware.

nodes.name: _collect_nnx_activation_shardings
nodes.summary: Collects NNX activation sharding specifications from the model graph for proper compilation.

nodes.name: get_shaped_inputs
nodes.summary: Creates shaped abstract inputs matching the training step signature for ahead-of-time compilation.

nodes.name: jit_and_compile
nodes.summary: JIT-compiles the training step function with proper sharding constraints and returns the compiled result.

nodes.name: is_oom

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: c4_exp.py
nodes.summary: Defines convergence test configurations for models pre-trained on the C4 dataset, including dataset hyperparameters for C4 variants and convergence configs for Llama3-405B and DeepSeek-671B.

nodes.name: convergence_utils.py
nodes.summary: Provides convergence testing utilities including dataset and convergence hyperparameter data classes, checkpoint loading, and convergence experiment setup for MaxText models.

nodes.name: DatasetHParams
nodes.summary: Data class defining dataset hyperparameters for convergence tests including paths, splits, and tokenization settings.

nodes.name: ConvHParams
nodes.summary: Data class defining convergence hyperparameters including batch size, learning rate schedules, and evaluation intervals.

nodes.name: setup_convergence_configs
nodes.summary: Computes derived convergence parameters from model and hyperparameter configs including warmup steps, decay schedules, and evaluation intervals.

nodes.name: _setup_model_convergence_
nodes.summary: Orchestrates convergence experiment setup by combining model config with dataset and convergence parameters to produce a complete MaxTextModel configuration.

nodes.name: setup_dataset
nodes.summary: Configures dataset tuning parameters from DatasetHParams, mapping dataset path, type, and tokenization settings into the model config.

nodes.name: disruption_handler.py
nodes.summary: Defines the disruption handler hierarchy for benchmark fault-tolerance testing, with SIGILL and SIGTERM handlers that send signals to targeted Kubernetes pods.

nodes.name: DisruptionConfig
nodes.summary: Data class defining disruption test configuration including trigger type/value, disruption method, recovery settings, and target pod regex patterns.

nodes.name: DisruptionHandler
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for disruption handlers that defines the interface for triggering disruptions and recoveries on benchmark workloads.

nodes.name: SIGILLHandler
nodes.summary: Disruption handler that sends SIGILL signals to targeted Kubernetes worker pods to simulate hardware faults during benchmarks.

nodes.name: SIGTERMHandler
nodes.summary: Disruption handler that sends SIGTERM signals to targeted Kubernetes worker pods to simulate graceful termination during benchmarks.

nodes.name: disruption_manager.py
nodes.summary: Manages disruption lifecycle for benchmark workloads using threaded monitoring, including workload registration, disruption triggering, and completion tracking.

nodes.name: DisruptionManager
nodes.summary: Thread-based manager that registers workloads for disruption testing, spawns monitor threads per disruption config, and coordinates disruption triggering across multiple concurrent workloads.

nodes.name: construct_disruption_configs
nodes.summary: Constructs a list of DisruptionConfig objects from a disruptions dictionary, adapting pod regex patterns based on the framework type (Pathways vs. direct).

nodes.name: disruption_utils.py
nodes.summary: Low-level Kubernetes pod utilities for disruption management, providing subprocess command execution, pod name resolution via regex, pod status checking, and pod startup waiting.

nodes.name: execute_command_as_subprocess
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command via subprocess.run and returns the result, with detailed error logging on failure.

nodes.name: get_pod_name_from_regex
nodes.summary: Finds a Kubernetes pod name matching a regex pattern within a given workload namespace using kubectl.

nodes.name: get_pod_status
nodes.summary: Queries the current status phase of a Kubernetes pod using kubectl.

nodes.name: wait_for_pod_to_start
nodes.summary: Polls until a Kubernetes pod matching the given regex transitions to Running status.

nodes.name: monitor.py
nodes.summary: Provides monitoring abstractions for disruption triggers, with StepMonitor watching training step counts and TimeMonitor using elapsed time to determine when to fire disruptions.

nodes.name: StepMonitor
nodes.summary: Monitors Kubernetes pod logs for training step progress and triggers disruption when the configured step threshold is reached.

nodes.name: TimeMonitor
nodes.summary: Timer-based disruption monitor that triggers after a configured number of seconds.

nodes.name: pw_utils.py
nodes.summary: Provides Pathways recipe utility functions including model selection/filtering, cluster config construction, Pathways config construction, and GCS bucket creation.

nodes.name: build_user_models
nodes.summary: Validates and filters user-selected models and frameworks against available model configurations, producing a framework-grouped model dictionary.

nodes.name: check_and_create_bucket
nodes.summary: Creates a GCS bucket if it does not already exist, with region-aware placement and error handling.

nodes.name: get_cluster_config
nodes.summary: Constructs an XpkClusterConfig from individual cluster parameters.

nodes.name: get_pathways_config
nodes.summary: Constructs a PathwaysConfig from individual Pathways parameters including server, proxy, and worker images and flags.

nodes.name: runner_utils.py
nodes.summary: Provides workload generation and execution orchestration for benchmark recipes, creating WorkloadConfigs from user settings and running them via the XPK benchmark runner with optional disruption management.

nodes.name: generate_and_run_workloads
nodes.summary: Top-level function that generates workload configurations from user settings and dispatches them to the XPK benchmark runner, optionally starting disruption tests.

nodes.name: _create_workload_config
nodes.summary: Creates a WorkloadConfig from individual parameters, adapting configuration based on whether the framework is Pathways or standard MaxText.

nodes.name: _generate_workloads
nodes.summary: Iterates over user-configured models and slice counts to generate a list of WorkloadConfig objects with optional disruption settings.

nodes.name: user_configs.py
nodes.summary: Defines the UserConfig data class that consolidates all user-facing benchmark recipe settings including cluster, model, Pathways, and execution parameters, with post-init validation and model selection.

nodes.name: UserConfig
nodes.summary: Comprehensive data class that aggregates all user-configurable benchmark parameters and performs post-init setup including cluster config, Pathways config, and model selection from available model registries.

nodes.name: upload_metrics_to_bq.py
nodes.summary: CLI tool for downloading benchmark metrics from GCS, parsing step-level performance data (TFLOPS, tokens/sec, step time), and uploading aggregated results to BigQuery.

nodes.name: add_parser_arguments
nodes.summary: Defines CLI arguments for the metrics upload tool including hardware, model, metrics file, and BigQuery configuration options.

nodes.name: parse_metrics
nodes.summary: Parses benchmark metrics from a local JSON file, computing average TFLOPS, tokens per second, median step time, and end-to-end step time.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main entry point that downloads metrics from GCS, parses them, and writes the aggregated benchmark results to BigQuery with full framework configuration context.

nodes.name: get_last_n_data
nodes.summary: Reads the last N entries from a JSONL metrics file and extracts the specified target metric values.

nodes.name: xla_flags_library.py
nodes.summary: Library of commonly-used XLA compiler optimization flags for TPU workloads, providing predefined flag combinations for VMEM limits, continuation fusion, latency hiding, and various compute/memory optimizations.
nodes.languageNotes: Declarative constants file defining XLA flag strings; no functions or classes, just string constants composed together for different optimization strategies.

nodes.name: xpk_configs.py
nodes.summary: Defines the XpkClusterConfig data class that configures XPK cluster connection parameters including cluster name, GCP project, zone, and device type.

nodes.name: XpkClusterConfig
nodes.summary: Data class holding XPK cluster connection details: cluster name, GCP project, zone, and device type for TPU workload submission.

nodes.name: globals.py
nodes.summary: Defines global path constants for the MaxText project including package directory, repo root, configs directory, and assets root, resolved from environment variables or filesystem detection.

nodes.summary: Standalone benchmark script for running Llama2 model benchmarks on v6e-256 TPU clusters via XPK workloads.

nodes.name: maxtext_trillium_model_configs.py

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the layers module, providing the namespace for all neural network layer implementations including attention, decoders, embeddings, and MoE.

nodes.name: attention_mla.py
nodes.summary: Implements Multi-Latent Attention (MLA) as used in DeepSeek models, with compressed KV projections, latent key-value decomposition, and an optional Indexer for learned attention patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the MLA architecture where keys/values share a low-rank latent space, reducing KV cache memory while preserving attention quality.

nodes.name: Indexer
nodes.summary: Implements learned indexer-based attention pattern selection with partial RoPE and dynamic mask generation.

nodes.name: MLA
nodes.summary: Multi-Latent Attention module supporting compressed KV projections, absorbed RoPE, and YARN-based position embeddings for efficient long-context inference.

nodes.name: mla_as_linen
nodes.summary: Factory function wrapping the NNX MLA module as a Linen module with all necessary configuration parameters.

nodes.name: attention_op.py
nodes.summary: Core attention operator implementation supporting multiple backends (dot-product, TPU flash, GPU cuDNN, ragged) with configurable masking strategies including causal, chunked, MoBa, and load-balanced patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Dispatches between multiple attention kernel implementations based on configuration, supporting both training and inference modes with different sharding strategies.

nodes.name: AttentionOp
nodes.summary: Comprehensive attention operator dispatching between dot-product, flash, splash, cuDNN, and ragged attention backends with support for causal/chunked/MoBa masking and KV cache integration.

nodes.name: ChunkedCausalMask
nodes.summary: Custom mask object for chunked causal attention supporting efficient block-level masking.

nodes.name: LoadBalancedCausalMask
nodes.summary: Mask for load-balanced causal attention used in context parallelism to distribute attention computation evenly.

nodes.name: attention_op_as_linen
nodes.summary: Factory function wrapping the NNX AttentionOp as a Linen module.

nodes.name: attentions.py
nodes.summary: Implements the main Attention layer combining query/key/value projections, rotary embeddings, KV caching, and attention operator dispatch, serving as the primary attention building block for all decoder layers.

nodes.name: Attention
nodes.summary: Full attention layer with configurable Q/K/V projections, rotary embeddings, KV cache management, output projection, and attention operator dispatch for training and inference.

nodes.name: attention_as_linen
nodes.summary: Factory function wrapping the NNX Attention module as a Linen module with comprehensive configuration parameters.

nodes.name: decoders.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Linen-based Decoder and DecoderLayer classes that compose attention, MLP, normalization, and optional MoE/pipeline layers into the full transformer decoder stack used for training and inference.

nodes.name: DecoderLayer
nodes.summary: Generic transformer decoder layer composing attention, MLP/MoE, normalization, and optional hyper-connections, dispatching to model-specific layer implementations.

nodes.name: Decoder
nodes.summary: Full transformer decoder stack managing embedding, layer scanning/pipelining, remat policies, and output heads for all supported model architectures.

nodes.name: SequentialBlockDecoderLayers
nodes.summary: Wrapper for sequentially applying multiple decoder layers within a scannable block.

nodes.languageNotes: Supports many rotary embedding variants required by different LLM families (LLaMA, Gemma, DeepSeek, Qwen3) each with architecture-specific scaling and interleaving patterns.

nodes.name: RotaryEmbedding
nodes.summary: Standard rotary positional embedding (RoPE) with configurable timescale range.

nodes.name: LLaMARotaryEmbedding
nodes.summary: LLaMA-style rotary embedding with configurable scaling factors for extended context lengths.

nodes.name: YarnRotaryEmbedding
nodes.summary: YaRN (Yet another RoPE eXtension) rotary embedding for efficient long-context extrapolation with dimension-dependent scaling.

nodes.name: Qwen3OmniMoeVisionRotaryEmbedding
nodes.summary: Vision rotary embedding for Qwen3 Omni models with 2D spatial position encoding.

nodes.summary: Implements VisionEncoder and AudioEncoder modules that dispatch to architecture-specific encoder implementations (Gemma3, Gemma4, LLaMA4, Qwen3) for multimodal model support.

nodes.summary: Vision encoder dispatching to Gemma3, Gemma4, LLaMA4, or Qwen3 vision encoder implementations based on model configuration.

nodes.name: AudioEncoder
nodes.summary: Audio encoder dispatching to Qwen3 audio encoder implementation based on model configuration.

nodes.summary: N-gram hash mapping layer computing multi-scale hash embeddings across different n-gram window sizes.

nodes.name: Engram
nodes.summary: Full Engram module combining compressed tokenization, multi-head embedding, and short convolution for n-gram-based sequence representation.

nodes.name: base.py

nodes.summary: Agent that generates parameter and shape mapping code via LLM with an iterative generate-and-verify loop, retrying up to a configured number of attempts until the verifier approves.

nodes.name: plan.py

nodes.summary: Assembles a complete Python conversion script from generated parameter mapping, hook function, and shape mapping code fragments using a template string.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the vLLM integration module, providing the namespace for MaxText-to-vLLM weight converters and rollout wrappers.

nodes.name: maxtext_vllm_rollout.py
nodes.summary: Provides MaxText-specific VllmSampler and VllmRollout subclasses that bypass Tunix's generic key-mapping and instead use model-specific converters for transferring MaxText weights to vLLM, handling QKV fusion, MoE expert weight merging, and efficient HBM memory management via incremental reshard.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pathwaysutils experimental_reshard for zero-copy weight transfer between MaxText and vLLM on TPU. Periodically triggers gc.collect and jax.effects_barrier to prevent host RAM accumulation during large weight transfers.

nodes.name: MaxTextVllmSampler
nodes.summary: VllmSampler subclass that delegates weight updates to a MaxText-to-vLLM converter, performing direct device_put into the vLLM model-runner state dict with shape validation and incremental memory-efficient reshard.

nodes.name: MaxTextVllmRollout
nodes.summary: VllmRollout subclass that constructs a MaxTextVllmSampler with a model-specific converter and runs an initial weight sync at construction time so vLLM starts with real MaxText model weights.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract base class BaseMaxTextToVLLMConverter that establishes the conversion contract (convert global, attention, and MoE weights) for transforming MaxText model states into vLLM weight tensors, plus a timer context manager for profiling conversion steps.

nodes.name: BaseMaxTextToVLLMConverter
nodes.summary: Abstract base class defining the conversion contract for MaxText-to-vLLM weight conversion with template method pattern: convert() orchestrates _convert_global, _convert_attn, and _convert_moe which subclasses must implement.

nodes.name: gemma4_moe.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Gemma 4 MoE-specific MaxText-to-vLLM weight converter, handling local/global attention QKV packing with GQA interleaving, MoE gate+up fusion with TP-aware chunk interleaving, dense MLP weight conversion, embedding denormalization, and per-layer norm unstacking.
nodes.languageNotes: Extensive use of jnp.transpose and jnp.unstack for reshaping multi-dimensional weight tensors between MaxText's and vLLM's different sharding conventions. Explicit gc.collect() calls after each conversion stage to manage TPU HBM during large model weight transfers.

nodes.name: Gemma4MaxTextToVLLMConverter
nodes.summary: Concrete converter for Gemma 4 MoE models that handles embedding denormalization, local/global attention QKV packing, MoE expert gate+up fusion with TP-interleaving, dense MLP conversion, and per-layer norm weight unstacking.

nodes.name: qwen3_moe.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Qwen3 MoE-specific MaxText-to-vLLM weight converter, handling QKV attention packing with GQA interleaving, MoE expert gate+up fusion with padding for TP alignment, and per-layer weight transpositions using jax.vmap for efficient batch conversion.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses jax.vmap to vectorize the per-expert gate+up fusion across all experts simultaneously, avoiding an explicit Python loop. Pads expert weights to the nearest TP-aligned dimension before fusion.

nodes.name: Qwen3MaxTextToVLLMConverter
nodes.summary: Concrete converter for Qwen3 MoE models that transposes and unstacks attention/MLP/MoE weights from MaxText's stacked-layer representation to vLLM's per-layer format with TP-aware gate+up expert fusion.

nodes.name: validate_converter.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end validation script that loads a MaxText model checkpoint, runs the appropriate converter (Gemma4 or Qwen3), compares converted weight keys and shapes against a native vLLM model, optionally uploads tensors to GCS for offline analysis, and performs a generation sanity check.

nodes.name: validate_converter
nodes.summary: Core validation routine that loads MaxText and vLLM models, runs the converter, checks key coverage and shape alignment, optionally uploads tensors to GCS, reshards weights, and performs a test generation to verify correctness end-to-end.

nodes.name: _check_key_coverage
nodes.summary: Validates that converted weight keys match the expected vLLM model state by checking for missing keys, extra keys, and shape mismatches, raising ValueError on shape misalignment.

nodes.name: _upload_tensors_to_gcs
nodes.summary: Uploads converted weight tensors as numpy arrays to a GCS bucket for offline comparison and debugging, filtering to only layer-0 and non-layer weights.

nodes.name: _log_weight_stats
nodes.summary: Logs statistical comparisons (shape, mean, std, min, max, relative L2 norm) between converted and reference vLLM weights for layer-0 and non-layer keys to aid debugging of weight conversion accuracy.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the RL post-training module, providing the namespace for GRPO/GSPO reinforcement learning training, evaluation, and utility functions.

nodes.name: evaluate_rl.py
nodes.summary: Provides the evaluation pipeline for RL training: generates multiple response passes via the vLLM rollout engine, scores them against ground-truth answers using math verification and format matching, and computes aggregate correctness metrics with majority-vote and pass@k support.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Orchestrates end-to-end evaluation by iterating over dataset batches, generating multi-pass responses, scoring each against ground truth, and accumulating correctness and format-matching statistics for reporting.

nodes.name: score_responses
nodes.summary: Scores a set of model responses against ground-truth answers using configurable scoring strategies (first-correct, best-of-n, majority-vote) with math verification and format-matching regex extraction.

nodes.name: generate_responses
nodes.summary: Generates multiple response passes for a batch of prompts using the vLLM rollout cluster, collecting and batching results for multi-pass evaluation strategies.

nodes.name: _score_single
nodes.summary: Scores a single model response by checking format compliance via regex and answer correctness via math verification, returning both format and correctness scores.

nodes.name: math_verify_pool.py
nodes.summary: Provides a managed multiprocessing pool for verifying mathematical answer correctness using the math_verify library, with robust timeout handling, process lifecycle management, and sympy-based symbolic equality fallback for edge cases.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses multiprocessing with 'forkserver' context to avoid JAX/CUDA fork issues. Implements a custom shutdown sequence with terminate/join/kill fallback to prevent zombie processes from blocking the training loop.

nodes.name: math_verify_pool
nodes.summary: Runs batched math verification in a managed multiprocessing pool with configurable timeout and process count, collecting results asynchronously and handling per-item timeouts gracefully.

nodes.name: verify_math_worker
nodes.summary: Worker function for math verification that parses gold and prediction expressions using math_verify's extraction configs and checks equivalence, with sympy-based fallback for numeric comparison when standard verification fails.

nodes.name: _shutdown_pool
nodes.summary: Robustly shuts down the global multiprocessing pool by terminating workers, joining with timeout, force-killing survivors, and running the join in a background thread to avoid blocking the main process.

nodes.name: silent_worker_init
nodes.summary: Worker initializer that suppresses all stdout/stderr, warnings, and logging from math_verify subprocesses to prevent noisy output from polluting the training logs.

nodes.name: are_equal_under_sympy
nodes.summary: Fallback symbolic equality check using sympy that parses gold and prediction strings as mathematical expressions and tests equivalence via simplification, used when standard math_verify fails.

nodes.name: train_rl.py
nodes.summary: Main RL training orchestrator that implements GRPO and GSPO reinforcement learning workflows for math reasoning, handling model loading, dataset preparation with tokenization, reward function setup, RL cluster configuration, training loop execution with periodic evaluation, and checkpoint management.
nodes.languageNotes: Applies tactical runtime patches to jax.lax.with_sharding_constraint and tpu_inference dtype casting for compatibility with the vLLM inference path on TPU. Uses functools.partial to inject maxtext_config into the rollout engine constructor for RLCluster integration.

nodes.name: _rl_train_impl
nodes.summary: Core RL training implementation that sets up configs and devices, loads models and tokenizer, creates the RL cluster with rollout/learner components, runs the training loop with weight sync and evaluation, and verifies model weight updates after each training step.

nodes.name: create_rl_components
nodes.summary: Constructs the complete RL training infrastructure including optimizer, checkpoint manager, profiler, metrics logger, RLCluster with rollout engine, reward functions (format matching, correctness checking, math verification), and GRPO/agentic GRPO learner configuration.

nodes.name: prepare_datasets
nodes.summary: Prepares train and evaluation datasets by loading from HuggingFace, applying data templates and formatting, filtering by prompt length, shuffling with grain, and batching for the RL training loop.

nodes.name: get_rollout_kwargs_for_parallelism
nodes.summary: Computes rollout parallelism configuration (tensor parallelism, data parallelism, pipeline parallelism) from sampler config and available devices, with extensive validation of compatible parallelism combinations.

nodes.name: _tpu_inference_compat_patches
nodes.summary: Context manager that applies tactical compatibility shims for tpu_inference, monkey-patching with_sharding_constraint to use jax.sharding.reshard and suppressing unwanted bf16-to-f32 dtype casts during inference.

nodes.name: get_dataset
nodes.summary: Loads a HuggingFace dataset with optional split and data file filtering, supporting both named datasets and local file paths.

nodes.name: prepare_train_and_eval_dataset
nodes.summary: Loads and splits a single dataset into train and validation sets, optionally filtering by question type, with configurable test size ratio.

nodes.name: rl_train
nodes.summary: Public entry point for RL training that applies TPU inference compatibility patches and delegates to the internal implementation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that initializes Pathways runtime, prints system information, and launches RL training.

nodes.name: utils_rl.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive utility module for RL training providing math answer extraction and normalization, correctness checking with math_verify and sympy, format matching with regex, optimizer construction with optax warmup-cosine scheduling, data processing and tokenization pipeline, and MCQ answer extraction for evaluation.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains extensive LaTeX normalization heuristics (100+ substitution rules, regex patterns) for robust comparison of mathematical answers across different formatting conventions. Uses optax.inject_hyperparams for dynamic learning rate scheduling with warmup-cosine decay.

nodes.name: normalize_final_answer
nodes.summary: Normalizes mathematical answer strings through extensive LaTeX cleanup, unit removal, comma-separated number handling, and numeric rounding to enable robust answer comparison across different formatting conventions.

nodes.name: fix_latex_escaping
nodes.summary: Fixes common LaTeX escaping issues in model-generated mathematical answers, normalizing fractions, exponents, whitespace, and special characters for accurate equivalence checking.

nodes.name: check_numbers
nodes.summary: Batch reward function that extracts and normalizes answers from model completions, runs math_verify for correctness checking, and optionally logs debug information to GCS for failed verifications.

nodes.name: check_correctness
nodes.summary: Checks if an extracted response matches any acceptable answer by normalizing both sides and using math_verify with sympy parsing for robust symbolic and numeric equivalence checking.

nodes.name: get_optimizer
nodes.summary: Constructs an optax optimizer with warmup-cosine decay learning rate schedule, optional gradient clipping, and AdamW with configurable weight decay, wrapped in inject_hyperparams for dynamic schedule tracking.

nodes.name: process_data
nodes.summary: Processes a single dataset example into the RL training format by extracting question/answer fields, applying chat templates, tokenizing with the model tokenizer, and serializing acceptable answers as JSON.

nodes.name: process_mcq
nodes.summary: Extracts multiple-choice question options from LaTeX-formatted text using regex, maps answer letters to option values, and generates all acceptable answer representations for correctness checking.

nodes.name: match_format_approximately
nodes.summary: Scores model completions on approximate format compliance by checking for balanced think/answer tags with graduated penalties for missing or extra tags.

nodes.name: match_format_exactly
nodes.summary: Scores model completions on exact format compliance, returning 1.0 for completions that match the expected think/answer format regex and 0.0 otherwise.

nodes.name: get_correctness_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes aggregate correctness metrics from rewards and advantages arrays, including all-correct rate, all-wrong rate, and mean correct fraction for RL training monitoring.

nodes.name: format_maxtext_messages
nodes.summary: Formats chat messages using MaxText's template configuration, applying system prompt and user/assistant turn templates for the RL training pipeline.

nodes.name: math_verify_func
nodes.summary: Delegates math verification to the multiprocessing pool, dispatching items and collecting scores with configurable timeout and process count from the trainer config.

nodes.name: MaxTextChatParser
nodes.summary: Chat template parser subclass that formats messages using MaxText's template configuration before delegating to the Tunix agentic chat template parser for structured turn extraction.

nodes.name: get_match_format_regex
nodes.summary: Compiles and validates the format-matching regex from the trainer config, ensuring it can match the expected think/answer output format before use in scoring.

nodes.name: extract_answer
nodes.summary: Extracts the final answer from a model response using a fallback regex pattern, returning the last match or a sentinel value if no answer is found.

nodes.name: preprocess_math_string
nodes.summary: Preprocesses a math string by normalizing the final answer and fixing LaTeX escaping for consistent comparison.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the multimodal module containing only the Apache 2.0 license header; serves as the package marker for multimodal preprocessing utilities.

nodes.name: processor.py
nodes.summary: Central multimodal data preprocessing router that dispatches image, video, audio, and text-fusion operations to model-specific processors (Gemma3, Gemma4, Llama4, Qwen3-Omni) based on model name configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses lazy imports (import-outside-toplevel) in each dispatch branch to avoid importing all model processors at startup, reducing startup cost when only one model is used.

nodes.name: preprocess_mm_data
nodes.summary: Dispatches multimodal data preprocessing to the appropriate model-specific handler (Gemma3/4, Llama4, Qwen3-Omni) based on the config's model name, loading images and returning a PreprocessorOutput.

nodes.name: preprocess_image_for_training
nodes.summary: Routes a single image through the appropriate model-specific preprocessing pipeline for training data augmentation.

nodes.name: get_image_offsets
nodes.summary: Computes the increase in total token count after inserting image placeholder tokens, routing to the correct model-specific offset calculator.

nodes.name: reformat_prompt
nodes.summary: Reformats text prompts for multimodal models by dispatching to model-specific prompt formatting functions that insert image and video placeholders.

nodes.name: reformat_response
nodes.summary: Appends model-specific end-of-turn tokens to responses (e.g., <|eot|> for Llama4, <end_of_turn> for Gemma, <|im_end|> for Qwen).

nodes.name: prepare_text_for_image_fusion
nodes.summary: Prepares text tokens for image-text fusion by dispatching to model-specific functions that insert special image tokens into the token sequence.

nodes.name: get_dummy_image_shape_for_init
nodes.summary: Returns the dummy image tensor shape needed for model initialization, delegating to model-specific shape providers.

nodes.name: get_dummy_audio_shape_for_init
nodes.summary: Returns the dummy audio tensor shape (batch, mel_bins, audio_length) for model initialization; currently only supports Qwen3-Omni.

nodes.name: get_bidirectional_mask_vision
nodes.summary: Creates a bidirectional attention mask for vision tokens by comparing decoder input tokens against model-specific image/video placeholder token IDs.

nodes.name: get_bidirectional_mask_audio
nodes.summary: Creates a bidirectional attention mask for audio token merging; currently only implemented for Qwen3-Omni.

nodes.name: processor_gemma3.py
nodes.summary: Gemma3-specific multimodal processor that handles image preprocessing (resize, normalize, pad), token sequence insertion for vision-language fusion, and prompt reformatting for Gemma3 model variants.

nodes.name: Gemma3PreprocessorOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding Gemma3-specific preprocessor output including the number of images, pixel values array, and pixel mask for vision processing.

nodes.name: preprocess_mm_data_gemma3
nodes.summary: Preprocesses images for Gemma3 by converting to PIL, resizing to 896x896, normalizing pixel values, and packaging into a Gemma3PreprocessorOutput.

nodes.name: insert_sequence
nodes.summary: Inserts a token sequence at specified positions within a padded token array, computing new positions for existing tokens and handling per-image soft-token placeholders for vision-language fusion.

nodes.name: add_extra_tokens_for_images_gemma3
nodes.summary: Expands token sequences by inserting Gemma3-specific image soft-token placeholders at image boundary positions, computing required offsets for multiple images.

nodes.name: _get_new_text_positions
nodes.summary: Calculates new positions for text tokens after inserting image token sequences by applying cumulative offset adjustments.

nodes.name: processor_gemma4.py
nodes.summary: Gemma4-specific multimodal processor implementing image preprocessing, token insertion, and prompt reformatting for Gemma4 model variants, with support for Pan-and-Scan tiling and XY position tracking.

nodes.name: Gemma4PreprocessorOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass for Gemma4 preprocessor output extending the Gemma3 format with positions_xy field for Pan-and-Scan tile position tracking.

nodes.name: preprocess_mm_data_gemma4
nodes.summary: Preprocesses images for Gemma4 by converting to PIL, resizing to fixed resolution, and producing a Gemma4PreprocessorOutput with pixel values and masks.

nodes.name: insert_sequence
nodes.summary: Inserts token sequences at specified positions in Gemma4 token arrays, similar to Gemma3 but adapted for Gemma4 token conventions.

nodes.name: add_extra_tokens_for_images_gemma4
nodes.summary: Expands Gemma4 token sequences by inserting model-specific image soft-token placeholders at image boundary positions.

nodes.name: processor_llama4.py
nodes.summary: Llama4-specific multimodal processor implementing advanced image tiling with aspect-ratio-aware resolution selection, tile splitting, padding, and token insertion for Llama4 vision-language models.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a custom aspect-ratio-aware tiling system that finds optimal resolutions for images using factor decomposition, avoiding quality loss from naive resize.

nodes.name: Llama4PreprocessorOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass for Llama4 preprocessor output storing pixel values, masks, and per-image aspect ratios needed for variable-resolution tile-based processing.

nodes.name: get_factors
nodes.summary: Computes all integer factor pairs of a given number, used for determining valid tile aspect ratios.

nodes.name: find_supported_resolutions
nodes.summary: Enumerates all supported image resolutions based on max tile count and tile size, grouping by aspect ratio for optimal resolution selection.

nodes.name: get_best_resolution
nodes.summary: Selects the optimal target resolution from supported resolutions that best matches the input image's aspect ratio while respecting canvas constraints.

nodes.name: pad_to_best_fit_jax
nodes.summary: Pads images to the best-fit target resolution using configurable background color, handling batch dimensions and multi-channel images with precise slice-based copying.

nodes.name: split_to_tiles
nodes.summary: Splits padded images into tiles of specified height and width counts using reshape and transpose operations, supporting both batched and single image inputs.

nodes.name: preprocess_mm_data_llama4
nodes.summary: Full Llama4 image preprocessing pipeline: selects best resolution, pads to fit, normalizes, splits to tiles, resizes individual tiles, and produces a Llama4PreprocessorOutput with aspect ratio metadata.

nodes.name: get_tokens_for_this_image
nodes.summary: Generates the token sequence for a single tiled image by creating patch tokens with row and tile separators based on aspect ratio dimensions.

nodes.name: add_extra_tokens_for_images_llama4
nodes.summary: Replaces consecutive image placeholder tokens with properly structured tile-aware patch token sequences using aspect ratio information from the preprocessor output.

nodes.name: get_num_tokens_for_this_image
nodes.summary: Calculates the total number of tokens needed for a tiled image based on its aspect ratio, patch count, and separator tokens.

nodes.name: get_image_offsets_llama4
nodes.summary: Computes the net token count increase from inserting Llama4 image tokens by summing per-image token requirements minus original placeholder counts.

nodes.name: reformat_prompt_llama4
nodes.summary: Reformats prompts for Llama4 by replacing generic image placeholders with model-specific image tokens.

nodes.name: pad_to_max_tiles
nodes.summary: Zero-pads tile arrays and masks to the maximum tile count for uniform batch processing of variable-tile images.

nodes.name: processor_qwen3_omni.py
nodes.summary: Qwen3-Omni multimodal processor supporting images, video, and audio preprocessing with smart resizing, video frame extraction via decord, mel-spectrogram audio feature extraction, and 3D rotary position embedding (M-RoPE) computation for vision-language-audio fusion.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements 3D Multimodal Rotary Position Embedding (M-RoPE) that assigns separate temporal, height, and width position indices for vision tokens and 1D positions for text/audio, enabling the model to understand spatial and temporal relationships.

nodes.name: QwenTokens
nodes.summary: Encapsulates special token IDs for Qwen model variants, dynamically loading vision_start, vision_end, image_pad, video_pad, audio_start, audio_end, and audio_pad token IDs from the tokenizer based on model-specific configurations.

nodes.name: Qwen3OmniPreprocessorOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding Qwen3-Omni preprocessor output for all modalities: image pixel values with grid THW, video values with per-grid timing, and audio mel features with lengths and masks.

nodes.name: smart_resize
nodes.summary: Resizes image dimensions to be multiples of a given factor while respecting min/max pixel constraints, optimizing for minimal distortion.

nodes.name: pre_process_qwen3_image
nodes.summary: Preprocesses images for Qwen3-Omni by applying smart resize, normalization, spatial merge, and patch extraction to produce grid-aware pixel values and THW metadata.

nodes.name: calculate_video_frame_range
nodes.summary: Calculates the valid start and end frame indices for video processing based on configuration parameters like fps limits, video_start, and video_end timestamps.

nodes.name: smart_nframes
nodes.summary: Determines the optimal number of video frames to extract, respecting min/max constraints and ensuring the count is compatible with temporal merge factors.

nodes.name: _read_video_decord
nodes.summary: Reads video frames from a file using the decord library, calculating frame range and sampling uniformly spaced frames with FPS and second-per-grid metadata.

nodes.name: preprocess_video
nodes.summary: Full video preprocessing pipeline: resizes frames, normalizes pixel values, applies temporal/spatial merging, and produces grid-aware patch arrays compatible with the vision encoder.

nodes.name: _np_extract_fbank_features
nodes.summary: Extracts mel-filterbank audio features from raw waveform using windowed STFT and mel-scale projection with log-scaling, producing spectrogram features for the audio encoder.

nodes.name: preprocess_mm_data_qwen3_omni
nodes.summary: Main Qwen3-Omni multimodal preprocessor that handles images, videos, and audio from config paths, producing a unified Qwen3OmniPreprocessorOutput with all modality features.

nodes.name: add_extra_tokens_for_qwen3_omni
nodes.summary: Expands token sequences for Qwen3-Omni by replacing vision/audio placeholder markers with properly sized pad token sequences based on image grid dimensions, video frame counts, and audio feature lengths.

nodes.name: get_rope_index
nodes.summary: Computes 3D Multimodal Rotary Position Embedding (M-RoPE) indices for mixed image/video/audio/text sequences, assigning separate temporal, height, and width position dimensions for vision tokens and 1D positions for text and audio.
nodes.languageNotes: This 255-line function is the most complex piece in the module, implementing the M-RoPE algorithm from the Qwen2-VL paper with extensions for audio-in-video and multi-modal position deltas.

nodes.name: get_llm_pos_ids_for_vision
nodes.summary: Generates 3D position IDs (temporal, height, width) for vision tokens in the LLM sequence, computing separate spatial grid positions and temporal indices for each frame.

nodes.name: get_chunked_index
nodes.summary: Splits a list of token indices into fixed-size chunks for processing multimodal token sequences, optionally removing a specific index from chunks.

nodes.name: reformat_prompt_qwen3_omni
nodes.summary: Reformats prompts for Qwen3-Omni by replacing generic image and video placeholders with model-specific vision tokens wrapped in vision_start/vision_end markers.

nodes.name: get_mm_offsets_qwen3_omni
nodes.summary: Computes the total token offset introduced by all multimodal tokens (image patches, video frames, audio features) for Qwen3-Omni models.

nodes.name: pre_process_audio_qwen3_omni
nodes.summary: Preprocesses raw audio arrays for Qwen3-Omni by padding to the required chunk length and extracting mel-filterbank features with an attention mask.

nodes.name: preprocess_mm_data_qwen3_omni_for_training
nodes.summary: Training-specific Qwen3-Omni image preprocessing that handles both single images and image lists, producing a Qwen3OmniPreprocessorOutput without video or audio data.

nodes.name: _get_feat_extract_output_lengths
nodes.summary: Calculates the output sequence length after audio feature extraction convolutional layers, accounting for kernel sizes and strides of two sequential convolutions.

nodes.name: gemma4_layers_test.py
nodes.summary: Parity tests validating that MaxText JAX implementations of Gemma4 vision layers (patch embedder, rotary embedding, attention, encoder blocks, pooler, and projector) produce numerically matching outputs against their PyTorch reference implementations.

nodes.name: gemma4_small_layers_test.py
nodes.summary: Parity tests for Gemma4 Small text-model layers including per-layer embedding (PLE), donor attention (sliding and global), and shared KV-cache attention layers, comparing JAX implementations against HuggingFace PyTorch references.

nodes.name: qwen3_5_layers_test.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end parity test verifying the Qwen3.5 MoE vision encoder and patch merger produce outputs matching the PyTorch reference, including weight copying and multi-image input validation.

nodes.name: qwen3_omni_layers_test.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive parity test suite for Qwen3 Omni MoE multimodal layers, covering vision (attention, patch merger, MLP, patch embed, rotary embedding, position embedding interpolation, encoder end-to-end), audio (attention, encoder layer, positional embedding, full encoder, full audio model), deepstack visual token processing, and multimodal preprocessing.

nodes.name: multimodal_test_utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared test utility library providing helper functions for multimodal model parity testing: random tensor creation, numerical comparison between JAX and PyTorch, and weight-copying routines for linear, norm, conv, attention, encoder, and vision layers.

nodes.name: copy_gemma4_attention_weights
nodes.summary: Copies attention weights (Q, K, V, output projections and RMS norms) from a PyTorch Gemma4 vision attention module to its JAX counterpart, reshaping weight matrices for head-dimension compatibility.

nodes.name: copy_gemma4_vision_encoder_weights
nodes.summary: Copies all weights from a PyTorch Gemma4 vision encoder to JAX, iterating over encoder blocks to transfer attention, MLP, and normalization weights, plus optional std_bias and std_scale parameters.

nodes.name: TestGemma4VisionAttention
nodes.summary: Test case verifying Gemma4 vision attention layer produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch implementations, including rotary embedding application.

nodes.name: TestGemma4VisionEncoderEndToEnd
nodes.summary: End-to-end parity test for the full Gemma4 vision encoder pipeline from pixel patches through all encoder layers, comparing JAX and PyTorch hidden state outputs.

nodes.name: TestGemma4VisionEntry
nodes.summary: Test case verifying Gemma4 vision patch embedder produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch implementations.

nodes.name: TestGemma4VisionRotaryEmbedding
nodes.summary: Test case verifying Gemma4 vision rotary embedding produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch, using multidimensional position encoding.

nodes.name: TestGemma4VisionEncoderBlock
nodes.summary: Test case verifying a single Gemma4 vision encoder block (attention + MLP + norms) produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch implementations.

nodes.name: TestGemma4VisionExit
nodes.summary: Test case verifying Gemma4 vision pooler (exit layer) produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch implementations.

nodes.name: _build_jax_config
nodes.summary: Builds a minimal JAX pyconfig configuration for Gemma4 Small model testing, specifying head dimensions, attention types, layer structure, and KV sharing patterns.

nodes.name: _build_torch_text_config
nodes.summary: Constructs a HuggingFace Gemma4TextConfig from JAX config parameters, translating MaxText config fields to the corresponding HuggingFace format for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _make_jax_attention
nodes.summary: Creates a JAX MaxText Attention layer configured for Gemma4 Small testing, supporting different attention types (sliding, global) and optional KV sharing.

nodes.name: TestGemma4SmallAttentionDonor
nodes.summary: Test class verifying Gemma4 Small donor attention layers (both sliding-window and global variants) produce matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch implementations.

nodes.name: TestGemma4SmallAttentionShared
nodes.summary: Test class verifying Gemma4 Small shared-KV attention layer, which reuses key-value projections from a donor layer, produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch implementations.

nodes.name: TestQwen3_5MoeVisionEncoderEndToEnd
nodes.summary: End-to-end test verifying the full Qwen3.5 MoE vision encoder and projector pipeline produces matching outputs between JAX and PyTorch, with multi-image grid input handling.

nodes.name: copy_qwen3_5_vision_encoder_weights
nodes.summary: Copies vision encoder weights from PyTorch Qwen3.5 model to JAX, including patch embeddings, positional embeddings, layer norms, attention, and MLP weights across all encoder blocks.

nodes.name: TestQwen3OmniMoeVisionAttention
nodes.summary: Test class verifying Qwen3 Omni MoE vision attention produces matching outputs against PyTorch and is JIT-compilable.

nodes.name: TestQwen3OmniMoeVisionPatchMerger
nodes.summary: Test class verifying Qwen3 Omni vision patch merger with and without post-shuffle matches PyTorch output and is JIT-compilable.

nodes.name: TestQwen3OmniMoeVisionPatchEmbed
nodes.summary: Test class verifying Qwen3 Omni vision patch embedding layer matches PyTorch output and is JIT-compilable.

nodes.name: TestQwen3OmniMoeVisionRotaryEmbedding
nodes.summary: Test class verifying Qwen3 Omni vision rotary embedding (grid-based cos/sin computation and rotation application) matches PyTorch reference implementation.

nodes.name: TestQwen3OmniMoeVisionEncoderEndToEnd
nodes.summary: End-to-end test verifying the complete Qwen3 Omni vision encoder and projector pipeline, comparing intermediate layer outputs and final projections against PyTorch.

nodes.name: TestDeepstackProcess
nodes.summary: Test class verifying the deepstack visual token insertion process correctly scatters visual tokens into hidden states at designated positions, matching a reference scatter implementation.

nodes.name: TestQwen3OmniPreprocessing
nodes.summary: Integration test verifying Qwen3 Omni multimodal preprocessing produces matching tokenized inputs, pixel values, video values, and audio features compared to HuggingFace processor outputs.

nodes.name: TestMaxTextAudioAttentionVsPyTorch
nodes.summary: Test class verifying MaxText audio attention layer produces matching outputs against PyTorch Qwen3 Omni audio attention, with detailed weight copying and configuration matching.

nodes.name: TestAudioEncoderLayer
nodes.summary: Test class verifying a single audio encoder layer matches PyTorch with different batch sizes and confirms JIT compilability.

nodes.name: TestAudioEncoder
nodes.summary: Test class verifying the full audio encoder (convolutions, positional embedding, transformer layers, layer norm) matches PyTorch step-by-step.

nodes.name: TestAudioModel
nodes.summary: End-to-end test for the complete audio model including encoder and projector, verifying both final output parity and intermediate convolution/positional embedding stages.

nodes.name: create_random_jax_torch
nodes.summary: Creates matching random tensors in both JAX and PyTorch formats from the same numpy random data, ensuring identical initialization for parity testing.

nodes.name: split_into_patches
nodes.summary: Splits an image tensor into spatial and temporal patches by reshaping along height, width, and temporal dimensions.

nodes.name: assert_all_close_jax_torch
nodes.summary: Asserts numerical closeness between a JAX array and a PyTorch tensor, converting both to numpy for comparison with configurable relative and absolute tolerances.

nodes.name: copy_attention_weights_to_maxtext
nodes.summary: Copies attention weights from PyTorch to MaxText JAX format, supporting both fused QKV and separate Q/K/V projection weight layouts, with proper head-dimension reshaping.

nodes.name: copy_encoder_weights
nodes.summary: Copies full encoder weights including convolutional layers, linear projections, positional embeddings, layer norm, and per-layer encoder weights from PyTorch to JAX.

nodes.name: copy_maxtext_audio_encoder_weights
nodes.summary: Copies audio encoder weights from PyTorch to MaxText JAX format, including convolutional feature extraction layers, linear projections, layer norm, and all transformer encoder layers.

nodes.name: copy_vision_encoder_weights
nodes.summary: Copies vision encoder weights from PyTorch to JAX MaxText format, including patch embeddings, positional embeddings, per-block attention/MLP/norm weights, and merger stage weights.

nodes.name: create_block_diagonal_attention_mask
nodes.summary: Creates a block-diagonal attention mask from cumulative sequence lengths, used for packed sequences where each segment attends only to itself.

nodes.name: copy_densegeneral_qkv_weights
nodes.summary: Copies Q/K/V linear projection weights from PyTorch to JAX DenseGeneral format, reshaping from flat to multi-head layout.

nodes.name: copy_densegeneral_out_weights
nodes.summary: Copies attention output projection weights from PyTorch to JAX DenseGeneral format, reshaping from flat to multi-head layout.

nodes.name: copy_maxtext_encoder_layer_weights
nodes.summary: Copies a single encoder layer's weights (layer norms, attention, MLP) from PyTorch to MaxText JAX format.

nodes.name: maxtext_generator.py
nodes.summary: Core inference engine that wraps the MaxText model, tokenizer, and JAX-based generation logic. Handles batched prefill, autoregressive decoding, sampling strategies, and multimodal input preprocessing on TPUs/GPUs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX JIT compilation for decode state operations and JAX random key splitting for batched sampling across multiple devices.

nodes.name: MaxTextGenerator
nodes.summary: Reusable class for parallel text generation using MaxText. Initializes the MaxEngine, loads model parameters, manages decode state, and orchestrates the full generate-batch pipeline including prefill, generation loop, and completion building.

nodes.name: LogProbs
nodes.summary: Dataclass storing detailed log probability information for a sequence of generated tokens, including token IDs, per-token log probabilities, and character offsets.

nodes.name: Completion
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a single completed generation result, containing the generated text, token IDs, log probabilities, finish reason, and token counts.

nodes.name: GenerationStream
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding mutable state for a single generation stream within a batch, tracking input tokens, generated IDs, log probabilities, and completion status.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Standalone entry point that instantiates MaxTextGenerator, runs generate_batch with example prompts, dumps completions for debugging, and measures throughput with a second timed batch run.

nodes.name: maxtext_server.py
nodes.summary: OpenAI-compatible API server built with FastAPI that exposes /v1/completions and /v1/chat/completions endpoints. Coordinates a multi-process JAX inference loop where the rank-0 process runs the HTTP server and all processes participate in batched generation via JAX multihost broadcast.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX multihost_utils.broadcast_one_to_all for cross-process request distribution and asyncio for non-blocking request queuing in the FastAPI event loop.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Server entry point that initializes the MaxTextGenerator, starts the uvicorn HTTP server thread on rank 0, and enters the main inference loop across all JAX processes.

nodes.name: main_loop
nodes.summary: Continuous batched inference loop: rank 0 collects queued requests and broadcasts payload to all JAX hosts; all hosts run generate_batch; rank 0 assembles and distributes responses.

nodes.name: _queue_and_wait_for_response
nodes.summary: Async function that places an incoming API request on the processing queue with a unique ID and polls for the response in a shared dictionary, raising HTTPException on timeout.

nodes.name: _build_chat_completion_response
nodes.summary: Constructs an OpenAI-compatible ChatCompletionResponse from raw generation results, handling Harmony encoding parsing, DeepSeek-V3.2 encoding, stop sequence truncation, and log probability formatting.

nodes.name: _build_completion_response
nodes.summary: Constructs an OpenAI-compatible CompletionResponse from raw generation completions, applying stop sequence truncation, log probability conversion, and token usage accounting.

nodes.name: _prepare_batch_for_broadcast
nodes.summary: Transforms a list of queued request items into a JSON payload suitable for JAX multihost broadcast, extracting prompts, sampling parameters, and building a request-to-completion mapping.

nodes.name: _collect_batched_requests
nodes.summary: Drains the request queue into a batch list, waiting up to BATCH_TIMEOUT_S seconds to accumulate requests before returning the collected items.

nodes.name: _process_results
nodes.summary: Maps generation completions back to their originating requests, builds the appropriate response objects, and places them in the shared response dictionary for async retrieval.

nodes.name: server_models.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic model definitions for the OpenAI-compatible API server, covering request validation (CompletionRequest, ChatCompletionRequest), response structures (CompletionResponse, ChatCompletionResponse), and shared types (SamplingParams, Usage, LogProbsPayload) using generic base classes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Pydantic Generic[TypeVar] pattern for BaseCompletionResponse to share fields across completion and chat response models while varying the choice type.

nodes.name: SamplingParams
nodes.summary: Pydantic base model defining common sampling parameters (max_tokens, temperature, top_p, top_k, stream, stop, seed) shared across completion and chat request types.

nodes.name: CompletionRequest
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for standard text completion requests, extending SamplingParams with model, prompt (supporting strings, token ID lists, or nested lists), echo, and logprobs fields with validation.

nodes.name: BaseCompletionResponse
nodes.summary: Generic Pydantic base response model parameterized by ChoiceType, providing shared fields (id, object, created, model, choices, usage) for both completion and chat completion responses.

nodes.name: ChatCompletionRequest
