nodes.summary: Pydantic model for chat completion requests, extending SamplingParams with a messages list (ChatMessage objects), model ID, and optional logprobs configuration.

nodes.name: ChatCompletionResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic response model for chat completion requests, specializing BaseCompletionResponse with ChatCompletionChoice and providing chat-specific defaults for id and object fields.

nodes.name: server_utils.py
nodes.summary: Utility functions for the MaxText API server providing prompt normalization, OpenAI-compatible log probability formatting, token counting, stop sequence truncation, debug logging, and DeepSeek-V3.2 encoding detection.

nodes.name: get_prompts_for_request
nodes.summary: Extracts and formats prompts from either CompletionRequest or ChatCompletionRequest objects, handling chat template application, DeepSeek-V3.2 encoding, and prompt normalization.

nodes.name: to_openai_logprobs
nodes.summary: Converts internal LogProbs objects to OpenAI-compatible LogProbsPayload format, decoding token IDs to strings, sanitizing non-finite values, and optionally populating top_logprobs.

nodes.name: apply_stops_to_text_and_logprobs
nodes.summary: Truncates generated text and the corresponding LogProbsPayload at the earliest occurrence of any stop sequence, using bisect for efficient token-offset alignment.

nodes.name: normalize_prompts
nodes.summary: Normalizes the flexible OpenAI prompt field (string, string list, token ID list, or nested token ID lists) into a uniform list of decoded string prompts.

nodes.name: log_debug_event
nodes.summary: Writes a structured JSON debug log entry (with timestamp, request ID, and event content) to a JSONL file when DEBUG_MODE is enabled.

nodes.name: count_tokens
nodes.summary: Counts the number of tokens in a string using the underlying tokenizer directly, bypassing the JetStream wrapper to avoid single-token padding issues.

nodes.name: decode_one_prompt
nodes.summary: Decodes a single prompt element that may be either a string (returned as-is) or a list of token IDs (decoded via the tokenizer).

nodes.name: finite_or_none
nodes.summary: Returns a float value only if it is finite (not NaN or infinity), otherwise returns None to sanitize log probability values.

nodes.name: decode_token_id
nodes.summary: Decodes a single integer token ID into its string representation using the MaxTextGenerator's tokenizer.

nodes.name: is_dsv32_encoding_enabled
nodes.summary: Checks whether DeepSeek-V3.2 specific encoding should be applied for a given model name, controlled by model name matching and an environment variable override.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines abstract base classes for benchmark evaluation datasets, including the SampleRequest named tuple for inference prompts and the BenchmarkDataset ABC that all concrete dataset loaders must implement.

nodes.name: SampleRequest
nodes.summary: Named tuple representing a single inference request containing a prompt, ground-truth reference answer, and optional metadata for scoring.

nodes.name: BenchmarkDataset
nodes.summary: Abstract base class requiring subclasses to implement sample_requests() to load and return a list of SampleRequest objects for benchmark evaluation.

nodes.name: mlperf.py
nodes.summary: Implements the MLPerf OpenOrca summarization dataset loader, streaming from the Open-Orca/OpenOrca HuggingFace dataset and applying chat templates to produce SampleRequest objects.

nodes.name: MlperfOpenOrcaDataset
nodes.summary: Concrete BenchmarkDataset implementation that streams the Open-Orca/OpenOrca HuggingFace dataset, applies chat templating with a system prompt, and returns SampleRequest objects for MLPerf Inference summarization benchmarking.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Provides a name-to-class registry for benchmark datasets, allowing lookup and instantiation of BenchmarkDataset subclasses by benchmark name, and supporting registration of custom dataset loaders.

nodes.name: get_dataset
nodes.summary: Looks up a benchmark name in the dataset registry and returns a new instance of the corresponding BenchmarkDataset subclass, raising KeyError with available options if the name is not found.

nodes.name: warmup.py
nodes.summary: Sends warmup HTTP requests to a vLLM server's completions endpoint before evaluation to trigger XLA compilation, with support for prompt-length bucketing based on dataset samples or a simple fallback prompt.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio with aiohttp for concurrent warmup requests, with a lazy import of aiohttp inside the async function to keep module-level imports lightweight.

nodes.name: _sample_by_buckets
nodes.summary: Selects one representative SampleRequest per prompt-length bucket from predefined word-count ranges to ensure warmup covers diverse input sizes.

nodes.name: _send_warmup_requests
nodes.summary: Asynchronously sends warmup completion requests to the vLLM server via aiohttp, gathering all responses concurrently and logging any failures.

nodes.name: warmup_server
nodes.summary: Public entry point that builds warmup prompt pairs either from bucketed dataset samples or a simple fallback prompt, then runs them asynchronously against the vLLM server to trigger XLA compilation.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the matmul benchmarking module within the MLPerf inference suite. Contains only the Apache 2.0 license header with no executable code.

nodes.name: matmul_dtypes.py
nodes.summary: Benchmarking script that profiles JAX batch matrix multiplication across various dtype combinations (int4, int8, bfloat16) to measure performance characteristics for MLPerf inference workloads.

nodes.name: timing_util.py
nodes.summary: Provides a timing utility function that benchmarks JAX operations over multiple runs with optional JAX profiler tracing support, returning average execution time.

nodes.name: simple_timeit
nodes.summary: Times a JAX function over multiple runs with a warmup call, optional JAX profiler tracing, and returns the average execution time in seconds.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses jax.block_until_ready to ensure asynchronous JAX operations complete before timing measurements, which is essential for accurate benchmarking on accelerators.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the attention kernels module that re-exports the splash_attention_kernel submodule, making Splash Attention and related utilities available as a single import.

nodes.name: jax_flash_attention.py
nodes.summary: Pure JAX implementation of block-sparse Flash Attention without Pallas, using nested fori_loops over query and key/value blocks with block-level mask skipping for efficiency.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the online softmax trick (tracking running max and log-sum-exp) within nested JAX lax.fori_loop iterations over query and key/value blocks, avoiding materializing the full attention matrix.

nodes.name: flash_attention_block_masked
nodes.summary: Computes masked flash attention using a block-sparse approach with nested loops over key/value and query blocks, supporting segment IDs, logit soft-capping, and optional residual saving.

nodes.name: mask_blocker
nodes.summary: Reshapes a 2D attention mask into blocks and computes per-block nonzero counts to determine which blocks can be skipped during block-sparse attention.

nodes.name: splash_attention_kernel.py
nodes.summary: Core implementation of Splash Attention (Sparse Flash Attention) targeting TPUs via JAX Pallas, providing forward and backward Pallas kernels, custom VJP rules, block-sparse masking, multi-query attention support, and configurable block sizes for high-performance attention computation.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavily uses JAX Pallas (pl.pallas_call, pltpu.PrefetchScalarGridSpec) for TPU kernel programming. Employs functools.partial and jax.custom_vjp decorators to define custom forward/backward passes. Registered as a JAX pytree node for compatibility with JAX transformations.

nodes.name: SegmentIds
nodes.summary: NamedTuple holding query and key/value segment ID arrays used to prevent cross-attention between concatenated sequence segments.

nodes.name: BlockSizes
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass configuring block dimensions for forward and backward Splash Attention kernels, including separate Q/KV block sizes for dQ and dKV gradient computations, and QKV memory layout settings.

nodes.name: QKVLayout
nodes.summary: Enum defining memory layout options (HEAD_DIM_MINOR vs HEAD_DIM_MAJOR) for query, key, and value tensors in Splash Attention.

nodes.name: SplashAttentionKernel
nodes.summary: JAX pytree-registered callable object encapsulating Splash Attention with pre-computed mask info, providing __call__ for standard execution plus manual_fwd/manual_bwd methods for shard_map-compatible manual sharding.

nodes.name: flash_attention_kernel
nodes.summary: Pallas kernel implementing the forward pass of Splash Attention on TPU, iterating over key/value blocks with block-sparse masking, online softmax accumulation, and configurable QKV layouts.

nodes.name: _splash_attention_forward
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the forward pass of Splash Attention by configuring Pallas grid specs, block specs, and scratch buffers, then invoking the flash_attention_kernel via pl.pallas_call on TPU.

nodes.name: _flash_attention_dq_kernel
nodes.summary: Pallas kernel computing the dQ (query gradient) portion of the Splash Attention backward pass on TPU, iterating over key/value blocks with block-sparse masking.

nodes.name: _splash_attention_bwd_dq
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the backward pass for dQ by configuring Pallas grid specs, block specs, and calling _flash_attention_dq_kernel via pl.pallas_call.

nodes.name: _flash_attention_dkv_kernel
nodes.summary: Pallas kernel computing dK and dV (key/value gradients) for Splash Attention backward pass on TPU, supporting both fused and separate backward kernel modes with MQA head accumulation.

nodes.name: _splash_attention_bwd_dkv
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the backward pass for dK/dV by configuring Pallas grid specs, block specs, and calling _flash_attention_dkv_kernel via pl.pallas_call, with support for fused backward kernel mode.

nodes.name: _splash_attention_bwd
nodes.summary: Top-level backward pass for Splash Attention that computes di (per-element correction), then dispatches to _splash_attention_bwd_dkv and optionally _splash_attention_bwd_dq to compute all gradients.

nodes.name: _splash_attention
nodes.summary: Entry point for Splash Attention with custom VJP, collapsing partial mask blocks and delegating to _splash_attention_custom for combined forward/backward execution.

nodes.name: _make_splash_attention
nodes.summary: Factory function that processes masks into MaskInfo structures for forward and backward passes, then constructs a SplashAttentionKernel with pre-computed block-sparse mask metadata.

nodes.name: make_attention_reference
nodes.summary: Creates a reference attention implementation that wraps the core attention_reference function with proper reshaping for MQA, head vmapping, and optional custom backward support.

nodes.name: _apply_mask_and_soft_cap
nodes.summary: Applies block-sparse masks, segment ID masks, optional mask functions, and logit soft-capping to attention logits within a Pallas kernel, combining multiple mask sources via logical AND.

nodes.name: _next_nonzero
nodes.summary: Helper function used in Pallas kernels to find the next nonzero block index in the block mask, supporting the block-sparse iteration pattern of Splash Attention.

nodes.name: attention_reference
nodes.summary: Standard reference implementation of masked multi-head attention using full materialization, primarily used for correctness testing against the optimized Splash Attention kernels.

nodes.name: _attention_reference_custom_bwd
nodes.summary: Custom backward pass for the reference attention implementation, computing dQ, dK, dV gradients analytically to validate gradient correctness of the optimized kernels.

nodes.name: get_kernel_name
nodes.summary: Generates a unique kernel name string from block metadata, MQA flag, residual saving, segmentation, and computation phase for Pallas kernel identification.

nodes.name: ragged_gather.py
nodes.summary: Implements a TPU sparse-core Pallas kernel for ragged gather operations, enabling efficient indexed row extraction from 2D arrays using DMA transfers between HBM and VMEM with sub-word packing support.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX Pallas TPU primitives (pl.kernel, pltpu.make_async_copy) to program low-level DMA transfers on TPU sparse cores, with bitwise packing for sub-32-bit datatypes.

nodes.name: main_kernel
nodes.summary: Core Pallas kernel body that orchestrates sparse-core DMA reads of indexed rows from HBM into VMEM, handles sub-32-bit bitcasting and lane-aligned column partitioning, and writes results back via async copies.

nodes.name: calculate_col_size
nodes.summary: Determines the optimal column partition size for the ragged gather kernel based on TPU chip version and available VMEM capacity, ensuring data fits within scratch memory limits.

nodes.name: ragged_gather
nodes.summary: Public JIT-compiled entry point that gathers rows from a 2D array using indices within a dynamic [start, end) range, dispatching to the sparse-core Pallas kernel when available or falling back to standard JAX indexing.

nodes.name: ragged_gather_reduce.py
nodes.summary: Implements a TPU sparse-core Pallas kernel for ragged gather-reduce (scatter-add) operations, gathering rows by index, applying weights and validity masks, then reducing groups of rows by summation -- optimized for MoE unpermute with sparse expert parallelism.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles sub-32-bit dtypes via bitcasting with explicit bit-shift packing/unpacking, and includes a JAX fallback path for environments without sparse core hardware or small tensors.

nodes.name: _fallback_implementation
nodes.summary: Pure JAX fallback that performs indexed gather, weight scaling, masking, and group-wise summation when TPU sparse core is unavailable or the input is small enough for tensor-core VMEM.

nodes.name: main_kernel
nodes.summary: Core Pallas kernel body for ragged gather-reduce that reads indexed rows via sparse-core DMA, applies per-row weights, accumulates rows belonging to the same reduction group across row partitions, and writes reduced results back to HBM.

nodes.name: _preprocess
nodes.summary: Preprocesses indices and validity masks for the gather-reduce kernel by sorting valid rows first, computing per-partition row counts, and rearranging topk weights to match the sorted order.

nodes.name: ragged_gather_reduce
nodes.summary: Public JIT-compiled entry point that gathers, weights, masks, and reduces rows from a 2D array in groups, dispatching to the sparse-core Pallas kernel for large inputs or falling back to a pure JAX implementation for small tensors or non-SC hardware.

nodes.name: ragged_sort.py
nodes.summary: Provides differentiable ragged token sorting and unsorting operations with custom VJP rules for MoE expert-parallel token routing, supporting both ring-based (AG-RS) and all-to-all permutation strategies.
nodes.languageNotes: Each public function defines a nested jax.custom_vjp closure pair (forward + backward) to provide correct gradients through the non-differentiable permutation, using ragged_gather in forward and ragged_gather_reduce in backward.

nodes.name: ring_ragged_sort
nodes.summary: Sorts and gathers tokens across the global expert space for AG-RS expert parallelism, extracting only the output rows assigned to this shard's experts, with a custom VJP backward that uses ragged_gather_reduce for gradient accumulation.

nodes.name: ring_ragged_unsort
nodes.summary: Reverses the ring-based ragged sort by scattering expert outputs back to their original token positions using ragged_gather_reduce in the forward pass and ragged_gather in the backward pass.

nodes.name: a2a_ragged_sort
nodes.summary: Sorts tokens within the valid prefix of a local shard using a permutation index for all-to-all expert parallelism, with a custom VJP backward using ragged_gather_reduce along the inverse permutation.

nodes.name: a2a_ragged_unsort
nodes.summary: Dual of a2a_ragged_sort that reverses the local permutation using ragged_gather_reduce in the forward pass and ragged_gather in the backward pass, operating only on the valid prefix of each shard.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build context exclusion file that prevents the .git directory and maxtext_venv virtual environment from being included in Docker images, reducing build context size.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook configuration defining six quality checks for the repository: codespell for typo detection, pylint for Python linting, pyink for Python formatting, mdformat for Markdown formatting, and yamllint for YAML validation.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-hooks.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines reusable pre-commit hook entry points (codespell, pyink, pylint) that allow this repository to be used as a pre-commit hook source by other projects.

nodes.name: .readthedocs.yml
nodes.summary: Read the Docs configuration for building Sphinx documentation on Ubuntu 22.04 with Python 3.12, referencing the docs/conf.py Sphinx config and documentation-specific requirements.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Contribution guidelines covering Google CLA requirements, community guidelines, and the code review process using GitHub pull requests.

nodes.name: PREFLIGHT.md
nodes.summary: Performance optimization guide for multihost TPU training, covering recommended DCN network settings via preflight.sh and NUMA binding configuration for v4 and v5p TPU accelerators on GCE and GKE platforms.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project documentation for MaxText, a high-performance JAX-based LLM library targeting Google Cloud TPUs and GPUs. Covers installation, supported models (Gemma, Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, Kimi, Mistral, GPT-OSS), pre-training and post-training use cases including SFT and GRPO/GSPO, and links to the ReadTheDocs site.

nodes.name: codecov.yml
nodes.summary: Codecov configuration implementing a two-flag coverage scheme: 'regular' flag updated by PR runs for patch coverage (10% threshold) and 'scheduled' flag updated by full CI runs for project-wide coverage (5% threshold), with carryforward enabled to maintain stable scores across run types.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project configuration using Hatch as the build backend, defining the maxtext package with optional dependency groups for TPU, GPU (CUDA 12), post-training, runner, and docs environments. Specifies Python 3.12+ requirement, entry point scripts for dependency installation and Docker image management, and wheel build targets for src/maxtext and src/dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses hatch-requirements-txt plugin to dynamically resolve optional dependencies from requirements files, avoiding duplication between pip requirements and pyproject.toml.

nodes.name: pytest.ini
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration defining test discovery patterns, import mode, and custom markers for platform-specific tests (tpu_only, gpu_only, cpu_only), CI scheduling (scheduled_only), integration tests, and post-training tests. Ignores approximately 20 test files that require special hardware or external dependencies.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive documentation for the MaxText OpenAI-compatible API server, covering installation, single-host and multi-host GKE deployment, API interaction via curl and Python, and benchmarking with evaluation frameworks like lm-eval-harness and evalchemy.

nodes.name: port_forward_xpk.sh
nodes.summary: Automates finding the correct pod running the MaxText API server in a GKE cluster and establishes a kubectl port-forward connection to it on port 8000, using job name, project, zone, and cluster as inputs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses kubectl pod discovery with two fallback strategies (label-based then name-prefix match) and log inspection to identify the server pod.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency list for the MaxText API server, specifying uvicorn, fastapi, openai-harmony, and pyyaml as required packages.

nodes.name: start_server.sh
nodes.summary: Launcher script that starts the MaxText API server by resolving the project root directory and invoking the Python module benchmarks.api_server.maxtext_server with user-provided configuration arguments.

nodes.name: build_maxtext.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for building and uploading MaxText Docker images for TPU and GPU workloads, covering prerequisites, PyPI/source installation with the runner extra, Docker image building for pre-training and post-training, and uploading to Artifact Registry or GCR.

nodes.name: development.md
nodes.summary: Development section landing page that includes the CONTRIBUTING.md file and links to sub-pages on updating dependencies and contributing documentation.

nodes.name: guides.md
nodes.summary: Index page for MaxText how-to guides, organizing links to eight topic areas including optimization, data pipelines, checkpointing, monitoring and debugging, Python notebooks, model bringup, distillation, and evaluation.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML landing page for the MaxText documentation site featuring a hero section with project tagline, three feature cards (high-performance, pre-training, post-training), and a JAX AI Stack section listing related libraries including JAX, Flax, Orbax, Optax, and Tunix.

nodes.name: index.md
nodes.summary: Root documentation index for the MaxText project, embedding the HTML landing page and README news section, and defining the top-level table of contents linking to installation, building, tutorials, running, guides, reference, development, and release notes.

nodes.name: install_maxtext.md
nodes.summary: Detailed installation guide for MaxText covering PyPI and source installation modes with four target options (tpu, cuda12, tpu-post-train, runner), virtual environment setup using uv, and verification steps to confirm a healthy installation.

nodes.name: reference.md
nodes.summary: Reference documentation index providing links to four topic areas -- performance metrics, supported models, architecture overview, and core concepts -- plus an API reference section for MaxText modules, classes, and functions.

nodes.name: release_notes.md
nodes.summary: Release notes documenting MaxText versions v0.1.0 through v0.2.2, covering new model support (DeepSeek, Gemma 4, Kimi-K2, Qwen3), RL/SFT enhancements, project restructuring, new installation targets, deprecations of legacy shims, and infrastructure improvements.

nodes.name: run_maxtext.md
nodes.summary: Guide for choosing how to run MaxText, with five options: localhost/single VM, single-host GPU, at-scale with XPK on GKE, multi-host via Pathways, and decoupled mode for local development without cloud dependencies.

nodes.name: tutorials.md
nodes.summary: Tutorials index page linking to four tutorial categories: getting started (first run), pre-training with real datasets, post-training (SFT, RL), and inference on vLLM.

nodes.name: contribute_docs.md
nodes.summary: Developer guide for contributing to the MaxText documentation site, covering local Sphinx/MyST builds, adding new pages to the toctree, and the automated Read The Docs deployment pipeline.

nodes.name: hlo_diff_testing.md
nodes.summary: Explains the HLO (High-Level Optimizer) graph diff verification testing framework, which ensures refactoring PRs do not unintentionally alter XLA compiler graph lowering, and documents methods for updating HLO reference baselines via GitHub Actions or locally.
nodes.languageNotes: HLO is XLA's intermediate representation capturing low-level math operations, tensor shapes, and TPU sharding mappings. The diff test compares compiled graph structure against stored baselines.

nodes.name: update_dependencies.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for updating MaxText's Python dependencies using the seed-env tool, covering base requirement modification, JAX nightly build commit hash selection, pinned requirements generation for TPU and GPU environments, and verification.

nodes.name: checkpointing_solutions.md
nodes.summary: Index page for the checkpointing solutions guide, linking to sub-pages on GCS checkpointing, emergency checkpointing, multi-tier checkpointing, and checkpoint conversion utilities between Hugging Face and MaxText formats.

nodes.name: data_input_pipeline.md
nodes.summary: Documents MaxText's three data input pipelines (Grain, Hugging Face, TFDS) with a comparison table of supported formats, features, and limitations, plus detailed guidance on multi-host dataloading strategies for random-access and sequential-access datasets.

nodes.name: distillation.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide to MaxText's online knowledge distillation trainer covering loss anatomy (KL soft loss, hard CE, feature loss), the full configuration surface for alpha/beta/temperature schedules, recommended starter configs for pruning recovery and compression, monitoring metrics, and a troubleshooting reference.
nodes.languageNotes: The distillation loss formula uses Hinton's T-squared scaling for KL divergence, with configurable cosine/linear schedules for the alpha, beta, and temperature hyperparameters.

nodes.name: eval_framework.md
nodes.summary: Placeholder documentation page that includes content from the eval module's README via a Sphinx include directive, serving as the entry point for evaluation framework documentation.

nodes.name: model_bringup.md
nodes.summary: Community contributor guide for integrating new models into MaxText, covering a six-step standardized workflow: architecture analysis, optional feature implementation, checkpoint conversion (scanned/unscanned Orbax formats), unit testing against reference PyTorch implementations, end-to-end logit comparison and eval benchmarks, and a completion checklist with FAQ.

nodes.name: monitoring_and_debugging.md
nodes.summary: Index page for monitoring and debugging guides, linking to sub-pages on diagnostic features, GCP observability, megascale hang troubleshooting, goodput monitoring, logs and metrics interpretation, Vertex AI TensorBoard, and XProf profiling.

nodes.name: optimization.md
nodes.summary: Index page for the optimization guides, linking to sub-pages on custom model configs, sharding strategies (FSDP, TP, EP, PP with roofline analysis), Pallas kernel optimization, and benchmarking and performance tuning.

nodes.name: run_python_notebook.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for running MaxText Python notebooks on TPUs via three methods: Google Colab (free tier v5e-1), VS Code Remote SSH with Jupyter extension, and local JupyterLab with port forwarding. Covers environment setup, kernel registration, available example notebooks (SFT and GRPO/GSPO training), and common pitfalls.

nodes.name: convert_checkpoint.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for bidirectional checkpoint conversion between Hugging Face and MaxText (Orbax) formats, covering supported models (Gemma, Llama, Qwen, Mixtral, DeepSeek, GPT-OSS), environment setup, conversion commands, correctness verification via forward-pass logit comparison, and instructions for adding new model support.

nodes.name: emergency_checkpointing.md
nodes.summary: Guide for configuring emergency checkpointing in MaxText, which saves model state frequently to local ramdisk for rapid recovery from hardware failures or preemptions during multi-slice TPU training on GKE. Covers cluster setup with XPK, ramdisk sizing calculations, MaxText configuration flags, and the distinction between reactive autocheckpointing and proactive emergency checkpointing.

nodes.name: gcs_checkpointing.md
nodes.summary: Documents GCS bucket-based checkpointing in MaxText, the standard method that saves model state directly to Google Cloud Storage. Covers checkpoint loading priority (resume, load from path, or initialize from scratch), configuration flags for checkpoint intervals and paths, and underlying Orbax storage format options (OCDBT, Zarr v3).

nodes.name: multi_tier_checkpointing.md
nodes.summary: Documents multi-tier checkpointing in MaxText, which combines RAM, in-cluster peer replication, and GCS persistent storage tiers to optimize checkpoint management for large-scale training. Covers architecture, comparison with GCS-only and emergency checkpointing, GKE cluster setup with XPK, ramdisk sizing, and MaxText configuration including backup intervals.

nodes.name: data_input_grain.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide to the Grain data input pipeline, covering determinism, resilience to preemptions, data shuffling strategies (global and hierarchical), supported data formats (ArrayRecord, Parquet, TFRecord), multi-source blending, Cloud Storage FUSE setup, evaluation configuration, and experimental chip-count scaling for distributed training.

nodes.name: data_input_hf.md
nodes.summary: Guide to using the Hugging Face streaming data pipeline in MaxText, with configuration examples for streaming directly from Hugging Face Hub and from downloaded data in Cloud Storage buckets, plus known limitations around server timeouts and multi-host scaling.

nodes.name: data_input_tfds.md
nodes.summary: Brief guide to setting up the TensorFlow Datasets (TFDS) data pipeline for MaxText, covering C4 dataset download and YAML configuration for training and evaluation with the SentencePiece tokenizer.

nodes.name: data_pipeline_perf.md
nodes.summary: Performance optimization guide for data input pipelines, explaining how to overlap CPU data loading with accelerator computation, diagnose synchronization issues using profiler traces, benchmark pipeline throughput in isolation, and tune Grain worker count to eliminate data loading bottlenecks.

nodes.name: olmo_grain.md
nodes.summary: Guide for the OLMo numpy Grain-based data pipeline, which reads AI2's pre-tokenized flat .npy token streams via gcsfuse, supports stateless deterministic resume, optional n-gram repetition filtering, and provides end-to-end setup from data download through index building to trainer configuration.

nodes.name: features_and_diagnostics.md
nodes.summary: Documents Ahead of Time (AOT) compilation features for both TPU and GPU targets, explaining how to compile, save, and load train steps using train_compile.py to flag OOM issues early and enable fast startup on target hardware.

nodes.name: gcp_workload_observability.md
nodes.summary: Explains how to enable GCP workload observability for MaxText, which sends heartbeat and performance (training step time) metrics to Cloud Monarch for monitoring and alerting by Google Cloud on-call teams.

nodes.name: megascale_hang_playbook.md
nodes.summary: Troubleshooting playbook for diagnosing Megascale hang issues in Cloud TPU Multislice environments, covering common causes such as fingerprint mismatches, bad TPU chips, data pipeline stalls, and providing guidance on HLO dump collection for debugging.

nodes.name: ml_workload_diagnostics.md
nodes.summary: Guide for enabling Google Cloud ML Diagnostics on MaxText workloads using the managed-mldiagnostics flag, which creates managed ML runs, uploads profiling traces, and reports training metrics to a unified diagnostics platform.

nodes.name: monitor_goodput.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for measuring and monitoring ML Goodput, Badput breakdown, and Step Time Deviation in MaxText using the ML Goodput Measurement library, covering prerequisites, configuration flags, Tensorboard visualization, and Google Cloud Monitoring integration.

nodes.name: understand_logs_and_metrics.md
nodes.summary: In-depth guide explaining how to interpret MaxText training logs, covering configuration information, environment details, memory analysis, TFLOP estimation, and training performance metrics including TFLOP/s/device, MFU, tokens throughput, loss, and total weights.

nodes.name: use_vertex_ai_tensorboard.md
nodes.summary: Guide for uploading MaxText training logs to Vertex AI Tensorboard, covering both XPK-managed GKE deployments (where setup is automatic) and GCE deployments (where manual configuration of project, region, and instance is required).

nodes.name: xprof_user_guide.md
nodes.summary: Guide for using XProf to profile and analyze JAX model training performance in MaxText, covering programmatic profiling mode with JAX profiler APIs and configuration parameters like profiler backend selection, profiler_steps, and warmup step skipping.

nodes.name: benchmark_and_performance.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for setting up benchmarks and tuning MaxText training performance, covering synthetic data setup, arithmetic intensity analysis, rematerialization policies, low-precision training with QWIX, sharding strategy selection, Pallas kernel tuning, communication overlap techniques, and scoped VMEM tuning.

nodes.name: custom_mesh_and_rule.md
nodes.summary: Documents how to use MaxText's custom_mesh_and_rule configuration to override default device mesh and logical axis rules for advanced sharding, including pre-defined configurations (pure-fsdp, ep-as-cp, cp-as-ep, pipeline-large-moe) and the sharding dump regression-testing mechanism.

nodes.name: custom_model.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for customizing LLM model configurations on Cloud TPUs (v5p, Trillium, Ironwood) to maximize MFU, covering initial config identification, TPU best practices for MXU utilization, roofline analysis for choosing FSDP/EP/TP/PP sharding strategies, and real-world examples of 900B dense and 700B/10T MoE models achieving 26-50% MFU.

nodes.name: pallas_kernels_performance.md
nodes.summary: Guide for writing and optimizing custom Pallas kernels in MaxText, explaining when custom kernels outperform XLA defaults (MoE ragged activations, attention bandwidth bottlenecks), the profile-tune-repeat workflow, TPU memory hierarchy (HBM/VMEM/SMEM), core design patterns (tiling, pipelining, online accumulation), and code examples for elementwise and blocked operations.

nodes.name: sharding.md
nodes.summary: In-depth reference on sharding strategies supported in MaxText including Data Parallelism (DP), Fully Sharded Data Parallelism (FSDP), Context Parallelism (CP), Tensor Parallelism (TP), Expert Parallelism (EP), and Pipeline Parallelism (PP/gPipe/circular), with formal arithmetic intensity derivations for each strategy, hierarchical mesh configuration, and MaxText's three-layer code implementation (physical axes, logical axes, per-tensor constraints).
nodes.languageNotes: Uses mathematical notation (LaTeX-style) extensively for arithmetic intensity derivations, with tensor dimension subscripts denoting sharding axes.

nodes.name: api.rst
nodes.summary: API reference landing page for the MaxText library, using Sphinx autodoc to generate complete API documentation from source code docstrings.

nodes.name: architecture.md
nodes.summary: Architecture documentation index page providing navigation cards to the MaxText design overview and the JAX/AI libraries deep-dive pages.

nodes.name: core_concepts.md
nodes.summary: Core concepts index page linking to detailed documentation on checkpoints, alternatives to MaxText, quantization, tiling strategies, JAX/XLA/Pallas integration, and Mixture of Experts configuration.

nodes.name: models.md
nodes.summary: Models documentation index page providing navigation to model tiering (Gold/Silver tiers for TPU generations) and the complete list of supported models and architectures.

nodes.name: performance_metrics.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference on performance metrics in MaxText, primarily covering Model Flops Utilization (MFU) with mathematical definitions, causal attention FLOP accounting, comparison with hardware FLOP measurements, and guidance on interpreting step time, tokens/s, and MFU for training time estimation.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains LaTeX math equations rendered via MathJax/KaTeX for MFU formulas and training time calculations. References specific PRs that corrected causal attention and sliding window FLOP accounting.

nodes.name: alternatives.md
nodes.summary: Compares MaxText to alternative LLM frameworks, specifically Nvidia Megatron-LM and MinGPT/NanoGPT, highlighting MaxText's pure Python/JAX approach, superior MFU, and massive scalability advantages.

nodes.name: batch_size.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reference for batch size concepts in MaxText, covering per-device batch size, global batch size computation, gradient accumulation steps, pipeline microbatches, batch size ramp-up for training stability, and RL-specific batch parameters.

nodes.name: checkpoints.md
nodes.summary: Explains MaxText's checkpoint format taxonomy along two axes (training state vs. inference, stacked vs. unstacked weights), describes how JAX's scan function drives the stacked format, and documents configuration flags for saving checkpoints during training including emergency checkpointing.

nodes.name: jax_xla_and_pallas.md
nodes.summary: In-depth guide to the foundational technology stack underlying MaxText: JAX (jit, grad, SPMD transformations), Pallas (custom hardware kernels for MoE and fused attention), XLA (operator fusion and compute/communication overlap), and Mosaic (Pallas compiler). Explains how these layers compose to deliver high performance from pure Python code.

nodes.name: moe_configuration.md
nodes.summary: Detailed reference for Mixture of Experts configuration in MaxText, covering architecture choices (dropless vs. dropping strategies, Tokamax/Megablox/JAX ragged dot), routing mechanisms (random routing, grouped routing, load balancing), MLP computation options (sparse vs. dense matmul), sharding strategies (ring-of-experts, FSDP), and Pallas kernel tile-size tuning parameters.

nodes.name: quantization.md
nodes.summary: Explains quantization concepts and usage in MaxText, covering the Qwix library (recommended) and deprecated AQT for quantized training. Documents configuration flags, the Qwix Interception API for non-intrusive layer replacement, supported quantization types (int8, fp8), and includes a DeepSeek V3 FP8 fine-tuning recipe with convergence validation methodology.

nodes.name: tiling.md
nodes.summary: Explains the tiling optimization technique in MaxText for reducing peak memory usage by processing tensors in smaller chunks. Covers gradient accumulation as batch-level tiling, vocabulary tiling to avoid materializing full logits tensors, and hierarchical tiling across the memory hierarchy (HBM, VMEM, VREGs). Distinguishes tiling from sharding.

nodes.name: decoupled_mode.md
nodes.summary: Explains decoupled mode (DECOUPLE_GCLOUD=TRUE) for running MaxText tests and local development without Google Cloud dependencies, covering the centralized gcloud_stub API, minimal local datasets, and guidelines for maintaining cloud-free test hygiene.

nodes.name: run_maxtext_localhost.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive getting-started guide for running MaxText on a single localhost or single-host VM, covering GCS bucket setup, configuration via base.yml, test training with synthetic data, decoding, and running pre-configured model architectures (llama3-8b, qwen3-4b, mixtral-8x7b) on both TPUs and GPUs.

nodes.name: run_maxtext_single_host_gpu.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for running MaxText on a single-host GPU VM (A3-high / 8x H100), covering VM creation, CUDA installation, Docker setup, NVIDIA Container Toolkit configuration, and training 1B and LLama2-7B models with XLA optimization flags.

nodes.name: run_maxtext_via_multihost_job.md
nodes.summary: Documents the multihost_job.py workflow for production-grade multi-slice TPU training jobs with hardware resiliency, covering code upload to GCS, TPU provisioning via CQR, automatic rescheduling on hardware interruption, and log management.

nodes.name: run_maxtext_via_multihost_runner.md
nodes.summary: Documents the multihost_runner.py workflow for quick iterative experiments on multiple TPU hosts or slices, covering SSH-based code distribution, queued resource provisioning, dependency installation, and cleanup procedures.

nodes.name: run_maxtext_via_pathways.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for running MaxText on GKE using Pathways orchestration, covering both batch (non-interactive) and headless (interactive/debugging) workload modes, environment configuration, port forwarding setup, and troubleshooting common issues.

nodes.name: run_maxtext_via_xpk.md
nodes.summary: Recommended workflow guide for running MaxText at scale on GKE using the Accelerated Processing Kit (XPK), covering prerequisites, environment setup, Docker image building, workload submission for both TPU and GPU clusters, Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation for faster startup, and job monitoring and management.

nodes.name: first_run.md
nodes.summary: Getting-started tutorial that walks users through running MaxText on single-host and multihost environments using Cloud TPUs or NVIDIA GPUs, covering prerequisites like storage setup, training on synthetic data, decoding, notebook usage, and scaling to multihost via GKE/XPK.

nodes.name: inference.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for running inference with MaxText models via vLLM, covering installation of the MaxText vLLM adapter plugin, offline inference with the vllm_decode script, online inference with vllm serve, and using MaxText models as samplers in reinforcement learning workflows like GRPO.

nodes.name: post_training_index.md
nodes.summary: Index page for MaxText post-training workflows, explaining the post-training stack (MaxText models, Tunix algorithms, vLLM on TPU, Pathways), listing supported techniques (SFT, LoRA, DPO/ORPO, multimodal SFT, RL) with links to individual tutorials, and describing the role of Pathways for efficient RL orchestration.

nodes.name: pretraining.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive pre-training tutorial demonstrating how to train models on real datasets using MaxText's three supported data input pipelines: HuggingFace (streaming from Hub), Grain (ArrayRecord/Parquet via GCS Fuse), and TFDS (TFRecord from Cloud Storage), with detailed dataset, tokenizer, and evaluation configuration for each.

nodes.name: dpo.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for preference optimization (DPO and ORPO) on single-host TPUs, covering data requirements for triplet-format preference data, checkpoint preparation, and training configuration with both reference-model and reference-free alignment methods.

nodes.name: full_finetuning.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step tutorial for full fine-tuning (FFT) on single-host TPUs, covering environment setup, HuggingFace checkpoint conversion, C4 dataset preparation, and training parameter configuration for high MFU.

nodes.name: gepa_optimization.md
nodes.summary: Explains GEPA (Generic Evaluation and Prompt Adaptation), an evolutionary framework for optimizing system prompts for MaxText models, including the evolutionary loop, prompt merging for synergetic gains, and robust math evaluation with MathAdapter.

nodes.name: knowledge_distillation.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tutorial covering both offline distillation (teacher dataset generation via vLLM followed by SFT) and online distillation (logit matching with frozen teacher), with examples using Qwen3-32B teacher and Llama-3.1-8B student models on single-host and multi-host TPU setups.

nodes.name: lora.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) fine-tuning on single-host TPUs using Tunix, covering trainable layer customization, checkpoint conversion, fresh training on HuggingFace datasets, resuming from LoRA checkpoints, and converting back to HuggingFace format.

nodes.name: multimodal.md
nodes.summary: Guide to multimodal functionalities in MaxText covering checkpoint conversion, text+image inference decode, and supervised fine-tuning with visual-question-answering datasets, supporting Gemma3 and Llama4 model families.

nodes.name: rl.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for reinforcement learning on single-host TPUs using GRPO and GSPO algorithms via the Tunix library, training Llama3.1-8B-IT on the GSM8K math reasoning dataset with vLLM for inference.

nodes.name: rl_on_multi_host.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for reinforcement learning on multi-host TPUs using Pathways orchestration, covering Docker image building, GRPO/GSPO workload submission on GKE clusters for training Llama3.1-70B-IT on GSM8K.

nodes.name: rl_qwen3_30b.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for reinforcement learning with Qwen3-30b-a3b-base on Ironwood GKE clusters with tpu7x-128 nodes, covering Docker setup, checkpoint conversion, XPK workload submission on the OpenMathInstruct-2 dataset.

nodes.name: sft.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on single-host TPUs using the Tunix library, covering installation, environment setup, checkpoint conversion, and training on HuggingFace datasets.

nodes.name: sft_on_multi_host.md
nodes.summary: Tutorial for supervised fine-tuning on multi-host TPUs (v6e-256), covering GKE cluster setup, Docker image building, checkpoint conversion, and workload submission via both Multi-Controller JAX (McJAX) and Pathways.

nodes.name: run_olmo3_7b_grain_resume_test.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end resume test for the OLMo 3 7B grain pipeline, verifying that checkpoint restore and data iterator resumption work correctly by running two sequential training phases (Run A: 50 steps from scratch, Run B: resume to 75 steps) and checking loss continuity.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail for strict error handling and tee for dual logging to stdout and file.

nodes.name: run_train
nodes.summary: Launches a MaxText pre-training run for OLMo 3 7B with configurable step count, using the olmo_grain dataset type with gcsfuse-mounted .npy data and HuggingFace tokenizer, piping output to a log file.

nodes.name: run_olmo3_7b_grain_smoke.sh
nodes.summary: Smoke test for OLMo 3 7B training with the olmo_grain dataset pipeline, validating that the numpy grain data source correctly wires through the trainer for 50 steps on a reduced 4-layer model without crashes or shape mismatches.
nodes.languageNotes: Validates the data pipeline path rather than full convergence, using a reduced model size (4 layers instead of 32) to fit on small TPU slices.

nodes.name: run_qwen3_30b_hf_to_maxtext.sh
nodes.summary: Converts a Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base model checkpoint from HuggingFace safetensors format to MaxText Orbax format, storing the result in a GCS output directory for subsequent training or inference.

nodes.name: run_qwen3_30b_maxtext_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: Converts a Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base model checkpoint from MaxText Orbax format back to HuggingFace format, enabling interoperability with the broader HuggingFace ecosystem for inference or further fine-tuning.

nodes.name: run_qwen3_30b_rl.sh
nodes.summary: Launches a GRPO reinforcement learning training workload for the Qwen3-30B-A3B model on a GKE Ironwood TPU cluster via XPK with Pathways, configuring extensive XLA flags for sparse-core optimizations and using OpenMathInstruct-2 as the RL dataset.
nodes.languageNotes: Deploys via xpk workload create-pathways with custom Pathways proxy server XLA flags targeting TPU v7 (Ironwood) sparse-core optimizations for MoE workloads.

nodes.name: cuda12_overrides.txt
nodes.summary: CUDA 12 dependency version overrides pinning datasets, fsspec, and gcsfs to specific versions for CUDA 12 GPU environments.

nodes.name: post_train_github_deps.txt
nodes.summary: Post-training GitHub dependency pins referencing specific commits of google-tunix, tpu-inference, and vllm for inference and post-training workflows.

nodes.name: pre_train_github_deps.txt
nodes.summary: Pre-training GitHub dependency pins referencing specific commits of google-jetstream and mlperf-logging for training benchmarking and streaming.

nodes.name: tpu_overrides.txt
nodes.summary: TPU dependency version overrides pinning datasets, fsspec, and gcsfs to specific versions for Google Cloud TPU environments.

nodes.name: tpu_post_train_overrides.txt
nodes.summary: TPU post-training dependency overrides pinning datasets, flax, fsspec, gcsfs, google-metrax, and optax to specific versions for TPU-based post-training workflows.

nodes.name: cuda12-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: CUDA 12 GPU-specific Python dependency file that extends the base requirements with transformer-engine[jax] for GPU-accelerated JAX workloads.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Core Python dependency manifest listing 48 packages required by MaxText, including JAX, Flax, Optax, TensorFlow, and various Google Cloud and ML ecosystem libraries.

nodes.name: runner-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Runner-specific dependencies for orchestrating MaxText workloads, including xpk for GKE-based TPU/GPU job management, Kubernetes client, and Google Cloud Filestore support.

nodes.name: tpu-post-train-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Extended dependency manifest for TPU post-training workflows, adding 44 packages on top of base requirements including PyTorch/TorchAX interop, model conversion tools (GGUF, compressed-tensors), serving infrastructure (OpenAI, Ray), and profiling/monitoring utilities.

nodes.name: tpu-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: TPU-specific dependency file that inherits all base requirements without adding additional packages, serving as the minimal TPU runtime dependency specification.

nodes.name: cuda12-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Auto-generated pinned Python dependency list for CUDA 12 GPU environments, produced by the seed-env tool from a host requirements file. Includes JAX, Flax, Orbax, and GPU-specific packages.

nodes.name: runner-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Auto-generated pinned Python dependency list for the CI/CD runner environment, produced by seed-env. Contains a lighter subset of dependencies needed for orchestrating builds, tests, and infrastructure tooling.

nodes.name: tpu-post-train-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Auto-generated pinned Python dependency list for TPU post-training workflows, produced by the seed-env tool. Includes the largest superset of packages, adding post-training libraries like accelerate, anthropic, and HuggingFace transformers on top of the base TPU dependencies.

nodes.name: tpu-requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Auto-generated pinned Python dependency list for Google Cloud TPU training environments, produced by the seed-env tool. Covers core JAX/Flax/Orbax ecosystem packages and TPU-specific runtime dependencies.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Primary Python dependency manifest for MaxText, listing core packages including JAX, Flax, Optax, TensorFlow, and various Google Cloud and ML tooling dependencies, plus pinned Git-based installs for JetStream and MLPerf logging.

nodes.name: requirements_decoupled_jax_0_7.1.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned dependency manifest targeting JAX 0.7.1 with strict minimum version constraints for all packages, used for reproducible builds decoupled from the default JAX version. Includes additional packages like chex, grpcio, matplotlib, pandas, and nltk not in the base requirements.

nodes.name: requirements_decoupled_rocm_jax_0_9_1.txt
nodes.summary: Dependency manifest targeting JAX 0.9.1 with the ROCm GPU plugin (jax-rocm7-plugin) for AMD GPU support, including additional packages like numba and math-verify not present in the default requirements.
nodes.languageNotes: This requirements file pins jax-rocm7-plugin for AMD ROCm GPU acceleration, enabling MaxText to run on AMD hardware in addition to NVIDIA/TPU.

nodes.name: requirements_docs.txt
nodes.summary: Documentation build dependencies specifying Sphinx and its extensions (myst-nb, myst-parser, sphinx-book-theme, sphinx-design, sphinx-copybutton) along with a reference to base requirements for import documentation generation.

nodes.name: docker_build_dependency_image.sh
nodes.summary: Builds the MaxText Docker dependency image for either TPU or GPU targets, supporting stable, nightly, and pinned modes with configurable JAX versions and base images.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Docker BuildKit for pip cache optimization and constructs docker build args dynamically from command-line key=value pairs.

nodes.name: run_docker_build
nodes.summary: Executes a docker build command with the given Dockerfile path and build arguments, using host networking.

nodes.name: build_gpu_image
nodes.summary: Builds the MaxText Docker image for GPU using the GPU-specific Dockerfile, optionally setting a pinned NVIDIA JAX base image.

nodes.name: build_tpu_image
nodes.summary: Builds the MaxText Docker image for TPU using the TPU-specific Dockerfile, with optional custom libtpu version and MantaRay base image support.

nodes.name: docker_upload_runner.sh
nodes.summary: Packages local MaxText source code into a runner Docker image on top of the prebuilt dependency image, then tags and pushes it to GCR or Artifact Registry for cloud deployment.
nodes.languageNotes: Performs pre-build validation by checking for dangling and absolute symbolic links using find, and respects .dockerignore exclusion patterns to avoid Docker context errors.

nodes.name: generate_requirements.sh
nodes.summary: Generates pinned Python requirements files using the seed-env tool and uv package manager, with support for dependency overrides incompatible with the JAX seed lock.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses uv package manager for dependency resolution and lock file export, converting exact version pins (==) to minimum version constraints (>=) for flexibility.

nodes.name: preflight.sh
nodes.summary: Pre-flight script that applies network optimizations by invoking rto_setup.sh, using sudo when available for privileged operations.

nodes.name: rto_setup.sh
nodes.summary: Configures TCP/IP network parameters for optimal performance on TPU/GPU clusters, including RTO minimum, Cubic hystart, SYN backlog tuning, and zero-copy offload.
nodes.languageNotes: Tunes low-level Linux kernel TCP settings via sysctl and /proc/sys writes to reduce latency and improve throughput for distributed training workloads.

nodes.name: setup.sh
nodes.summary: Main environment setup script that installs MaxText dependencies for TPU or GPU in stable, nightly, or libtpu-only modes, handling Python version checks, JAX installation, and optional custom libtpu from GCS.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports interactive Python version upgrade via uv venv creation, and uses uv pip for all package installations with lowest-resolution strategy for reproducibility.

nodes.name: unset_optional_vars
nodes.summary: Unsets optional environment variables (JAX_VERSION, LIBTPU_VERSION, LIBTPU_GCS_PATH) when their value is the sentinel string 'NONE'.

nodes.name: install_custom_libtpu
nodes.summary: Downloads and installs a custom libtpu.so binary from a user-specified GCS path, replacing any existing local copy.

nodes.name: install_maxtext_package_without_deps
nodes.summary: Installs the MaxText package in editable mode without its dependencies, enabling Docker layer caching by separating package installation from dependency installation.

nodes.name: install_maxtext_with_deps
nodes.summary: Installs MaxText with all pre-training dependencies from the appropriate requirements file (TPU or GPU), then installs the MaxText package without deps.

nodes.name: install_post_training_deps
nodes.summary: Installs MaxText post-training workflow dependencies from the TPU post-training requirements file, restricted to TPU devices only.

nodes.name: setup_gcsfuse.sh
nodes.summary: Mounts a Google Cloud Storage bucket via gcsfuse in read-only mode with optimized caching settings, and optionally prefills the metadata cache by listing the dataset directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Configures gcsfuse with aggressive metadata caching (unlimited TTL and size) and kernel list cache for optimal read performance on large training datasets.

nodes.name: convert_gemma2_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: Example shell script that converts a Gemma2-2B MaxText checkpoint to HuggingFace format, then verifies the conversion by comparing logits via the forward_pass_logit_checker utility.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses nested Bash parameter expansion fallbacks (MAXTEXT_ASSETS_ROOT / MAXTEXT_PKG_DIR / MAXTEXT_REPO_ROOT) for portable path resolution across installed-package and source-checkout environments.

nodes.name: convert_gemma2_to_mt.sh
nodes.summary: Example shell script that converts a HuggingFace Gemma2-2B checkpoint to MaxText format, performs decoding with the converted checkpoint, and verifies correctness by comparing logits against the original HuggingFace model.

nodes.name: convert_gemma3_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: Example shell script that converts a Gemma3-4B MaxText checkpoint to HuggingFace format and verifies the conversion by downloading the output from GCS and running a forward-pass logit comparison.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the high-performance model configuration directory with reproduction instructions for running on TPU/GPU clusters (GCE and GKE platforms) and a comprehensive reference table of XLA compiler flags used by MaxText for optimizing collective operations and compute-communication overlap.

nodes.name: base.yml
nodes.summary: Central base configuration file defining all default hyperparameters for MaxText models, covering model architecture (transformer layers, attention, MoE, MLA, multimodal), training (optimizer, learning rate, data pipeline, checkpointing), parallelism (ICI/DCN sharding across data/tensor/pipeline/expert/sequence/context dimensions), quantization, inference, profiling, and monitoring options. All other config files inherit from and override this base.
nodes.languageNotes: With 600+ top-level YAML keys spanning 1257 lines, this is an unusually large flat configuration file. It serves as the single source of truth for all tunable parameters in the MaxText framework, using YAML anchors and comments as informal documentation for each parameter group.

nodes.name: decoupled_base_test.yml
nodes.summary: Test-specific configuration that inherits from base.yml but disables all cloud-coupled features (GCS checkpointing, Vertex TensorBoard, goodput metrics) and sets minimal batch/step sizes to enable fast local testing without requiring Google Cloud infrastructure.

nodes.name: 2d-fsdp.yml
nodes.summary: Custom mesh and sharding rule that introduces a secondary FSDP axis (fsdp_transpose) alongside the primary fsdp axis, enabling efficient training across O(1000) chips by preventing sharding limits and improving GMM efficiency for large device counts with limited model dimensions.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines logical_axis_rules mapping tensor dimensions to mesh axes across vocabulary embedding, attention, MoE, and dense layers with dual FSDP axes for 2D fully-sharded data parallelism.

nodes.name: cp-as-ep.yml
nodes.summary: Custom mesh and sharding rule where the context parallelism axis acts as expert parallelism within the core MoE part (between EP all-to-all communications), using data, stage, fsdp, context, and expert mesh axes.
nodes.languageNotes: The context axis is repurposed for expert parallelism in the MoE activations and weights (exp shards across both context and expert), while retaining its role as context_sharding for sequence-level parallelism.

nodes.name: ep-as-cp.yml
nodes.summary: Custom mesh and sharding rule where the expert parallelism axis acts as context parallelism for all components except the core dMoE part, using data, stage, fsdp, and expert mesh axes with context_sharding set to 'expert'.
nodes.languageNotes: Inverse of cp-as-ep: the expert axis provides context parallelism (attention length shards across expert), while the MoE expert dimension remains on the expert axis alone.

nodes.name: pipeline-large-moe.yml
nodes.summary: Custom mesh and sharding rule optimized for pipeline parallelism in large-scale Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) training, incorporating tensor parallelism via a tensor axis for weight prefetch memory management and limiting sharding strategies when EP x FSDP exceeds 512.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces a tensor axis for weight sharding during pipeline FSDP all-gather operations (pipeline_fsdp_ag_once / pipeline_fsdp_ag_per_repeat). The context axis supports fractional per-device batch sizes rather than sequence parallelism.

nodes.name: pure-fsdp.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal custom mesh and sharding rule using only a single FSDP axis, providing the simplest sharding strategy suitable for small-scale training with all tensor dimensions and activations sharded across the fsdp axis alone.

nodes.name: 1024b.sh
nodes.summary: Experimental training launcher for a 1024-billion parameter scale model, using full rematerialization, tensor parallelism of 16, and per-device batch size of 2 with flash attention on synthetic data.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets LIBTPU_INIT_ARGS with XLA TPU optimization flags for async collective operations and megacore fusion before invoking MaxText pre-training via Python module invocation.

nodes.name: 128b.sh
nodes.summary: Experimental training launcher for a 128-billion parameter scale model, using minimal rematerialization, tensor parallelism of 8, and per-device batch size of 2 with flash attention on synthetic data.

nodes.name: 256b.sh
nodes.summary: Experimental training launcher for a 256-billion parameter scale model, using minimal rematerialization, tensor parallelism of 8, and per-device batch size of 2 with flash attention on synthetic data.

nodes.name: 32b.sh
nodes.summary: Experimental training launcher for a 32-billion parameter scale model, using minimal rematerialization, tensor parallelism of 4, and per-device batch size of 8 with flash attention on synthetic data.

nodes.name: 512b.sh
nodes.summary: Experimental training launcher for a 512-billion parameter scale model, using full rematerialization, tensor parallelism of 16, and per-device batch size of 2 with flash attention on synthetic data.

nodes.name: 64b.sh
nodes.summary: Experimental training launcher for a 64-billion parameter scale model, using minimal rematerialization, tensor parallelism of 4, and per-device batch size of 4 with flash attention on synthetic data.

nodes.name: 16vm.sh
nodes.summary: Launch script for Llama 2 7B pre-training on 16 A3 GPU nodes (128 H100 GPUs) with tuned XLA flags for multi-node all-reduce, all-gather, and reduce-scatter thresholds, using DCN data parallelism of 16 and ICI FSDP parallelism of 8.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets XLA_FLAGS environment variable with GPU-specific compiler optimizations for latency hiding, pipelined collectives, and double buffering.

nodes.name: 1vm.sh
nodes.summary: Launch script for Llama 2 7B pre-training on a single A3 GPU node (8 H100 GPUs) with tuned XLA flags, using DCN data parallelism of 1 and ICI FSDP parallelism of 8.

nodes.name: 2vm.sh
nodes.summary: Launch script for Llama 2 7B pre-training on 2 A3 GPU nodes (16 H100 GPUs) with tuned XLA flags for multi-node collectives, using DCN data parallelism of 2 and ICI FSDP parallelism of 8.

nodes.name: 4vm.sh
nodes.summary: Launch script for Llama 2 7B pre-training on 4 A3 GPU nodes (32 H100 GPUs) with tuned XLA flags for multi-node collectives, using DCN data parallelism of 4 and ICI FSDP parallelism of 8.

nodes.name: 8vm.sh
nodes.summary: Launch script for Llama 2 7B pre-training on 8 A3 GPU nodes (64 H100 GPUs) with tuned XLA flags for multi-node collectives, using DCN data parallelism of 8 and ICI FSDP parallelism of 8.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents expected performance results (TFLOP/sec/chip) for Llama 2 7B pre-training across 1, 2, 4, 8, and 16 A3 (H100-80GB) GPU node configurations.

nodes.name: llama2_70b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for the LLaMA 2 70B model, using cuDNN flash attention with Transformer Engine, full rematerialization, synthetic data, and a per-device batch size of 6 with 4096 sequence length.

nodes.name: llama2_7b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for the LLaMA 2 7B model, using cuDNN flash attention with Transformer Engine, minimal rematerialization with context, synthetic data, and a per-device batch size of 4 with 4096 sequence length.

nodes.name: llama3.1_405b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for the LLaMA 3.1 405B model, the largest LLaMA variant, using full rematerialization with scan layers, synthetic data, and a minimal per-device batch size of 1.0 with 4096 sequence length.

nodes.name: llama3_70b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for the LLaMA 3 70B model, using cuDNN flash attention with Transformer Engine, full rematerialization, synthetic data, batch reuse, and a per-device batch size of 4 with 8192 sequence length.

nodes.name: llama3_8b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for the LLaMA 3 8B model, using cuDNN flash attention with Transformer Engine, minimal rematerialization with context, synthetic data, batch reuse, and a per-device batch size of 12 with 8192 sequence length.

nodes.name: mixtral_8x1b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for a scaled-down Mixtral 8x1B variant (using mixtral-8x7b model params with 5 decoder layers), with cuDNN flash attention, full rematerialization, megablox and sparse matmul disabled, and synthetic data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses base_num_decoder_layers override to create a smaller variant of the mixtral-8x7b architecture for testing or benchmarking purposes.

nodes.name: mixtral_8x2b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for a scaled-down Mixtral 8x2B variant (using mixtral-8x7b model params with 10 decoder layers), with cuDNN flash attention, full rematerialization, megablox and sparse matmul disabled, and synthetic data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses base_num_decoder_layers override to create a medium variant of the mixtral-8x7b architecture for testing or benchmarking purposes.

nodes.name: mixtral_8x7b.yml
nodes.summary: GPU training configuration for the full Mixtral 8x7B mixture-of-experts model, using cuDNN flash attention, minimal rematerialization, a custom Mistral tokenizer, nsys profiler, megablox disabled, and capacity factor of 1.0.
nodes.languageNotes: Unlike the scaled-down variants, this full config specifies a custom Mistral v1 tokenizer path and enables nsys profiling for GPU performance analysis.

nodes.name: inference.yml
nodes.summary: Inference-specific sharding configuration that defines logical axis rules mapping activation, weight, and KV cache dimensions to mesh axes for efficient tensor parallelism and data sharding during model inference.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a comprehensive set of logical axis rules to control how tensors are partitioned across TPU/GPU mesh dimensions during inference, including special rules for KV caches, paged attention, and mixture-of-experts activations.

nodes.name: inference_jetstream.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal JetStream inference configuration that extends the base config with JetStream-specific settings for JAX profiling and model warmup control.

nodes.name: vllm.yml
nodes.summary: vLLM integration configuration that sets up MaxText for use as a vLLM backend, enabling NNX mode, disabling scanned layers, and defining a custom mesh axis layout with attention-data-parallelism and expert-parallelism sharding rules optimized for vLLM's inference pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a distinct 5-axis mesh layout (data, attn_dp, model, expert, attn_dp_expert) compared to the standard inference config, reflecting vLLM's different parallelism strategy. Also enables NNX (the newer Flax module system) and skips JAX distributed initialization since vLLM manages it.

nodes.name: llama2_70b_v5e-16.yml
nodes.summary: JetStream inference configuration for LLaMA 2 70B on a 16-chip TPU v5e slice using interleaved multihost sharding with tensor=8 and autoregressive=2 parallelism, bf16 weights and KV cache.

nodes.name: llama3_405b_v5e-64.yml
nodes.summary: JetStream inference configuration for LLaMA 3.1 405B on a 64-chip TPU v5e slice using interleaved multihost sharding with tensor=8 and autoregressive=8 parallelism, specifying a tiktoken tokenizer.

nodes.name: llama3_70b_v5e-16.yml
nodes.summary: JetStream inference configuration for LLaMA 3 70B on a 16-chip TPU v5e slice using interleaved multihost sharding with tensor=8 and autoregressive=2 parallelism, using tiktoken tokenizer and bf16 precision.

nodes.name: deepseek-custom.yml
nodes.summary: Small custom model configuration derived from DeepSeek V3.2-671B for testing, with DeepSeek Sparse Attention, Engram memory modules, and multi-head compression (mHC) enabled.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes advanced DeepSeek-specific features: Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) with LoRA-based Q/KV compression, engram layers for learned memory, indexer-based sparse attention, and Sinkhorn-based routing.

nodes.name: deepseek2-16b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for DeepSeek V2-Lite 16B, a smaller MoE model with Multi-head Latent Attention and YaRN-based rotary position embedding.

nodes.name: deepseek2-236b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for DeepSeek V2 236B MoE model with Multi-head Latent Attention. Note: not fully supported at this time.

nodes.name: deepseek3-671b-batchsplit.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for DeepSeek V3 671B using batch split schedule with custom mesh axes and sharding rules for distributed training, including device-limited routing groups.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes explicit logical axis rules and mesh configuration for batch-split parallelism, a more advanced sharding strategy than the standard DeepSeek V3 config.

nodes.name: deepseek3-671b.yml
nodes.summary: Standard model configuration for DeepSeek V3 671B, a large MoE model with 256 experts, Multi-head Latent Attention, and YaRN rotary position embedding.

nodes.name: deepseek3-test.yml
nodes.summary: Small-scale DeepSeek V3 model configuration for testing purposes, with reduced dimensions to enable quick validation runs.

nodes.name: deepseek3-tiny.yml
nodes.summary: Tiny DeepSeek V3 model configuration for lightweight testing, with minimal dimensions and expert counts.

nodes.name: deepseek3.2-671b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for DeepSeek V3.2 671B, identical to the V3 config but adding indexer-based sparse attention parameters for efficient context retrieval.

nodes.name: gemma-2b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 2B dense decoder with 18 layers, 2048 embedding dimension, GQA (1 KV head), and GELU activations.

nodes.name: gemma-7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 7B dense decoder with 28 layers, 3072 embedding dimension, and multi-query attention.

nodes.name: gemma2-27b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 2 27B dense decoder with sliding window attention, logit soft capping, and post-attention/FFW normalization.

nodes.name: gemma2-2b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 2 2B dense decoder with sliding window attention, logit soft capping, and post-layer normalization.

nodes.name: gemma2-9b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 2 9B dense decoder with sliding window attention, logit soft capping, and post-layer normalization.

nodes.name: gemma3-12b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 3 12B multimodal decoder with sliding window attention, local/global RoPE timescales, and Vision Transformer (ViT) parameters for image input.

nodes.name: gemma3-27b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 3 27B multimodal decoder with sliding window attention, local/global RoPE timescales, and ViT parameters for image input.

nodes.name: gemma3-4b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 3 4B multimodal decoder with sliding window attention, local/global RoPE timescales, and ViT parameters for image input.

nodes.name: gemma4-26b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 4 26B MoE multimodal decoder with shared/routed experts, sliding window attention, shared KV projections, V-norm scaling, and ViT parameters for vision input.
nodes.languageNotes: First Gemma generation to combine MoE routing with multimodal ViT, introducing global/local head dimensions and RoPE proportions for heterogeneous attention layers.

nodes.name: gemma4-31b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 4 31B dense multimodal decoder with shared KV projections, V-norm scaling, global/local RoPE proportions, and ViT parameters for vision input.

nodes.name: gemma4-e2b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 4 E2B multimodal text+image decoder with per-layer hidden size input, KV-shared layers, double-wide MLP, and ViT parameters for vision processing.

nodes.name: gemma4-e4b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Gemma 4 E4B multimodal text+image decoder with per-layer hidden size input, KV-shared layers, double-wide MLP, and ViT parameters for vision processing.

nodes.name: gpt-oss-120b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for GPT-OSS 120B, a Mixture-of-Experts model with 128 experts, sliding window attention, YaRN RoPE, and sigmoid-linear MLP activations using the gpt_oss decoder block.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines MoE-specific parameters (num_experts=128, num_experts_per_tok=4) alongside attention sink and sliding window features unique to the GPT-OSS architecture.

nodes.name: gpt-oss-20b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for GPT-OSS 20B, a smaller Mixture-of-Experts variant with 32 experts sharing the same gpt_oss decoder block, sliding window attention, and YaRN RoPE as the 120B model.

nodes.name: gpt3-175b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for GPT-3 175B with 96 decoder layers, GELU activation, fused QKV, trainable position embeddings, and Adam optimizer settings including dataset paths for C4 training.

nodes.name: gpt3-22b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for GPT-3 22B with 48 decoder layers, 256 head dimension, GELU activation, fused QKV, and Adam optimizer hyperparameters using the gpt3 decoder block.

nodes.name: gpt3-52k.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal GPT-3 test configuration (~52K parameters) with 1 decoder layer and tiny dimensions, designed for testing purposes only, using dot_product attention due to small head_dim.

nodes.name: gpt3-6b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for GPT-3 6B with 48 decoder layers, 256 head dimension, GELU activation, fused QKV, and Adam optimizer hyperparameters using the gpt3 decoder block.

nodes.name: kimi-k2-1t.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Kimi-K2 1T-parameter MoE model with 384 experts, Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA), shared experts, YaRN RoPE, and the deepseek decoder block architecture.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) with separate Q/KV LoRA ranks, nope/rope head dimensions, and routed bias with sigmoid scoring, following DeepSeek-style architecture.

nodes.name: llama2-13b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 2 13B with 40 decoder layers, SiLU activation, grouped-query attention (40 KV heads), and FSDP norm sharding rules using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama2-70b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 2 70B with 80 decoder layers, SiLU activation, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads vs 64 query heads), and FSDP norm sharding using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama2-7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 2 7B with 32 decoder layers, SiLU activation, multi-head attention (32 KV heads), and FSDP norm sharding using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3-405b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3 405B with 126 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, and the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3-70b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3 70B with 80 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, and the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3-8b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3 8B with 32 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, and expanded vocabulary (128K) using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3.1-405b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3.1 405B with 126 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3.1-70b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3.1 70B with 80 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3.1-8b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3.1 8B with 32 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama3.3-70b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 3.3 70B with 80 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), extended RoPE timescale of 500K, using the llama2 decoder block.

nodes.name: llama4-17b-128e.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 4 17B with 128 MoE experts, interleaved dense/MoE layers, NoPE/RoPE alternation, chunk attention, temperature tuning, and multimodal ViT settings using the llama4 decoder block.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces NoPE layer intervals (every 4th layer skips RoPE), chunk attention windows, and integrated Vision Transformer parameters for multimodal capabilities.

nodes.name: llama4-17b-16e.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for LLaMA 4 17B with 16 MoE experts, every layer being MoE, QK normalization, NoPE/RoPE alternation, chunk attention, and multimodal ViT settings using the llama4 decoder block.
nodes.languageNotes: Differs from the 128-expert variant by using all-MoE layers (interleave_moe_layer_step=1), QK normalization, and fewer experts per token.

nodes.name: mistral-7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Mistral 7B with 32 decoder layers, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), SiLU activation, and extended RoPE timescale of 1M using the mistral decoder block.

nodes.name: mixtral-8x22b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Mixtral 8x22B, a sparse Mixture-of-Experts model with 8 experts (2 active per token), 56 decoder layers, and 6144 embedding dimension using the mixtral decoder block.

nodes.name: mixtral-8x7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Mixtral 8x7B, a sparse Mixture-of-Experts model with 8 experts (2 active per token), 32 decoder layers, and 4096 embedding dimension using the mixtral decoder block.

nodes.name: olmo3-32b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for AllenAI OLMo 3 32B with YaRN RoPE scaling, sliding window attention (4096 window cycling every 4 layers), QK normalization, and 64 decoder layers at 5120 embedding dimension.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses YaRN (Yet another RoPE extensioN) for long-context position encoding with factor 8.0 scaling from 8192 to 65536 positions.

nodes.name: olmo3-7b-pt.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for AllenAI OLMo 3 7B at pretraining stages 1 and 2, using default RoPE (no YaRN scaling) with 8192 max position embeddings, sliding window attention, and 32 decoder layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinct from olmo3-7b.yml which uses YaRN scaling at 65k for stage-3 and post-trained checkpoints.

nodes.name: olmo3-7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for AllenAI OLMo 3 7B (post-trained/stage-3) with YaRN RoPE scaling, sliding window attention, QK normalization, and 32 decoder layers at 4096 embedding dimension.

nodes.name: qwen2.5-1.5b.yml
