nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 2.5 1.5B Instruct with attention bias enabled, tied word embeddings, qwen2 decoder block, 28 decoder layers, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen2.5-14b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 2.5 14B with attention bias, qwen2 decoder block, 48 decoder layers, 5120 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen2.5-7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 2.5 7B with attention bias, qwen2 decoder block, 28 decoder layers, 3584 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-0.6b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 0.6B, a small dense model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 28 decoder layers, tied word embeddings, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-1.7b-base.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 1.7B base (pre-trained) model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 28 decoder layers at 2048 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-1.7b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 1.7B instruct/fine-tuned model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 28 decoder layers at 2048 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-14b-base.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 14B base model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 48 decoder layers at 5120 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-14b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 14B instruct model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 48 decoder layers at 5120 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-235b-a22b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 235B-A22B, a large Mixture-of-Experts model with 128 experts (8 active per token), 94 decoder layers, load balance loss, and qwen3_moe decoder block.

nodes.name: qwen3-30b-a3b-base.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 30B-A3B base MoE model with 128 experts (8 active per token), 48 decoder layers, normalized top-k probabilities, and qwen3_moe decoder block.

nodes.name: qwen3-30b-a3b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 30B-A3B instruct MoE model with 128 experts (8 active per token), 48 decoder layers, normalized top-k probabilities, and qwen3_moe decoder block.

nodes.name: qwen3-32b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 32B dense model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 64 decoder layers at 5120 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-480b-a35b.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 480B-A35B, the largest Qwen 3 MoE model with 128 experts (8 active per token), 94 decoder layers at 5120 embedding dimension, and qwen3_moe decoder block.

nodes.name: qwen3-4b-base.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 4B base model with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 36 decoder layers at 2560 embedding dimension, and HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-4b-thinking-2507.yml
nodes.summary: Model architecture configuration for Qwen 3 4B Thinking (July 2025 revision), a reasoning-focused variant with QK normalization, qwen3 decoder block, 36 decoder layers, and extended RoPE timescale of 5M.

nodes.name: qwen3-4b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3-4B, a dense 4-billion-parameter decoder with 36 layers, 2560 embedding dim, GQA (32 query / 8 KV heads), SwiGLU activation, QK normalization, and tied word embeddings using the HuggingFace tokenizer.

nodes.name: qwen3-8b-base.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3-8B-Base, a dense 8-billion-parameter base (pretrained, non-instruct) decoder with 36 layers, 4096 embedding dim, GQA (32 query / 8 KV heads), SwiGLU activation, and untied word embeddings.

nodes.name: qwen3-8b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3-8B (instruct variant), a dense 8-billion-parameter decoder with identical architecture to Qwen3-8B-Base (36 layers, 4096 embedding dim, GQA) but targeting the instruct-tuned checkpoint.

nodes.name: qwen3-custom-30b-a3b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for a custom Qwen3-30B-A3B Mixture-of-Experts variant using the 'qwen3_custom_moe' decoder block with 128 experts (8 active per token), 48 layers, compressed attention output (768-dim), and separate MoE expert input dimension.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses attention_output_dim and moe_expert_input_dim to compress the attention output before routing to experts, reducing per-expert computation cost.

nodes.name: qwen3-next-80b-a3b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B, a hybrid architecture using the 'qwen3_next' decoder block with 512 MoE experts (10 active, 1 shared), 48 layers, Gated Delta Net (GDN) linear attention layers interleaved every 4 layers, and partial rotary embeddings (25%).
nodes.languageNotes: Combines softmax attention with Gated Delta Net (GDN) linear attention in an inhomogeneous layer pattern (cycle interval of 4), using partial rotary factor of 0.25 for position encoding.

nodes.name: qwen3-omni-30b-a3b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B, a multimodal MoE model combining a 'qwen3_moe' language decoder (128 experts, 8 active, 48 layers) with a vision encoder (ViT with deepstack), audio encoder (Whisper-style with 32 layers), and multi-dimensional RoPE (MRoPE) for cross-modal position encoding.
nodes.languageNotes: Full multimodal config with vision (deepstack ViT at indexes 8/16/24), audio (Whisper-style encoder with mel spectrogram input and convolutional downsampling), and MRoPE sections [24, 20, 20] for spatial/temporal position encoding.

nodes.name: qwen3.5-35b-a3b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, a hybrid MoE model using the 'qwen3_5' decoder block with 256 experts (8 active, 1 shared), 40 layers, Gated Delta Net linear attention (cycle interval 4), vision encoder (ViT), and MRoPE for multimodal position encoding.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Qwen3.5 architecture combining GDN linear attention with MoE routing and multimodal vision support via ViT. Extended vocabulary (248320 tokens) compared to Qwen3's 151936.

nodes.name: qwen3.5-397b-a17b.yml
nodes.summary: Model configuration for Qwen3.5-397B-A17B, the largest Qwen3.5 variant with 512 MoE experts (10 active, 1 shared), 60 layers, 4096 embedding dim, Gated Delta Net linear attention (cycle interval 4, 64 GDN value heads), vision encoder (ViT), and MRoPE for multimodal support.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest Qwen3.5 model with 397B total / 17B active parameters. Scales up from the 35B-A3B variant with more layers (60 vs 40), larger embedding dim (4096 vs 2048), more experts (512 vs 256), and more GDN value heads (64 vs 32).

nodes.name: distillation-sft.yml
nodes.summary: Extends the base distillation config with SFT (supervised fine-tuning) mode enabled, training only on completion tokens using the UltraChat 200k dataset via HuggingFace with a Llama-style chat template.

nodes.name: distillation.yml
nodes.summary: Base knowledge distillation configuration defining student/teacher model overrides (both Llama 3.1 8B), distillation loss hyperparameters (alpha, temperature), dataset settings using ClimbMix from HuggingFace, and training loop parameters including learning rate schedule and batch sizing.

nodes.name: distillation_gpt_oss_20b.yml
nodes.summary: Production distillation configuration for the GPT-OSS 20B model on v7x TPUs (~17% MFU), using Grain ArrayRecord data loading, FSDP+data parallelism, Tokamax splash attention with fused backward kernels, and AdamW with weight decay masking on embeddings and norms.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses placeholder GCS checkpoint paths that must be replaced with actual student/teacher checkpoint locations.

nodes.name: distillation_qwen3_30b_base.yml
nodes.summary: Production distillation configuration for the Qwen3 30B-A3B base model on v7x TPUs (~20% MFU), with decoder feature loss enabled (distill_beta=1.0) across 8 layer indices, Grain-based data loading, and Tokamax splash attention with HEAD_DIM_MINOR layout for Q and V.

nodes.name: distillation_qwen3_30b_base_pdbs8.yml
nodes.summary: Variant of the Qwen3 30B distillation config with per-device batch size 8 and custom rematerialization with activation offloading to fit larger batches in HBM, achieving ~22% MFU on v7x TPUs.

nodes.name: dpo.yml
nodes.summary: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) post-training configuration using the Anthropic HH-RLHF dataset with Qwen3 0.6B as the default model, configuring DPO-specific parameters like beta, label smoothing, and ORPO lambda alongside chosen/rejected data columns.

nodes.name: lora_module_path.yml
nodes.summary: Registry of recommended LoRA target module paths for each supported model architecture (Llama 3.1, Qwen3, Mistral, DeepSeek2, Gemma2/3, OLMo3, GPT3), specifying regex patterns for self-attention and MLP projection layers with a fallback default pattern.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry is a regex pattern matching decoder layer paths for self-attention projections (Q/K/V/out) and MLP projections (wi_0/wi_1/wo), with model-specific variations for architectures like DeepSeek2's dense+MoE layers.

nodes.name: rl.yml
nodes.summary: Comprehensive reinforcement learning configuration for GRPO/GSPO-token training, defining hardware allocation (trainer/sampler device fractions), RL algorithm parameters (beta, epsilon, loss aggregation, agentic rollout), inference generation settings (temperature, top-k/p, vLLM integration), reward shaping for math verification tasks, and dataset options supporting GSM8K and OpenMathInstruct.
nodes.languageNotes: This config consolidates all RL training parameters across model sizes, including GRPO algorithm hyperparameters, agentic rollout with async vLLM inference, multi-strategy evaluation (pass@K, majority voting), and math verification reward functions.

nodes.name: rl_mt_jt.yml
nodes.summary: MaxText-specific RL configuration override that defines detailed logical axis rules for tensor sharding across data, FSDP, tensor, and expert parallelism dimensions, along with data sharding order and log probability return settings.
nodes.languageNotes: The logical_axis_rules map semantic tensor dimensions (activation_batch, activation_heads, etc.) to physical mesh axes for SPMD partitioning, following JAX/GSPMD conventions.

nodes.name: sft-vision-chartqa.yml
nodes.summary: Multimodal SFT configuration for fine-tuning vision-language models on the ChartQA dataset from HuggingFace, with frozen vision encoder parameters, completion-only training, and query/label data columns.

nodes.name: sft-vision-slidevqa.yml
nodes.summary: Multimodal SFT configuration for fine-tuning vision-language models on the SlideVQA dataset, supporting up to 20 page images per example with frozen vision encoder and completion-only training on question/answer pairs.

nodes.name: sft.yml
nodes.summary: Base supervised fine-tuning (SFT) configuration enabling gradient accumulation, completion-only training, sequence packing, and optional LoRA/QLoRA support with configurable rank and alpha, using the UltraChat 200k dataset from HuggingFace.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the mixed precision quantization configuration format, explaining how regex-based layer keys map to quantization parameters (w_bits, a_bits, w_scale, a_scale, tile_size) and how to enable quantization via base.yml settings.

nodes.name: dense_llm_subchannel.json
nodes.summary: Mixed precision quantization config using subchannel tiling for dense LLMs, applying 8-bit default with 4-bit weight quantization and varying tile sizes (128, 256) on attention and MLP layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses regex patterns as JSON keys to match JAX layer names for per-layer quantization granularity, with tile_size controlling subchannel quantization block dimensions.

nodes.name: dense_llm_weight_only_scale.json
nodes.summary: Mixed precision weight-only quantization config for dense LLMs, applying 8-bit default with 4-bit weights and custom clipping scales (0.8 for query, 0.9 for key) on attention and MLP layers.

nodes.name: int4_weight_only.json
nodes.summary: Simple quantization config applying uniform 4-bit weight-only quantization to all model layers via the __default__ key.

nodes.name: int8_weight_only.json
nodes.summary: Simple quantization config applying uniform 8-bit weight-only quantization to all model layers via the __default__ key.

nodes.name: 22b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 22B parameter model on TPU v4-128 slices, configuring FSDP parallelism, model dimensions, and XLA optimizations before invoking the MaxText pre-training executable.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses shell parameter expansion with nested defaults (${MAXTEXT_CONFIGS_DIR:-${MAXTEXT_REPO_ROOT:-$PWD}/...}) for flexible config path resolution.

nodes.name: 52b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 52B parameter model on TPU v4-384 slices, configuring FSDP parallelism across 192 chips, model architecture dimensions, and XLA optimizations before invoking the MaxText pre-training executable.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents expected performance results (TFLOP/sec/chip and MFU) for 22B and 52B parameter models running on various TPU v4 slice configurations, serving as a benchmark reference.

nodes.name: 128b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 128B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, configuring XLA optimizations, FSDP/tensor parallelism (16x16), flash attention, and rematerialization with qkv_proj_offloaded policy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LIBTPU_INIT_ARGS to set XLA TPU compiler flags for async collective fusion and data-parallel all-reduce optimizations.

nodes.name: 16b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 16B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using full rematerialization policy with per-device batch size 6 and flash attention.

nodes.name: 32b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 32B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using full rematerialization policy with per-device batch size 4 and flash attention.

nodes.name: 64b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 64B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using full rematerialization policy with per-device batch size 2 and flash attention.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Performance benchmarks for high-performance model training on TPU v5e, providing TFLOP/sec/chip and MFU results for 16B, 32B, 64B, and 128B parameter models across 1x to 32x v5e-256 slices.

nodes.name: gpt3_175b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a GPT-3 175B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using FSDP auto-parallelism with 16-way tensor parallelism, flash attention, and full rematerialization.
nodes.languageNotes: Adds xla_tpu_spmd_rng_bit_generator_unsafe flag compared to other v5e scripts, specific to the GPT-3 175B configuration.

nodes.name: llama2_13b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a Llama 2 13B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using qkv_proj_offloaded rematerialization with per-device batch size 8 and a custom Llama 2 tokenizer.

nodes.name: llama2_70b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a Llama 2 70B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using qkv_proj_offloaded rematerialization with per-device batch size 2 and a custom Llama 2 tokenizer.

nodes.name: llama2_70b_v5e-16.yml
nodes.summary: JetStream inference configuration for Llama 2 70B on a v5e-16 slice, defining experimental sharding strategy with detailed logical axis rules for tensor and autoregressive parallelism across attention heads, KV cache, and MLP layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends inference_jetstream.yml base config and uses logical_axis_rules to specify fine-grained tensor sharding across TPU axes.

nodes.name: llama2_7b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a Llama 2 7B parameter model on TPU v5e-256 slices, using save_qkv_proj rematerialization with per-device batch size 4 and a custom Llama 2 tokenizer.

nodes.name: llama3_405b_v5e-64.yml
nodes.summary: JetStream inference configuration for Llama 3.1 405B on a v5e-64 slice, using 8-way tensor and 8-way autoregressive parallelism with the Llama 3 tiktoken tokenizer and experimental sharding strategy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses tiktoken-based tokenizer (tokenizer_llama3.tiktoken) instead of SentencePiece, reflecting the Llama 3 tokenizer architecture change.

nodes.name: llama3_70b_v5e-16.yml
nodes.summary: JetStream inference configuration for Llama 3 70B on a v5e-16 slice, using experimental sharding strategy with tensor and autoregressive parallelism and the Llama 3 tiktoken tokenizer.

nodes.name: 1024b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 1024B parameter model on TPU v5p-2048 or v5p-4096 slices, configuring XLA async collective optimizations, ICI tensor parallelism of 16, and full rematerialization policy.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses LIBTPU_INIT_ARGS environment variable to pass XLA TPU optimization flags that enable async collective fusion and overlap of compute with collectives.

nodes.name: 128b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 128B parameter model on TPU v5p-256 or v5p-512 slices, using minimal rematerialization with tensor parallelism of 8 and per-device batch size of 1.

nodes.name: 256b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 256B parameter model on TPU v5p-1024 slices, using minimal rematerialization with tensor parallelism of 8 and per-device batch size of 1.

nodes.name: 32b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 32B parameter model on TPU v5p-128 slices, using minimal rematerialization with tensor parallelism of 4, per-device batch size of 6, and additional SparseCore collective offload flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes extra XLA flags for SparseCore partitioning and collective offload (xla_sc_disable_megacore_partitioning, xla_tpu_enable_sparse_core_collective_offload_all_gather) not present in the larger model configs.

nodes.name: 512b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 512B parameter model on TPU v5p-1024 slices, using full rematerialization with tensor parallelism of 8 and per-device batch size of 2.

nodes.name: 64b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for a 64B parameter model on TPU v5p-128 slices, using minimal rematerialization with tensor parallelism of 4 and per-device batch size of 3.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents expected performance results (TFLOP/sec/chip and MFU percentages) for 32B through 1024B parameter models running on various TPU v5p slice configurations, serving as a benchmark reference for the accompanying training scripts.

nodes.name: llama2_70b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for the Llama2-70B model on TPU v5p-512, targeting a 4M token global batch size with save_dot_except_mlpwi rematerialization policy, 4096 max sequence length, and xplane profiling enabled.

nodes.name: llama2_7b.sh
nodes.summary: Training launch script for the Llama2-7B model on TPU v5p-512, targeting a 4M token global batch size with minimal rematerialization, 4096 max sequence length, and xplane profiling enabled.

nodes.name: gpt3_175b_base.sh
nodes.summary: Base configuration script for GPT-3 175B training on v5p TPUs, setting XLA optimization flags, LIBTPU tuning parameters, and invoking MaxText training with configurable parallelism dimensions (data, FSDP, tensor), rematerialization policy, flash attention, and int8 quantization. Used by MLPerf 4.0 submissions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses extensive XLA and LIBTPU environment variables for TPU fusion and async collective optimization tuning. Supports both train and train_compile modes via argument-driven branching.

nodes.name: v5p_1024.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that trains GPT-3 175B on a v5p-1024 TPU slice by invoking the base script with per-device batch size 4, full rematerialization, and ICI parallelism of 1x64x8 (data x FSDP x tensor).

nodes.name: v5p_12288.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that trains GPT-3 175B on a v5p-12288 TPU slice (custom 8x16x48 topology) by invoking the base script with per-device batch size 1, minimal rematerialization, and ICI parallelism of 16x48x8.

nodes.name: v5p_2048.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that trains GPT-3 175B on a v5p-2048 TPU slice by invoking the base script with per-device batch size 2, save_dot_except_mlpwi rematerialization, and ICI parallelism of 8x16x8.

nodes.name: v5p_3072.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that trains GPT-3 175B on a v5p-3072 TPU slice by invoking the base script with per-device batch size 2, save_dot_except_mlpwi rematerialization, and ICI parallelism of 12x16x8.

nodes.name: v5p_4096.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that trains GPT-3 175B on a v5p-4096 TPU slice (custom 4x8x64 topology) by invoking the base script with per-device batch size 1, minimal rematerialization, and ICI parallelism of 4x64x8.

nodes.name: v5p_8192.sh
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that trains GPT-3 175B on a v5p-8192 TPU slice (custom 8x16x32 topology) by invoking the base script with per-device batch size 1, minimal rematerialization, and ICI parallelism of 16x32x8.

nodes.name: gemma2_27b.sh
nodes.summary: TPU v6e (Trillium-256) training launch script for the Gemma2 27B model, configuring XLA/TPU compiler flags for VMEM limits, async collectives, and FSDP transpose parallelism with flash attention and 8192 sequence length.
nodes.languageNotes: Shell script that sets LIBTPU_INIT_ARGS with XLA compiler tuning flags specific to the Gemma2 27B model size on Trillium TPUs.

nodes.name: gemma2_9b.sh
nodes.summary: TPU v6e (Trillium-256) training launch script for the Gemma2 9B model, using a larger per-device batch size of 3 compared to the 27B variant, with VMEM limit tuned to 114688 KiB and flash attention at 8192 sequence length.

nodes.name: gemma3_27b.sh
nodes.summary: TPU v6e (Trillium-256) training launch script for the Gemma3 27B model, sharing the same XLA tuning profile as Gemma2 27B (122880 KiB VMEM, FSDP transpose parallelism of 256) with flash attention and 8192 sequence length.

nodes.name: gpt3_175b.sh
nodes.summary: TPU v6e (Trillium-256) training launch script for the GPT-3 175B model, using data-parallel all-reduce optimizations and a shorter 2048 sequence length with VMEM limit of 98304 KiB.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes additional XLA flags for data-parallel optimization (xla_tpu_enable_data_parallel_all_reduce_opt, xla_tpu_data_parallel_opt_different_sized_ops) not present in the Gemma configs.

nodes.name: llama2_7b_4096.sh
nodes.summary: TPU v6e (Trillium-256) training launch script for the LLaMA 2 7B model with 4096 sequence length, using a high per-device batch size of 12 and a simpler set of XLA async collective flags.

nodes.name: mixtral_8x7b.sh
nodes.summary: TPU v6e (Trillium-256) training launch script for the Mixtral 8x7B mixture-of-experts model, using the highest per-device batch size (32) with a compact 1024 sequence length and 81920 KiB VMEM limit.

nodes.name: 01_analysis.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for the first stage of the checkpoint conversion agent pipeline, instructing an LLM to analyze MaxText and Hugging Face parameter sets and produce a comprehensive transformation plan with shared mapping patterns, one-off transformations, and unmapped parameter identification.

nodes.name: 01_plan.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template that takes an analysis output and DSL specification to generate a JSON transformation plan for every mapped parameter group, using layer placeholders for repeated patterns.

nodes.name: 01_plan_check.txt
nodes.summary: Verification prompt that checks whether a generated transformation plan covers every parameter group from the analysis, flagging any uncovered parameters.

nodes.name: 02_transformer_config.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for generating a Hugging Face Transformers configuration object from YAML data, mapping model hyperparameters to the appropriate transformers Config class for a target model.

nodes.name: 03_param_mapping.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for generating the Python parameter mapping function that maps MaxText parameter paths to Hugging Face equivalents, handling scan_layers mode, multimodal configs, and architectural mismatches like local vs global attention layers.

nodes.name: 03_param_mapping_check.txt
nodes.summary: Validation prompt that verifies a parameter mapping script covers all HF parameters, with special handling for weight tying logic where lm_head.weight may intentionally be absent.

nodes.name: 04_dsl.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for extending the transformation DSL to support a new model, performing gap analysis against existing operations and defining new reusable bidirectional ops only when no suitable existing op exists.

nodes.name: 04_hook_fn_dsl.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for generating Python hook functions from DSL specifications, implementing bidirectional transformation logic with closures for config values and scan_layers support for stacked tensor handling.

nodes.name: 04_hook_fn_prompt_chain.txt
nodes.summary: Comprehensive prompt template for generating parameter transformation hook functions directly from analysis and parameter mapping, covering tensor reshaping, embedding padding/slicing, RMSNorm scaling, attention query scaling, and multimodal projections.

nodes.name: 04_pitfalls.txt
nodes.summary: List of common pitfalls to avoid during model checkpoint conversion, including separate Q/K/V kernels, unnecessary transposes, multimodal config structure, embedding scaling, RMSNorm scaling, and vocabulary padding issues.

nodes.name: 05_shape_mapping.txt
nodes.summary: Prompt template for generating the HF weights-to-shape mapping function that maps Hugging Face parameter names to their expected tensor shapes using dynamic config values, serving as a manifest for validating converted checkpoints.

nodes.name: 05_shape_mapping_check.txt
nodes.summary: Validation prompt that checks whether every Hugging Face parameter is covered in a shape mapping code output, reporting uncovered parameters if any exist.

nodes.name: rate_outputs.txt
nodes.summary: Evaluation rubric prompt for scoring a DSL implementation against a ground-truth Python reference, applying correctness scoring (80 points based on per-layer transformation accuracy) and code quality scoring (20 points for readability and abstraction).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) reinforcement learning module, covering key concepts like FSDP/DP/TP sharding strategies, prerequisites for GCP/GKE/Pathways setup, and instructions for running GRPO training on TPU clusters.

nodes.name: grpo.yml
nodes.summary: Primary GRPO training configuration extending base.yml, defining GRPO-specific hyperparameters (beta, epsilon, num_generations), Pathways inference device allocation, decode sampling strategy, and Splash attention block sizes tuned for short sequences.
nodes.languageNotes: YAML config inherits from base.yml via base_config key, a MaxText convention for composable configuration.

nodes.name: grpo_inference.yml
nodes.summary: GRPO inference model configuration extending rl_mt_jt.yml, setting up dot_product attention, weighted decode sampling, and Splash attention block sizes for loading the inference model used during GRPO rollouts.
nodes.languageNotes: Inherits from rl_mt_jt.yml rather than base.yml to include inference-specific sharding rules needed for GRPO sampling.

nodes.name: grpo_trainer_test.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal test configuration for the GRPO trainer extending grpo.yml, using a small default model with synthetic data, short sequence lengths, and deterministic settings for reproducible unit testing.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for running MLPerf offline inference benchmarks on Google Cloud TPUs, covering TPU VM setup, loadgen installation, dataset downloading, checkpoint quantization (LLama2-70b, Mixtral-8x7b, Llama3.1-405b), and benchmark execution for performance, accuracy, and audit scenarios.

nodes.name: llama_offline_run.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that orchestrates MLPerf offline inference benchmarks for LLama2-70b on TPUs, supporting performance, accuracy, and audit run modes with configurable batch sizes, quantization settings, and profiling options.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bash flag parsing with a case/esac block and delegates the actual inference to the Python maxtext.inference.mlperf.offline_mode module.

nodes.name: run_loadgen
nodes.summary: Core function that invokes the maxtext offline_mode Python module with all configured parameters including model args, dataset path, batch sizes, and output logging, then tees the output to a log file.

nodes.name: run_loadgen_accuracy
nodes.summary: Runs the loadgen in accuracy mode and then evaluates results by invoking either the standard or fast accuracy evaluation script against the HuggingFace checkpoint.

nodes.name: mixtral_offline_run.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that orchestrates MLPerf offline inference benchmarks for Mixtral-8x7b MoE model on TPUs, with support for performance, accuracy, and audit modes, custom axis ordering for compute and KV-cache, and dataset column renaming for Mixtral-specific data formats.
nodes.languageNotes: Configures MoE-specific parameters (megablox, sparse_matmul, capacity_factor) and custom axis ordering for the Mixtral architecture.

nodes.name: run_loadgen
nodes.summary: Core function that invokes the offline_mode Python module for Mixtral benchmarks with model-specific parameters including dataset column renaming for Mixtral's different data format.

nodes.name: run_loadgen_accuracy
nodes.summary: Runs the Mixtral loadgen in accuracy mode and then invokes the accuracy evaluation script with tokenizer-based evaluation rather than HuggingFace checkpoint-based evaluation.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Python dependency list for MLPerf inference benchmarks specifying versions of transformers, NLTK, evaluate, rouge-score, sentencepiece, accelerate, and omegaconf needed for accuracy evaluation.

nodes.name: example.proto
nodes.summary: Protobuf schema defining Example and SequenceExample message types for TensorFlow-compatible training data serialization, copied from the TensorFlow project to enable MaxText's input pipeline to read tf.train.Example records.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors TensorFlow's canonical example.proto to maintain wire-format compatibility for reading TFRecord datasets without depending on TensorFlow's proto library.

nodes.name: feature.proto
nodes.summary: Protobuf schema defining the fundamental Feature container types (BytesList, FloatList, Int64List, Feature, Features, FeatureList, FeatureLists) that underlie TensorFlow Example records, enabling MaxText to deserialize TFRecord data without a TensorFlow dependency.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses proto3 oneof for the Feature kind discriminator and packed repeated fields for efficient wire encoding of numeric lists.

nodes.name: generate_proto.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that invokes protoc to compile feature.proto and example.proto into Python modules, enabling the MaxText input pipeline to import the generated protobuf bindings.

nodes.name: Example
nodes.summary: Top-level message wrapping a Features map, representing a single training example as a key-value store of named features.

nodes.name: SequenceExample
nodes.summary: Message representing sequential training data with time-independent context features and time-varying feature lists, used for sequence modeling tasks.

nodes.name: Feature
nodes.summary: Core discriminated union message that holds exactly one of BytesList, FloatList, or Int64List, representing a single typed feature value in TFRecord data.

nodes.name: Features
nodes.summary: Map container from feature name strings to Feature messages, serving as the main key-value store inside an Example.

nodes.name: FeatureList
nodes.summary: Repeated Feature container representing a sequence of feature values over time steps, used within SequenceExample for temporal data.

nodes.name: FeatureLists
nodes.summary: Map container from feature name strings to FeatureList messages, organizing multiple named sequences within a SequenceExample.

nodes.name: gemma_7b.sh
nodes.summary: Scratch script that sets environment variables and launches the Gemma 7B model inference server via maxengine_server with bfloat16 weights and autoregressive parallelism.

nodes.name: run_inference_microbenchmark.sh
nodes.summary: Scratch script that runs inference microbenchmarking for LLaMA 2 7B with int8 quantization, KV cache quantization, and bfloat16 weights on a single device with autoregressive parallelism.

nodes.name: setup_transformer.sh
nodes.summary: Scratch script that installs PyTorch (CPU-only), tokenizers, and HuggingFace transformers libraries as pip dependencies for local development or conversion tasks.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive guide for reproducing AI2's OLMo-3 7B stage-1 pretraining in MaxText, covering XPK multi-host and single-host workflows, data preparation, checkpoint conversion from HuggingFace, resume semantics, and loss-overlay validation against AI2's published WandB curve.

nodes.name: run_olmo3_7b_stage1.sh
nodes.summary: Direct-host launcher for OLMo-3 7B stage-1 pretraining that configures all hyperparameters (LR, warmup, Adam, z-loss, grad-clip), sets Ironwood-tuned XLA/LIBTPU flags for TPU performance, computes per-device batch sizes from the target global batch, optionally mounts GCS via gcsfuse, and invokes maxtext.trainers.pre_train.train with the olmo3-7b-pt model config.
nodes.languageNotes: Env-var driven shell script with extensive inline documentation. Uses bash arithmetic for batch-size validation and Python one-liners for warmup fraction computation. Configures Ironwood-specific XLA flags for sparse-core collective offload achieving 44.5% MFU.

nodes.name: xpk_olmo3_7b_stage1.sh
nodes.summary: XPK wrapper for multi-host TPU pretraining of OLMo-3 7B that handles cluster submission, pod log monitoring, and automatic preemption-resilient resubmission (resume_until_done) via Kueue-aware polling. Delegates all hyperparameter logic to the inner run_olmo3_7b_stage1.sh launcher.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-mode dispatch pattern (submit/monitor/resume_until_done) with kubectl-based workload lifecycle management. The resume_until_done loop implements a state machine that detects Kueue preemption by monitoring zero-active-pod counts and JobSet terminal state.

nodes.name: require_env
nodes.summary: Validates that all required environment variables are set, collecting missing ones and exiting with an error listing them if any are absent.

nodes.name: submit_workload
nodes.summary: Constructs and submits an XPK workload to the TPU cluster, forwarding all environment variables (HF_TOKEN, data paths, hyperparameters) into the container command that invokes the inner run_olmo3_7b_stage1.sh launcher with gcsfuse enabled.

nodes.name: monitor_workload
nodes.summary: Polls kubectl for pod status until a Running pod appears, then streams multi-pod logs to stdout and a local file. Exits with diagnostics if pods enter a terminal non-running state.

nodes.name: resume_until_done
nodes.summary: Auto-resubmit loop that drives pretraining to completion across preemptions: submits the workload, monitors for terminal state or Kueue preemption (zero-active-pod detection), checks the latest checkpoint step on GCS, and resubmits with backoff until the target step is reached or MAX_RETRIES is exhausted.

nodes.name: test_convergence_125m_params.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end convergence test for a 125M parameter model on NVIDIA A3 GPUs, training for 2550 steps with FSDP parallelism and asserting the final loss is below a configurable threshold.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports multiple dataset types (c4-array_record, HuggingFace) via conditional branches, and configures XLA GPU optimization flags for latency hiding and pipelined collectives.

nodes.name: test_convergence_1b_params.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end convergence test for a 1B parameter model on NVIDIA A3 GPUs, training for 2550 steps and validating loss convergence against a threshold. Uses default model dimensions unlike the 125M variant which specifies custom embedding and layer sizes.
nodes.languageNotes: Nearly identical structure to the 125M test but omits explicit model dimension overrides (base_emb_dim, base_num_query_heads, etc.), relying on config defaults for the 1B model size.

nodes.name: test_gemma3_logits.sh
nodes.summary: Integration test for Gemma3-4B on NVIDIA A3 GPU that converts a Flax checkpoint from Kaggle to MaxText format, then runs a forward pass logit checker to verify output correctness with configurable absolute and relative tolerances.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes commented-out code for generating unscanned checkpoints on GPU (skipped due to CPU process errors), using a pre-generated unscanned checkpoint path instead.

nodes.name: test_llama2_7b.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test for Llama2-7B on NVIDIA A3 GPUs covering the full workflow: converting a Meta PyTorch checkpoint to Orbax format, generating an unscanned parameter checkpoint, training for 30 steps, and running autoregressive decoding.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates the complete MaxText lifecycle for a third-party model including checkpoint conversion, training with cuDNN flash attention via TransformerEngine, and decode with reduced GPU memory fraction.

nodes.name: test_checkpoint_compatibility.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test verifying checkpoint compatibility across different input pipeline types (grain and tfds) by training, checkpointing, and resuming with alternating pipelines.

nodes.name: test_checkpointing.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test for checkpoint save and restore functionality, running two sequential training jobs to verify that training correctly resumes from a saved checkpoint with consistent loss metrics.

nodes.name: test_generate_param_only_checkpoint.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test that creates a training checkpoint, generates a parameter-only (decode-optimized) checkpoint from it, and then runs decode inference to verify the full checkpoint conversion pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses getopts for argument parsing with support for dry-run mode, attention type, quantization, and tensor parallelism configuration.

nodes.name: helpFunction
nodes.summary: Prints usage information for the test script, listing all supported command-line options including dataset path, output path, tensor parallelism, attention type, and quantization.

nodes.name: test_jdi.sh
nodes.summary: Minimal end-to-end test that verifies JAX distributed initialization (jax.distributed.initialize) works correctly, ensuring the multi-host JAX runtime can be set up.

nodes.name: test_mtc_phase_2_save_path.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test for multi-tier checkpointing (MTC) phase 2 save path, training a model with local checkpoint directory and periodic multi-tier backup enabled.

nodes.name: test_multi_tier_checkpointing.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test for emergency/multi-tier checkpointing that trains, saves local checkpoints, restores from them, and validates loss consistency via eval_assert.

nodes.name: 1_test_deepseek.sh
nodes.summary: Step 1 of the DeepSeek V3 671B end-to-end test pipeline that converts HuggingFace checkpoints (bf16) to MaxText-compatible scanned and unscanned formats on CPU, preparing them for subsequent training and decoding steps.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu environment variable to force CPU-only execution for checkpoint conversion, avoiding TPU/GPU requirements during this conversion step.

nodes.name: 1_test_deepseek_mtp.sh
nodes.summary: Step 1 of the DeepSeek V3 671B Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) test pipeline that converts HuggingFace checkpoints to MaxText scanned format on CPU with MTP support enabled via the --enable_mtp flag.

nodes.name: 2_test_deepseek.sh
nodes.summary: Step 2 of the DeepSeek V3 671B end-to-end test pipeline that runs on v5p-128 TPU daily, verifying forward pass logits against golden references and performing inference decoding with the converted checkpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stringent numerical tolerances (atol=1.5, rtol=1.5, max_kl_div=0.1) with float32 precision settings to ensure logit reproducibility across checkpoint conversions.

nodes.name: 2_test_deepseek_mtp.sh
nodes.summary: Step 2 of the DeepSeek V3 671B MTP test pipeline that runs a short 10-step fine-tuning job on v5p-128 TPU to verify the Multi-Token Prediction training loop works correctly with expert parallelism.
nodes.languageNotes: Configures MTP with mtp_num_layers=1 and mtp_loss_scaling_factor=0.1, using flash attention and 16-way expert parallelism with 4-way FSDP parallelism across the v5p-128 TPU slice.

nodes.name: test_gemma2.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test for Gemma2-2B on TPU v4-8 that converts a Kaggle Flax checkpoint to MaxText format, runs decoding (scanned and unscanned), finetuning, pretraining, and validates forward pass logits against golden values.

nodes.name: test_gemma2_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test for converting a Gemma2-2B MaxText checkpoint to Hugging Face format and validating the conversion by comparing forward pass logits and KL divergence against the original HF golden model.

nodes.name: test_gemma2_to_mt.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test for converting a Gemma2-2B Hugging Face checkpoint to MaxText format (both scanned and unscanned), validating logits against the HF golden model, and running decoding and finetuning workflows with the converted checkpoint.

nodes.name: test_gemma3.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test validating the Gemma3-4B pre-training pipeline on TPU by running inference on a pre-converted checkpoint, executing pre-training, and verifying inference on the resulting checkpoint. Supports both text-only and multimodal modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Linear shell script with conditional branches for multimodal vs text-only inference paths; invokes MaxText Python modules via python3 -m.

nodes.name: test_gemma3_multimodal_sft.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test validating the Gemma3-4B multimodal supervised fine-tuning (SFT) pipeline on TPU using the ChartQA dataset. Installs JetStream, runs inference on the converted checkpoint, performs SFT, then re-runs inference on the fine-tuned checkpoint.

nodes.name: test_gemma3_rl.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test validating the Gemma3-4B reinforcement learning (GRPO) pipeline on TPU by running vLLM inference, performing RL training, and verifying inference on the RL-trained checkpoint.

nodes.name: test_gemma3_sft.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test validating the Gemma3-4B text-only supervised fine-tuning (SFT) pipeline on TPU by running vLLM inference, performing SFT training, and verifying inference on the resulting checkpoint.

nodes.name: test_gemma3_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: Converts a MaxText Gemma3-4B checkpoint back to Hugging Face format, supporting both scanned and unscanned checkpoint layouts and optional multimodal conversion.

nodes.name: test_gemma3_to_mt.sh
nodes.summary: Converts a Hugging Face Gemma3-4B checkpoint to MaxText format in both scanned and unscanned layouts, then validates correctness via a forward-pass logit comparison against the original HF model.
nodes.languageNotes: Produces both scanned (for training) and unscanned (for inference) checkpoint variants; uses KL divergence threshold for logit comparison validation.

nodes.name: convert_gemma4.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script that converts a Gemma 4 26B instruction-tuned HuggingFace checkpoint to MaxText format, then validates the conversion by running a forward pass logit checker with KL divergence comparison. Supports both text-only and multimodal modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses tile dimension flags (wi_tile_fwd_embed_dim, wo_tile_fwd_mlp_dim) to reduce VMEM usage for fitting into v5p TPU chips.

nodes.name: convert_gemma4_pt.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script that converts a Gemma 4 26B pre-trained (non-instruction-tuned) HuggingFace checkpoint to MaxText format and validates via forward pass logit checking. Mirrors convert_gemma4.sh but targets the base pre-trained model variant.

nodes.name: test_gemma4_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script that converts a MaxText-format Gemma 4 26B checkpoint back to HuggingFace format, then validates the round-trip conversion by comparing logits and KL divergence against the original HF model. Designed to run on a v5p-8 TPU.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a looser KL divergence threshold (0.1) compared to the HF-to-MaxText scripts (0.03), reflecting expected precision loss in the round-trip conversion.

nodes.name: convert_gemma4.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script that converts a HuggingFace Gemma4-31B-IT checkpoint to MaxText format and validates correctness by running a forward pass logit checker with KL divergence comparison, supporting both text-only and multimodal modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bash conditional branching to handle text-only vs multimodal conversion paths, invoking Python modules for the actual conversion and validation logic.

nodes.name: convert_gemma4_pt.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script that converts a HuggingFace Gemma4-31B pre-trained (non-instruction-tuned) checkpoint to MaxText format and validates correctness via forward pass logit comparison, mirroring the structure of convert_gemma4.sh but for the base model variant.

nodes.name: test_gemma4_to_hf.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script that converts a MaxText Gemma4-31B checkpoint back to HuggingFace format and validates the round-trip conversion by running a forward pass logit checker with KL divergence comparison against the original HuggingFace model.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents the full conversion round-trip workflow in comments and references convert_gemma4.sh as a prerequisite for producing the MaxText checkpoint input.

nodes.name: 1_test_llama3.1_70b.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test step 1 for Llama 3.1 70B: downloads Meta's PyTorch checkpoint from GCS, converts it to MaxText-compatible Orbax format (scanned), then generates an unscanned checkpoint for efficient decoding.

nodes.name: 2_test_llama3.1_70b.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test step 2 for Llama 3.1 70B on v4-128 TPU: runs decoding with both scanned and unscanned checkpoints, performs finetuning with the scanned checkpoint, and validates forward pass logits against golden values.

nodes.name: 3_test_llama3.1_70b.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test step 3 for DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B: downloads HuggingFace checkpoint, converts to MaxText format, validates forward pass logits against golden data for scanned/unscanned formats, tests round-trip HuggingFace conversion, and runs inference decoding on TPU.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains extensive example output comments demonstrating the model's generation behavior, which inflates line count significantly beyond the actual test logic.

nodes.name: 1_test_llama3.1_8b.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test step 1 for LLama3.1-8B: downloads Meta checkpoint from GCS, converts it to MaxText-compatible Orbax format via llama_or_mistral_ckpt, then generates an unscanned (unrolled) checkpoint for efficient decoding.

nodes.name: 2_test_llama3.1_8b.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test step 2 for LLama3.1-8B on v5p-8 TPU: runs decoding on both scanned and unscanned checkpoints, fine-tunes with context parallelism, validates forward-pass logits against golden data, and tests Orbax-to-HuggingFace checkpoint conversion.

nodes.name: 3_test_llama3.1_8b.sh
nodes.summary: Comprehensive checkpoint round-trip validation for DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-8B: converts HuggingFace checkpoint to MaxText scanned format, verifies forward-pass logits, converts back to HuggingFace, generates unscanned checkpoint, and runs decoding with sample output comparisons.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains extensive commented model output samples that serve as reference golden text for manual verification of decoding quality.

nodes.name: run_sft.sh
nodes.summary: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) workflow script for LLama3.1-8B: optionally converts a HuggingFace checkpoint to MaxText format, runs SFT on the ultrachat_200k dataset, and locates the latest fine-tuned checkpoint for downstream use.

nodes.name: 1_test_llama4.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script (step 1 of 2) that converts a Llama4 Scout or Maverick HuggingFace checkpoint to MaxText (Orbax) unscanned format on a CPU VM, downloading the checkpoint from GCS and running the conversion via the llama4_ckpt_unscanned module.

nodes.name: 2_test_llama4.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test script (step 2 of 2) that takes the converted MaxText (unscanned Orbax) Llama4 checkpoint and runs inference on a TPU VM, performing forward-pass logit checking with configurable tolerances to validate checkpoint correctness.

nodes.name: Run_Llama4.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for running Llama4 models (Scout 17B-16E and Maverick 17B-128E) in MaxText, covering checkpoint conversion from PyTorch/HuggingFace formats, pre-training on TPUs, decoding/inference, and supported Mixture-of-Experts strategies (dropless, MegaBlocks, JAX ragged_dot).

nodes.name: llama_finetuning_test.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end TPU test that fine-tunes a Llama 2 7B model from a pre-existing checkpoint and asserts the final training loss stays below a configurable threshold.

nodes.name: run_sft.sh
nodes.summary: Full supervised fine-tuning (SFT) workflow script that optionally converts a HuggingFace checkpoint to MaxText format, runs SFT training on the ultrachat_200k dataset, and converts the fine-tuned checkpoint back to HuggingFace format.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses gcloud storage ls to discover the latest numbered checkpoint directory, sorting numerically to find the most recent one.

nodes.name: test_checkpoint_resharding.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test that verifies checkpoint resharding by training with one parallelism configuration (DCN data + ICI FSDP) and restoring with a different configuration (DCN data + ICI FSDP + tensor parallelism).

nodes.name: test_convergence_1b_params.sh
nodes.summary: Convergence test for a 1B parameter model on 64 chips, supporting both Grain and HuggingFace dataset types, with configurable steps, batch size, and loss threshold following Chinchilla scaling laws.
nodes.languageNotes: Parses key=value pairs from command-line arguments and exports them as environment variables, supporting flexible parameterization for CI runs.

nodes.name: test_decode_load_quantized_ckpt.sh
nodes.summary: Test script that loads a pre-quantized (int8) checkpoint for a Llama 2 model and runs inference decoding, with support for dry-run mode via command-line flags.

nodes.name: test_decode_save_quantized_ckpt.sh
nodes.summary: Test script that loads a Llama 2 model checkpoint, runs int8 quantized decoding, and saves the quantized parameters to a GCS path for later reuse by the load test.

nodes.name: test_gpt3.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test that converts a GPT-3 52K model checkpoint from Paxml format to MaxText format and then runs a short training session with synthetic data to verify the conversion.

nodes.name: test_grpo.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test for the GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) reinforcement learning trainer, configuring separate training and inference parallelism with Pathways support on TPUs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX_PLATFORMS=proxy with a gRPC backend target for Pathways-based multi-slice training and inference configuration.

nodes.name: test_sft_trainer.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test for the SFT trainer that fine-tunes a pre-trained model with HuggingFace datasets, asserts loss convergence, and validates the fine-tuned model by running inference decoding with a user prompt.

nodes.name: test_vocab_creation.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end test that verifies vocabulary creation by running a short training session with a custom vocabulary path and asserting that the vocabulary file was properly generated.

nodes.name: reference_hlo_deepseek3.txt
nodes.summary: Reference XLA HLO (High-Level Optimizer) dump for the DeepSeek V3 model's jit_train_step computation, used as a golden snapshot in regression tests to verify that JAX compilation produces the expected sharded computation graph with 99 input/output aliases and 4-way SPMD partitioning.
nodes.languageNotes: XLA HLO is the intermediate representation produced by JAX/XLA compilation. This file captures the scheduled HLO module including tensor layouts, sharding annotations, and SPMD partitioning configuration for a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with both dense and MoE layers.

nodes.name: reference_hlo_llama3_8b.txt
nodes.summary: Reference XLA HLO dump for the LLaMA 3 8B model's jit_train_step computation, serving as a golden test fixture to validate that JAX compilation produces the expected sharded computation graph with 39 input/output aliases, 4-way SPMD partitioning, and a 128256-token vocabulary embedding.
nodes.languageNotes: The HLO captures LLaMA 3 8B's architecture with 4096-dim hidden size, 32 attention heads, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), and 14336-dim FFN, compiled with bf16 precision and fused computations for embedding gather/scatter operations.

nodes.name: reference_hlo_qwen3_1.7b.txt
nodes.summary: Reference XLA HLO dump for the Qwen3 1.7B model's jit_train_step computation, used as a golden test fixture to verify JAX compilation produces the expected sharded computation graph with 42 input/output aliases, 4-way SPMD partitioning, and a 151936-token vocabulary embedding.
nodes.languageNotes: The HLO captures Qwen3 1.7B's architecture with 2048-dim hidden size, 16 attention heads, grouped-query attention (8 KV heads), and 6144-dim FFN, compiled with bf16 precision and rope-based positional embeddings.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected activation sharding data for DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-16 (slice 1, default rule), mapping each intermediate activation tensor to its logical axes and JAX PartitionSpec for sharding verification tests.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry maps a named activation tensor (with dtype and shape) to its logical sharding axes and corresponding JAX PartitionSpec, used to validate that the sharding strategy produces the expected partition layout.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected logical sharding specifications for all DeepSeek2-16B model parameters and optimizer states on TPU v7x-16 (slice 1, default rule), defining the partition_spec and shape for each parameter path including dense layers, MoE layers, self-attention, and embeddings.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains 84 sections covering model parameters (.params), first-moment optimizer state (.mu), second-moment optimizer state (.nu), and step counters, with partition specs mapping logical axis names like 'embed', 'dense_layers', 'mlp' to tensor dimensions.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected named sharding specifications for DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-16 (slice 1, default rule), providing full mesh configuration (axis names, shape) alongside partition specs and tensor shapes for every model parameter and optimizer state.
nodes.languageNotes: The most detailed of the three sharding files, each entry includes the full TPU mesh definition with 12 axes (diloco, data, stage, fsdp, fsdp_transpose, context, context_autoregressive, tensor, tensor_transpose, tensor_sequence, expert, autoregressive) with FSDP=16 as the primary parallelism axis.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected activation sharding specifications for DeepSeek2-16B on a TPU v7x-16 single-slice topology using the pure-FSDP sharding rule. Lists logic axes and PartitionSpec mappings for each activation tensor.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected logical sharding specifications for all model parameters and optimizer state of DeepSeek2-16B under pure-FSDP on TPU v7x-16. Covers decoder layers, MoE blocks, attention, embeddings, and Adam optimizer mu/nu buffers with their logical axis assignments.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected named sharding (PartitionSpec) assignments for all parameters and optimizer state of DeepSeek2-16B under pure-FSDP on TPU v7x-16. Maps each tensor to its concrete mesh axis placements across dense layers, MoE blocks, self-attention, and optimizer buffers.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture capturing expected activation sharding layouts for the DeepSeek2-16B model on a TPU v7x-8 slice 1 with the 2D-FSDP sharding rule (fsdp_transpose_parallelism=2), recording logic axes and PartitionSpec for each activation tensor.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry maps an activation tensor (with dtype and shape annotation) to its logical axis names and JAX PartitionSpec, enabling regression tests for sharding correctness.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture storing expected logical sharding specifications for all parameters and optimizer state of the DeepSeek2-16B model under a 2D-FSDP rule on TPU v7x-8 slice 1, mapping each parameter path to its partition_spec and shape.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers params, optimizer mu (first moment), optimizer nu (second moment), and step counter. Each entry lists logical partition axis names and corresponding tensor dimensions.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture recording the full named sharding information (mesh definition, partition specs, and tensor shapes) for every parameter and optimizer state variable of the DeepSeek2-16B model on TPU v7x-8 slice 1 with a 2D-FSDP sharding rule.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry includes the complete mesh configuration (axis names: data, stage, fsdp, fsdp_transpose, context, expert with their sizes) alongside the partition spec and shape, providing the most detailed sharding snapshot of the three companion files.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected activation sharding dump for DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-8 slice 1 with context-parallelism-as-expert-parallelism rule (FSDP=-1, context=2, expert=2), recording logic axes and PartitionSpec for each activation tensor to validate sharding correctness in tests.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected logical sharding specifications for DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-8 slice 1 with cp-as-ep rule, mapping each model parameter and optimizer state tensor to its logical partition spec and shape across 84 entries including dense layers, MoE blocks, shared experts, and token embedder.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers both model params and Adam optimizer states (mu/nu), with separate entries for dense layers versus MoE layers reflecting the DeepSeek2 hybrid architecture.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected named sharding specifications for DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-8 slice 1 with cp-as-ep rule, providing full mesh configuration (data, stage, fsdp, context, expert axes with shape 1x1x2x2x2) alongside partition specs and shapes for all model parameters and optimizer states.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry includes the full 5-axis mesh definition (data=1, stage=1, fsdp=2, context=2, expert=2) alongside tensor-level partition specs, providing the most complete sharding representation of the three companion files.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture containing expected activation sharding specifications for DeepSeek2-16B model on TPU v7x-8 (slice 1) with expert-parallelism-as-context-parallelism rule (FSDP=-1, expert=2). Maps each activation tensor to its logical axes and JAX PartitionSpec.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON dump of JAX PartitionSpec objects mapping activation tensors (e.g., attention Q/K/V, MLP intermediates) to mesh axis assignments for FSDP and expert parallelism.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture containing expected logical sharding specifications for all parameters and optimizer states of DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-8 (slice 1, ep-as-cp rule). Covers decoder layers, MoE blocks (gate, shared experts, routed experts), self-attention (MLA with wkv_a/wkv_b), and Adam optimizer mu/nu states.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry maps a parameter path (e.g., decoder dense layers, MoE gate kernels, optimizer first/second moments) to its partition spec and tensor shape, enabling regression tests for sharding correctness.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture containing expected named sharding specifications (including full mesh configuration) for DeepSeek2-16B on TPU v7x-8 (slice 1, ep-as-cp rule). Extends logical_shardings.json by including the mesh axis names (data, stage, fsdp, expert) and their shapes alongside partition specs for every parameter and optimizer state.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry includes the full 4-axis mesh definition (data=1, stage=1, fsdp=4, expert=2) so tests can verify both the logical sharding and the physical mesh layout for a specific TPU topology.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected activation sharding test fixture for DeepSeek2-16B on v6e-16 TPU (slice 1) with FSDP auto-parallelism and 4-way expert parallelism. Records logical axes and PartitionSpec for each intermediate activation tensor.
nodes.languageNotes: Each entry maps an activation tensor (with dtype and shape in the key) to its logical axis assignment and JAX PartitionSpec for FSDP/expert parallelism.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected logical sharding test fixture for DeepSeek2-16B on v6e-16 TPU (slice 1) with FSDP auto-parallelism and 4-way expert parallelism. Maps every model parameter and optimizer state variable to its logical partition spec and tensor shape.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers params, optimizer mu (first moment), optimizer nu (second moment), and step counter, each with partition_spec arrays naming the logical mesh axes assigned to each tensor dimension.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected named sharding test fixture for DeepSeek2-16B on v6e-16 TPU (slice 1) with FSDP auto-parallelism and 4-way expert parallelism. Records full mesh topology (axis names, shape) alongside partition specs for every parameter and optimizer state, providing the most detailed sharding snapshot of the three fixture files.
nodes.languageNotes: The mesh definition includes 12 named axes (diloco, data, stage, fsdp, fsdp_transpose, context, context_autoregressive, tensor, tensor_transpose, tensor_sequence, expert, autoregressive) with a 4-way FSDP x 4-way expert layout on 16 TPU v6e chips.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected activation sharding dump for DeepSeek2-16B on v6e-16 slice 1 with pipeline-large-moe rule, FSDP auto-parallelism, expert parallelism of 4, and ring-of-experts enabled. Records logical axes and PartitionSpec for each intermediate activation tensor.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected logical sharding specifications for all model parameters and optimizer states of DeepSeek2-16B on v6e-16 slice 1 with pipeline-large-moe rule, expert parallelism of 4, and ring-of-experts. Covers decoder layers, MoE blocks, shared experts, attention, embeddings, and Adam mu/nu states.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Expected named sharding annotations for all model parameters and optimizer states of DeepSeek2-16B on v6e-16 slice 1 with pipeline-large-moe rule, expert parallelism of 4, and ring-of-experts. Provides detailed per-parameter sharding specifications including shape, dtype, and partition mapping across the full model tree.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture defining activation input sharding specifications for the GPT-OSS-20B model on TPU v7x-16 (slice 1, default rule), mapping each activation tensor to its logical axes and JAX PartitionSpec for FSDP parallelism.
nodes.languageNotes: Maps JAX activation tensors (query, key, value, MLP intermediates) to PartitionSpec sharding descriptors used by MaxText's sharding infrastructure.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture containing logical sharding specifications for all parameters and optimizer states of the GPT-OSS-20B model on TPU v7x-16 (slice 1, default rule), covering decoder layers, attention, MLP blocks, embeddings, and Adam mu/nu moments.
nodes.languageNotes: Enumerates logical axis names and shapes for every model parameter and optimizer state variable across two decoder layers, including attention (query/key/value/out), MLP (gate/wi/wo), layer norms, and token embeddings.

nodes.name: named_shardings.json

nodes.languageNotes: The largest of the three sharding files (6000+ lines), providing complete NamedSharding objects with mesh topology and per-axis partition assignments for comprehensive sharding validation tests.

nodes.name: input_shardings.json

nodes.languageNotes: Contains JAX PartitionSpec annotations mapping logical axes to physical mesh dimensions (fsdp, expert) for activation tensors.

nodes.name: logical_shardings.json

nodes.languageNotes: Covers params, optimizer mu (first moment) and nu (second moment) state for a 2-layer decoder with GptOssAttention and GptOssMlp sublayers, each with detailed logical axis assignments.

nodes.languageNotes: At 6004 lines, this is the most detailed sharding file in the set, containing explicit named sharding for every parameter tensor including both model params and Adam optimizer mu/nu states across a 2-layer GPT-OSS decoder.

nodes.name: .coveragerc
nodes.summary: Python coverage configuration enabling branch coverage for the maxtext source package, excluding tests and virtual environments from reporting.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore rules for the understand-anything analysis tool, specifying files and directories to exclude from codebase knowledge graph generation.

nodes.name: .yamllint
nodes.summary: YAML lint configuration extending the relaxed preset with all major rules disabled, allowing flexible YAML formatting across the project.

nodes.name: AUTHORS
nodes.summary: Project authorship attribution file listing Google LLC as the author of MaxText.

nodes.name: encoding_dsv32.py
nodes.summary: Implements DSv32 message encoding and decoding for the API server, handling OpenAI-format tool calls, system/user/assistant message rendering, thinking mode support, and completion text parsing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses custom DSML (Domain-Specific Markup Language) format for encoding tool call arguments, with template-based message rendering.

nodes.name: render_message
nodes.summary: Renders a single chat message (system, user, assistant, or tool) into the DSv32 encoding format, handling tool calls, thinking mode, and response format templates.

nodes.name: encode_messages
nodes.summary: Encodes a complete list of chat messages into a single DSv32 formatted string, optionally dropping thinking messages and adding a BOS token.

nodes.name: parse_tool_calls
nodes.summary: Parses tool call blocks from raw completion text by reading delimited sections for tool names, IDs, and DSML-encoded arguments.

nodes.name: parse_message_from_completion_text
nodes.summary: Parses a complete assistant message from raw completion text, extracting thinking content, tool calls, and regular text into an OpenAI-compatible message format.

nodes.name: encode_arguments_to_dsml
nodes.summary: Converts tool call arguments from JSON format into DSML (Domain-Specific Markup Language) format for the DSv32 encoding scheme.

nodes.name: drop_thinking_messages
nodes.summary: Removes thinking messages from the conversation history prior to the last user message, preserving the most recent thinking context for active inference.

nodes.name: _read_until_stop

nodes.summary: Converts E2M1 (2-bit exponent, 1-bit mantissa) encoded values to standard floating point by manually reconstructing the value from sign, exponent, and mantissa bits.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main entry point for MXFP4 dequantization: iterates safetensor shards, identifies quantized weight pairs (blocks + scales), dequantizes them, and writes output files with updated model index.

nodes.name: main

nodes.summary: Strips whitespace and applies NLTK sentence tokenization to both prediction and target strings for consistent ROUGE score computation.

nodes.summary: Aggregates ROUGE score results from multiple parallel chunks by computing the mean across all chunk results for each metric type.

nodes.summary: Parses command-line arguments for the accuracy evaluation script including checkpoint path, accuracy file path, dataset file, tokenizer type, dtype, and verbosity options.

nodes.name: sharding_utils.py
nodes.summary: Provides analytical tools to calculate matmul resource requirements (compute, communication, memory) under various sharding strategies and visualize comparisons between parallelism schemes.
nodes.languageNotes: Models TPU ICI bandwidth, latency-bound communications, and multi-axis sharding (data, FSDP, tensor, expert parallelism) with matplotlib-based comparison plots.

nodes.name: calculate_matmul_resources
nodes.summary: Calculates compute FLOPs, communication volume, and memory usage for a matmul operation given activation/weight shapes and a multi-axis sharding configuration.

nodes.name: plot_sharding_scheme_comparison
nodes.summary: Generates grouped bar charts comparing compute time, communication time, comm/compute ratio, and memory footprint across multiple sharding schemes.

nodes.name: test_sharding_utils.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive test suite verifying the sharding resource calculator against hand-computed expected values for 10 different sharding strategies including data, FSDP, tensor, expert, and mixed parallelism.

nodes.name: ShardingTests
nodes.summary: Test class with 10 test methods validating matmul resource calculations across different sharding configurations: no sharding, data/feature/weight/activation parallelism, tensor parallelism, expert parallelism, and mixed strategies.

nodes.name: feature_pb2.py
nodes.summary: Auto-generated Protobuf Python bindings for TensorFlow Example feature types (BytesList, FloatList, Int64List, Feature, Features, FeatureList, FeatureLists) used in the input data pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated by protoc from feature.proto; should not be edited manually. Defines the wire format for training data features.

nodes.name: gather_reduce_pallas.py
nodes.summary: Implements a high-performance Pallas kernel for gather-reduce operations on TPU SparseCore, used to group and sum rows of operands based on index arrays with optional top-k weighting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX experimental Pallas API with TPU SparseCore extensions (plsc), indirect indexing, bitcast operations, and multi-pipeline kernel composition for HBM-VMEM data movement.

nodes.name: sc_gather_reduce
nodes.summary: Pallas kernel that performs gather-reduce on TPU SparseCore by grouping operand rows via index lookup, accumulating with optional top-k weights, and writing results back in bf16.

nodes.name: common.py
nodes.summary: Provides TPU device detection and dtype selection utilities for MegaBlox GMM (Grouped Matrix Multiply) kernels, handling generation-specific bfloat16 matmul support.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive multimodal preprocessing utilities providing image loading/normalization, audio spectrogram computation (mel filter banks, FFT, windowing), and embedding merging for vision-language and audio-language models.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements NumPy-based audio feature extraction (spectrogram, mel filter bank, window functions) compatible with HuggingFace feature extractors, plus JAX-based multimodal embedding merging with vmap.

nodes.name: PreprocessorOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding multimodal preprocessor outputs including pixel values, pixel masks, aspect ratios for images, and audio values/masks for audio inputs.

nodes.name: merge_mm_embeddings
nodes.summary: Merges multimodal embeddings (image/audio) into text embeddings at positions indicated by a mask, handling variable numbers of tokens per modality patch via JAX vmap.

nodes.name: spectrogram
nodes.summary: Computes a spectrogram from a waveform using STFT with configurable windowing, FFT length, dithering, preemphasis, optional mel filtering, and various log scaling modes.

nodes.name: mel_filter_bank
nodes.summary: Creates a frequency-domain mel filter bank matrix with configurable frequency range, number of filters, normalization mode (slaney), and multiple mel scale options (htk, slaney, kaldi).

nodes.name: window_function
nodes.summary: Generates window functions (boxcar, hamming, hanning, povey) for STFT computation with options for periodic windowing, zero-padding, and centering.

nodes.name: amplitude_to_db
nodes.summary: Converts an amplitude spectrogram to decibel scale with configurable reference, minimum value, and optional dynamic range compression.

nodes.name: power_to_db
nodes.summary: Converts a power spectrogram to decibel scale with configurable reference, minimum value, and optional dynamic range compression.

nodes.name: hertz_to_mel
nodes.summary: Converts frequency values from Hertz to mel scale using htk, slaney, or kaldi formulas.

nodes.name: mel_to_hertz
nodes.summary: Converts mel-scale values back to Hertz using htk, slaney, or kaldi inverse formulas.

nodes.name: load_audio
nodes.summary: Loads an audio file from a local path using librosa, returning the waveform resampled to a target sample rate.

nodes.name: normalize_images
nodes.summary: Normalizes image pixel values by subtracting mean and dividing by standard deviation for each channel.

nodes.name: load_image_from_path
nodes.summary: Loads an image from a file path, converts it to RGB, and returns it as a NumPy array.

nodes.summary: Package initializer for the scratch_code module containing exploratory scripts and notebooks for analysis and verification.

nodes.summary: Analyzes ShareGPT conversation datasets to compute token statistics and estimate prefill/generate latency times for inference benchmarking.

nodes.summary: Estimates total prefill and generate latency for a set of conversations using bucket-based prefill timing and per-token decode timing.

nodes.name: get_conversations_from_file
nodes.summary: Loads and filters ShareGPT conversations from a JSON file, applying maximum input/output token constraints and reporting statistics.

nodes.summary: Jupyter notebook demonstrating how to configure and run MaxText models from configuration files for interactive exploration.
