nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step guide for running MaxText knowledge distillation on GKE TPU clusters via XPK, covering config selection, smoke testing, Docker image building, job submission, monitoring, resumption, and checkpoint management.

nodes.name: run_distill_xpk.sh
nodes.summary: Reference launcher script for MaxText knowledge distillation on GKE TPU clusters via XPK, handling Docker image preparation, workspace upload, workload submission, log monitoring, and automatic resume-until-done loops.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a multi-subcommand CLI (prep_image, upload_runner, submit, monitor, resume_until_done) with robust environment variable validation and GCR image management.

nodes.name: submit_workload
nodes.summary: Submits a distillation training workload to a GKE TPU cluster via xpk, configuring TPU type, node count, Docker image, and the MaxText distillation training command.

nodes.name: resume_until_done
nodes.summary: Auto-retry loop that monitors a submitted workload and re-submits on failure until the job completes successfully, enabling resilient long-running distillation training.

nodes.name: prep_image
nodes.summary: Layers Tunix and its dependencies on top of the MaxText base Docker image, then force-reinstalls compatible JAX/jaxlib/libtpu versions.

nodes.name: monitor_workload
nodes.summary: Streams logs from a running XPK workload on GKE, polling until the job reaches a terminal state.

nodes.name: verify_saved_logits.py
nodes.summary: Verification script that validates the correctness and completeness of saved top-k teacher logits in ArrayRecord files, checking for required keys, shape consistency, and expected step counts.

nodes.name: verify_array_records
nodes.summary: Iterates over ArrayRecord files in a directory, deserializing safetensors records and asserting required keys, top-k dimension shapes, and total step counts match expectations.

nodes.name: accelerator_to_spec_map.py
nodes.summary: Provides a static lookup table mapping user-facing TPU/GPU accelerator names (e.g., 'v5p-128', 'v6e-256', 'tpu7x-8192') to their hardware characteristics including topology, chip config, devices per slice, and wrap dimensions.
nodes.languageNotes: Primarily a data-driven module using Python dataclasses. The bulk of the file is a large dictionary literal mapping ~200 accelerator names to SystemCharacteristics instances.

nodes.name: SystemCharacteristics
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding hardware properties of an accelerator slice: platform type, topology name, chip config, chips-per-host bounds, devices per slice, and wrap-around dimensions.

nodes.name: elastic_utils.py
nodes.summary: Implements elastic training utilities for dynamic slice scaling on Pathways-backed TPU pods, including checkpoint cleanup after elastic events, retry decorators, device enumeration for live slices, and scale-up event detection.

nodes.name: clean_up_checkpoints
nodes.summary: Cleans up incomplete checkpoints in GCS after an elastic event by checking for commit_success markers and deleting the latest checkpoint directory if it was not fully committed.

nodes.name: elastic_retry
nodes.summary: Creates an elastic retry decorator that re-executes the decorated function upon elastic events, cleaning up partial checkpoints and invoking optional callbacks between retries.

nodes.name: live_devices
nodes.summary: Returns the list of currently live JAX devices, filtering by active slice indices when running on Pathways with elastic training enabled.

nodes.name: get_devices_per_host
nodes.summary: Dynamically calculates the number of TPU/GPU chips per physical worker host by counting live devices grouped by task ID.

nodes.name: maybe_elastic_scale_up
nodes.summary: Checks for a pending scale-up event and, if detected, waits for the current checkpoint to finish saving before raising a ScaleUpSignalError to trigger reinitialization.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines the StopTraining custom exception used to cleanly halt a training loop from within the training logic.

nodes.name: max_logging.py
nodes.summary: Provides thin wrapper functions around absl.logging (log, debug, info, warning, error) with correct stacklevel reporting and a NoisyLogFilter class to suppress known noisy log messages.

nodes.name: NoisyLogFilter
nodes.summary: A logging filter that suppresses known noisy log messages such as 'Type mismatch' warnings from tunix and 'No mapping for flat state' messages.

nodes.name: pipeline_utils.py
nodes.summary: Implements circular pipeline parallelism utilities including partition spec manipulation for FSDP/MoE weight sharding, custom VJP-based gradient accumulation across microbatches, and Flax nn.scan wrappers for pipeline stage execution with weight prefetching and activation rematerialization.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of JAX custom_vjp to implement memory-efficient backward passes with manual gradient accumulation, jax.linear_transpose for deriving reduce-scatter from all-gather, and Flax nn.scan/nn.remat for pipeline orchestration.

nodes.name: get_mesh_axis_dim_indices
nodes.summary: Finds the tensor dimension index sharded across a specific physical mesh axis in a PyTree of PartitionSpecs, returning -1 if the axis is not found.

nodes.name: derive_stage_weight_partition_specs
nodes.summary: Derives modified physical partition specs for weights inside the scanned pipeline loop by removing all-gathered axes and dropping the outer pipeline stage dimension.

nodes.name: remove_gathered_mesh_axes
nodes.summary: Strips FSDP and MoE expert mesh axes from a PartitionSpec, replacing them with None to indicate replication after an all-gather operation.

nodes.name: strip_pipeline_repeat_logical_axis
nodes.summary: Removes the 'circular_repeats' dimension from a logical PartitionSpec PyTree to prepare specs for inner pipeline loop execution.

nodes.name: remove_logically_partition
nodes.summary: Unwraps LogicallyPartitioned wrappers from weight tensors in a PyTree, extracting the underlying values for direct computation.

nodes.name: create_gradient_accumulation_scan
nodes.summary: Creates a memory-efficient jax.lax.scan loop for pipeline microbatches with a custom VJP that rematerializes forward passes and manually accumulates gradients on heavy parameters across scan iterations.

nodes.name: create_pipeline_stage
nodes.summary: Builds a complete pipeline stage execution block that prefetches FSDP weights, runs microbatches via custom VJP scan, and uses jax.linear_transpose to derive reduce-scatter from the forward all-gather for efficient backward communication.

nodes.name: create_flax_pipeline_scan
nodes.summary: Wraps the pipeline stage function in Flax nn.remat and nn.scan to execute the full pipeline schedule with gradient checkpointing and proper variable/RNG handling across iterations.

nodes.name: qk_clip_utils.py
nodes.summary: Implements QK-Clip (Muon Clip) for Multi-Latent Attention (MLA) layers, calculating maximum attention logit metrics and applying per-head scaling factors to query and key projection weights to prevent logit explosion during training.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses JAX tree_map_with_path to traverse nested parameter PyTrees by path, enabling targeted weight modification of specific MLA projection matrices (wq_b, wkv_b) based on their position in the model hierarchy.

nodes.name: calculate_max_logit_metric
nodes.summary: Extracts max_logits values sowed by Attention layers from intermediate outputs and computes the global maximum logit scalar across all layers.

nodes.name: apply_qk_clip
nodes.summary: Applies QK-Clip to MLA attention weights by computing per-head scaling factors from max logits and applying them to the query (wq_b) and key (wkv_b) projection matrices, scaling nope and rope components differently.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: write_metadata
nodes.summary: Creates a local TFDS builder, generates dataset_info.json and features.json for C4 shards, and patches the split info to use a list format compatible with the local reader.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: get_minimal_c4_en_dataset.py

nodes.summary: Reads records from a TFRecord source and writes them as sharded ArrayRecord files with per-shard byte limits.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: write_parquet

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Connects to MinIO, discovers C4 TFRecord shards, downloads and samples records, and writes train and validation Parquet files for HuggingFace-format testing.

nodes.name: GRPOTest

nodes.name: generate_hf_golden_logits.py

nodes.name: save_golden_logits

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration that registers custom markers (gpu_only, tpu_only, cpu_only, etc.), handles hardware-specific test skipping, applies decoupled-mode test selection, monkeypatches absl parameterized test iteration to propagate pytest markers, and forces early JAX initialization on GPU to prevent CUDA context conflicts.
nodes.languageNotes: Monkeypatches absl.testing.parameterized._ParameterizedTestIter.__iter__ to propagate pytest markers through the decorator stack, solving a compatibility issue between absl and pytest.

nodes.name: _has_tpu_backend_support
nodes.summary: Checks whether JAX has TPU backend support installed (PJRT TPU plugin) by probing for the libtpu package, distinct from detecting actual TPU hardware.

nodes.name: pytest_collection_modifyitems
nodes.summary: Pytest hook that skips hardware-specific tests when backend is unavailable, deselects external integration tests in decoupled mode, and marks remaining tests with the decoupled marker.

nodes.name: pytest_configure
nodes.summary: Registers custom pytest markers (gpu_only, tpu_only, cpu_only, tpu_backend, external_serving, external_training, decoupled, skip_on_tpu7x) dynamically during configuration.

nodes.name: test_8x7b.sh

nodes.name: normalize.py
nodes.summary: CLI tool that normalizes raw Transformer Engine benchmark results by computing percentage differences from the FP8 baseline, outputting summary tables in CSV or TSV format.

nodes.name: plot_loss_curves.py
nodes.summary: CLI tool that parses training log files for loss data, groups them by parallelism configuration (dp/tpsp/fsdp), and generates per-configuration loss curve plots for Transformer Engine benchmarking.

nodes.name: parse_loss_data
nodes.summary: Parses a training log file for lines matching the step/seconds/TFLOP/tokens/loss pattern and returns a list of extracted tuples.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that discovers log files, groups them by parallelism config extracted from filenames, and generates matplotlib loss curve plots for each configuration.

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: run_single_node_model_parallel.sh
nodes.summary: Comprehensive benchmarking shell script that runs MaxText with Transformer Engine across different parallelization strategies (DP, TPSP, FSDP) and quantization recipes (FP8, TE DelayedScaling, CurrentScaling, MXFP8), with optional profiling/tracing.

nodes.name: run_and_parse
nodes.summary: Executes a MaxText training run with specified configuration and parses the training output to extract performance metrics like TFLOP/s.

nodes.name: test_collective_matmul_llama2_7b.sh

nodes.name: test_feature.py
nodes.summary: Test module for verifying GPU-specific XLA features in HLO files, including collective matmul operation counts and FP8 GEMM operation verification against expected values.

nodes.name: XlaGpuFeatureTestCase
nodes.summary: Test case class providing methods to verify GPU-specific features in HLO files, including checking counts of collective matmul unrolled operations and FP8 GEMM operations via regex pattern matching.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that dispatches to either collective_matmul or fp8_gemm test scenarios based on command-line arguments.

nodes.name: test_fp8_gemm_llama2_7b.sh

nodes.name: test_profiler.py

nodes.name: ProfilerTest

nodes.name: eval_assert.py

nodes.name: get_last_n_data

nodes.name: test_determinism

nodes.name: main

layers.name: Model Architecture
layers.description: Neural network layer implementations (attention, MoE, embeddings, normalizations), model definitions for LLM families (Llama, Gemma, DeepSeek, Qwen, etc.), custom Pallas kernels, multimodal processors, and optimizer implementations

layers.name: Training Engine
layers.description: Pre-training, post-training (SFT, DPO, distillation, RL), and distributed training orchestration including DiLoCo support and train step compilation

layers.name: Inference Engine
layers.description: MaxEngine serving engine, offline/online decoding, vLLM integration, MLPerf benchmarking harness, and inference microbenchmark tooling for TPU and GPU

layers.name: Data Pipeline
layers.description: Input data processing pipelines for TFDS, Grain, HuggingFace datasets, tokenization, sequence packing, multi-host data loading, and instruction/DPO/distillation data formatting

layers.name: Core Utilities
layers.description: Shared utilities (checkpointing, GCS, sharding, logging, GCP monitoring), type definitions, cloud integrations (vLLM/TunIX adapters), evaluation framework, example notebooks, and tokenizer assets

layers.name: Configuration & Checkpoints
layers.description: YAML/JSON model configs for all supported architectures and hardware targets (TPU v4-v6e, GPU A3), checkpoint conversion scripts for HuggingFace/Orbax/PaxML formats, and quantization configurations

layers.name: Experimental
layers.description: Experimental features including GRPO reinforcement learning trainer, AI-powered checkpoint conversion agent with LLM-based parameter mapping, and research prototypes

layers.name: Testing
layers.description: Unit tests for layers/models/utilities, integration tests for checkpointing/training/decoding, end-to-end TPU/GPU validation scripts, golden logit reference data, and test fixtures

layers.name: Benchmarks
layers.description: Performance benchmarking infrastructure for TPU v5e/v5p/Trillium, XPK job runners, convergence experiments, disruption management, BigQuery metrics upload, and benchmark recipes

layers.name: Project Infrastructure
layers.description: Project documentation (guides, tutorials, API reference), build/deploy tooling (Dockerfiles, dependency management, setup scripts), data generation utilities, orchestration helpers, and root project configuration (pyproject.toml, linting, CI configs)

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what MaxText is: a high-performance, open-source LLM library written in pure Python/JAX targeting Google Cloud TPUs and GPUs. It supports pre-training and scalable post-training (SFT, GRPO) for models like Gemma, Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen, and Mistral. The README establishes the project's scope, installation methods, and links to comprehensive documentation on ReadTheDocs.

tour.title: Configuration System
tour.description: The configuration system is the backbone of MaxText. The base.yml file defines every default hyperparameter -- model architecture, training settings, sharding strategy, and hardware options -- while pyconfig.py loads these YAML configs, applies command-line overrides, and produces a validated Pydantic-based MaxTextConfig object. Understanding this system is essential because nearly every module reads from this config. Per-model YAML files like llama3-8b.yml override only the parameters that differ from the base.
tour.languageLesson: Python's Pydantic library provides runtime type validation for dataclasses. MaxText uses it to catch configuration errors early -- if you set a string where an int is expected, the error surfaces at config load time rather than deep inside the training loop.

tour.title: Shared Types and Constants
tour.description: With 60 fan-in edges, common_types.py is the second most-imported file in the entire codebase. It defines the shared vocabulary used everywhere: axis name constants for sharding (like 'data', 'fsdp', 'tensor'), enum types for decoder blocks and attention variants, and type aliases for JAX arrays. The globals module complements this with directory paths, epsilon values, and the mapping between MaxText model keys and HuggingFace tokenizer identifiers.
tour.languageLesson: Python Enums (like DecoderBlockType, AttentionType) enforce that only valid options are used for configuration, preventing silent misconfiguration. String axis names like 'fsdp' and 'tensor' are how JAX's SPMD partitioner maps logical dimensions to physical TPU/GPU mesh axes.

tour.title: Neural Network Building Blocks
tour.description: The layers module provides the fundamental building blocks that all models share. Initializers set up weight matrices with proper scaling. Normalizations implements RMSNorm variants used by modern LLMs. Linears provides DenseGeneral (multi-axis dense layers with quantization) and MlpBlock (gated MLP with configurable activations). NNX wrappers enable interoperability between Flax's legacy Linen API and the newer NNX API. These components are composed by every model architecture in the project.
tour.languageLesson: Flax Linen uses a functional style where modules declare parameters via self.param() and compute via __call__. Flax NNX is the newer imperative API. MaxText's nnx_wrappers.py provides ToLinen and ToNNX bridges so both styles coexist, allowing gradual migration without rewriting the entire codebase.

tour.title: Attention and Embeddings
tour.description: The attention module is the heart of every transformer model in MaxText. It combines query/key/value projections, rotary embeddings (RoPE, LLaMA, YaRN variants), KV caching for autoregressive generation, and attention operator dispatch. The embeddings module provides the complementary input processing: token embeddings, multiple rotary embedding flavors, and vision-specific embeddings for multimodal models. Together, these two modules define how input tokens become contextualized representations.
tour.languageLesson: JAX's jit (just-in-time compilation) transparently compiles these attention computations to XLA HLO graphs optimized for the target accelerator. The @nn.compact decorator in Flax lets you define parameters inline within __call__ rather than in a separate setup() method, keeping the layer definition concise.

tour.title: Decoder Stack and Model Assembly
tour.description: The decoder modules compose attention, MLP, normalization, and optional MoE layers into the full transformer decoder stack. The Linen-based decoders.py provides the production decoder with pipeline parallelism and scan-based layer stacking, while nnx_decoders.py offers a pure NNX alternative. These modules take the building blocks from Steps 4-5 and wire them into complete decoder layers and full decoder stacks that can be instantiated as a model.
tour.languageLesson: JAX's scan (jax.lax.scan) is used to stack identical decoder layers without duplicating Python objects, dramatically reducing compilation time and memory for models with dozens of layers. The scan transformation fuses the repeated layer application into a single loop in the compiled HLO.

tour.title: Quantization and Performance
tour.description: Quantization is critical for training and serving large models efficiently. With 39 fan-in and 84 fan-out edges, quantizations.py is one of the most connected files in the codebase. It implements four quantization backends -- AQT int8, FP8 (NVIDIA/NANOO), Qwix (recommended), and Transformer Engine -- that plug into the linear layers from Step 4. The splash attention kernel implements Sparse Flash Attention via Pallas (JAX's kernel language for TPUs), providing custom forward/backward kernels with block-sparse masking for memory-efficient attention.
tour.languageLesson: Pallas is JAX's embedded DSL for writing custom TPU/GPU kernels in Python. Unlike XLA's automatic fusion, Pallas gives explicit control over the memory hierarchy (HBM, VMEM, registers) and instruction scheduling, enabling attention kernels that are impossible to express through standard JAX operations alone.

tour.title: Mixture of Experts
tour.description: The MoE module implements expert routing with top-k gating, load balancing, and support for both sparse and dense matmul dispatch paths. It supports multiple routing strategies (Tokamax, Megablox, JAX ragged dot) and expert parallelism, which is essential for models like DeepSeek and Mixtral that use hundreds of experts. The MoE configuration reference document provides the full picture of architecture choices, sharding strategies, and tuning parameters.
tour.languageLesson: Mixture of Experts conditionally activates only a subset of parameters per token, achieving higher model capacity without proportional compute cost. The router (gating network) learns which experts to activate, while load balancing losses prevent expert collapse where all tokens route to the same few experts.

tour.title: Model Implementations
tour.description: MaxText provides first-class implementations of major LLM architectures. Gemma3 implements Google's model with alternating local/global attention and a SigLIP vision encoder. Llama4 adds vision encoding with unfold convolution and iRoPE. Qwen3 includes gated delta-rule recurrence and omni-modal encoders. DeepSeek uses batch-split MoE with custom forward/backward passes and Pallas splash attention for maximum TPU efficiency. Each model reuses the building blocks from Steps 4-8 while adding architecture-specific innovations.

tour.title: Data Input Pipeline
tour.description: The input pipeline module handles all data loading, from raw datasets to padded, sharded batches ready for training. The factory interface dispatches to the appropriate backend (Grain, HuggingFace, TFDS, OLMo, or synthetic) based on configuration. Grain provides deterministic, preemption-resilient loading; HuggingFace enables streaming from the Hub; and specialized processors handle instruction tuning, DPO preference data, and multimodal inputs. The data pipeline documentation compares all three backends and explains multi-host dataloading strategies.

tour.title: Pre-Training Loop
tour.description: The pre-training module is where everything comes together: it defines the loss function, JIT-compiled train/eval steps, the training loop with checkpointing, metrics logging, and the main entry point for training runs. It orchestrates the config system (Step 2), model instantiation (Steps 6-9), data loading (Step 10), and sharding (Step 3) into a functioning training pipeline. The companion train_compile module provides ahead-of-time compilation so you can catch OOM errors before allocating TPU resources.

tour.title: Post-Training: SFT and RL
tour.description: Beyond pre-training, MaxText provides a full post-training stack. SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning) integrates with the Tunix PeftTrainer for both LoRA and full fine-tuning. The RL module implements GRPO and GSPO reinforcement learning workflows for math reasoning, using vLLM for inference rollouts and math_verify for answer checking. The base hooks abstraction defines the contract that all post-training workflows implement, ensuring consistent metric logging and goodput tracking across SFT, DPO, RL, and distillation.

tour.title: Inference Engine
tour.description: MaxEngine is the core inference engine that powers serving and RL rollouts. It provides JIT-compiled prefill, autoregressive token generation, model loading with quantization support, LoRA adapter management, prefix caching, and multi-sampling. The inference utilities complement this with token sampling strategies (top-k, nucleus/top-p, weighted) and log-probability computation. This engine is what the RL trainer from Step 12 uses to generate candidate responses during GRPO training.

tour.title: Sharding and Distributed Training
tour.description: Sharding is what makes MaxText scale from a single chip to thousands. The sharding utility provides logical-to-physical axis mapping, partition spec creation, and FSDP sharding management. The sharding guide is one of the most important documents in the project, providing formal arithmetic intensity derivations for every parallelism strategy (FSDP, TP, CP, EP, PP) along with MaxText's three-layer sharding implementation (physical axes, logical axes, per-tensor constraints). Understanding sharding is essential for achieving high MFU at scale.
tour.languageLesson: JAX's SPMD (Single Program, Multiple Data) model uses named mesh axes and PartitionSpec annotations to automatically shard arrays across devices. Unlike manual data parallelism, SPMD lets you express any combination of data, model, and pipeline parallelism through declarative axis annotations that the XLA compiler resolves into optimal communication patterns.

tour.title: Containerization and Deployment
tour.description: MaxText provides separate Dockerfiles for TPU and GPU environments, each installing the appropriate JAX backend, Google Cloud SDK, and gcsfuse for Cloud Storage access. The TPU Dockerfile uses Python slim-bullseye with custom libtpu support, while the GPU Dockerfile extends the NVIDIA JAX base image with CUDA 12. A lightweight runner Dockerfile layers only source code on top of a pre-built base image for fast iteration. These containers are what XPK submits to GKE for production-scale training, connecting the entire software stack from this tour to real hardware.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds and layered Dockerfiles are used here to separate the slow dependency-installation step from the fast source-code copy step. The runner Dockerfile inherits from a pre-built base image, so rebuilding after code changes takes seconds rather than the 10+ minutes needed to reinstall all dependencies.
